Pak Study MCQs · pages 251-300

Sharpen your Pak Study recall

Tap a card to reveal the explanation. Take it one seal at a time.

500 questions in this set
1

The oldest and most important highway of Pakistan is :

Correct Answer: Grand trunk road
Show explanation
Explanation

The Grand Trunk Road is one of Asia's oldest and longest major roads.

It is formerly known as Uttarapath, Sadak-e-Azam, Shah Rah-e-Azam, Badshahi Sadak, and Long Walk

Length: 3,655 km (2,271 mi)

Status: Currently functional

Existed before 322 BCE–present

For at least 2,500 years, it has linked Central Asia to the Indian subcontinent.

History: Mauryan, Sur, Mughal, and British Empires

East end: Teknaf, Bangladesh

West end: Kabul, Afghanistan

2

Where is Minar-e-Pakistan is located?

Correct Answer: Great Iqbal park
Show explanation
Explanation

Minar-e-Pakistan is located in Greater Iqbal Park, Lahore


  • Construction_ 1960-68
  • Construction started: March 23, 1960
  • Opened: October 21, 1968
  • Height: 70 m
  • Architect: Nasreddin Murat-Khan
  • Architectural styles: Modern architecture, Mughal architecture
  • Minar-e-Pakistan was made in Lahore in memory of Pakistan's resolution
3

Soon Valley has located it?

Correct Answer: Khushab
Show explanation
Explanation

The Soon Valley is situated in the northwest of the Khushab district

Its largest settlement is the town of Naushera.

The valley extends from the village of Padhrar to Sakesar, the highest peak in the Salt Range.

The total area of the valley is about 737 square kilometers (182,173 acres), of which 75% is mountainous and 25% is plain.



4

Name the biggest Park (area wise) in Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Ayub National Park
Show explanation
Explanation

Ayub National Park named after the Pakistani President and Heneral Ayub Khan.

The park was established before the creation of Pakistan and covers an area of 313 acres (127 ha).

Its claimed to be the Asia's largest park.
5

As per Article 33 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the State shall discourage _____ sectarian and provincial prejudices among the citizens.

Correct Answer: Parochial
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 33 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 directs the State to discourage parochial, racial, tribal, sectarian, and provincial prejudices.

The aim is to promote unity and harmony among all citizens of Pakistan.

6

In the case of Muhammad Nawaz Sharif vs President of Pakistan (PLD 1993 S.C 473), was filed against the dissolution of the National Assembly under Article _____ of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973.

Correct Answer: 58(2)B
Show explanation
Explanation

In PLD 1993 SC 473 (Nawaz Sharif vs. President of Pakistan), the case was filed against the dissolution of the National Assembly by the President under Article 58(2)(b).

This article (introduced by the 8th Amendment) empowered the President to dissolve the National Assembly, which was later repealed by the 13th Amendment.

7

As per Article 207(3)(a) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, a person who has held office as permanent judge of the Supreme Court _______ or act in any Court or before any authority in Pakistan.

Correct Answer: Shall not plead
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 207(3)(a) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 restricts a former Supreme Court judge from pleading or acting before any court or authority in Pakistan.

This ensures judicial independence and impartiality by avoiding conflicts of interest after retirement.

8

As per Article 19-A of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, every citizen shall have the right to have ______ in all matters of public importance subject to regulation and reasonable restrictions imposed by law.

Correct Answer: Access to information
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 19-A grants citizens the right of access to information in all matters of public importance.

This right is subject to reasonable restrictions imposed by law for regulation and security purposes.

9

As per Article 59(C) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the Senate shall consist of how many general seats of Federal Capital?

Correct Answer: Two
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 59(C) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 provides 2 general seats for the Federal Capital in the Senate.

These seats represent Islamabad directly, apart from the provincial representation.

10

As per Article 203-C (2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the Federal Shariat Court shall consist of not more than _____ Muslim Judges, including the Chief Justice, to be appointed by the President in accordance with Article 175A.

Correct Answer: 8
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 203-C(2) (as amended) specifies that the Federal Shariat Court shall consist of not more than 8 Muslim Judges, including the Chief Justice.

These Judges are appointed by the President under Article 175A of the Constitution.

11

As per Article 201 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, subject to Article 189, any decision of a _____ shall to the extent that it decides a question of law or is based upon or enunciates a principle of law, be binding on all courts subordinate to it.

Correct Answer: High Court
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 201 states that decisions of a High Court on questions or principles of law are binding on all subordinate courts.

This ensures consistency and uniformity in the application of law within a province.

12

In the case of Khan Asfand Yar Wali and Others vs Federation of Pakistan (PLD 2001 S.C 607) _____ was the Presiding Officer/Chief Justice of Pakistan.

Correct Answer: Irshad Hassan Khan
Show explanation
Explanation

Khan Asfand Yar Wali vs Federation of Pakistan (PLD 2001 SC 607) was decided when Irshad Hassan Khan was the Presiding Officer and Chief Justice of Pakistan.

This case is often cited regarding constitutional interpretation and federal powers in Pakistan.

13

As per Article 1(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, Pakistan shall be ______ Republic to be known as the Islamic Republic of Pakistan.

Correct Answer: Federal
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 1(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 states that “Pakistan shall be a Federal Republic to be known as the Islamic Republic of Pakistan.”

This highlights Pakistan’s system of government as a federation of provinces under one republic.

14

As per Article 63-A(6) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the defection clauses shall not apply to the _____?

Correct Answer: Chairman or Speaker of a House
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 63-A(6) states that the defection clauses do not apply to the Chairman or Speaker of a House.

This exemption exists because they are expected to act impartially in conducting proceedings.

15

Article 12 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, deals with the protection against _____?

Correct Answer: Retrospective punishment
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 12 of the Constitution of Pakistan prohibits laws that impose retrospective punishment for an act committed before it was made an offence.

This ensures fairness by protecting individuals from being punished under ex-post facto laws.

16

As per the Case of Miss Asma Jillani vs Government of Pakistan (PLD 1972 S.C 139), the precise question the Supreme Court was whether the High Court had jurisdiction under Article 98 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1962 to enquire into the validity of detention under the Martial Law Regulation No. _____ of 1971.

Correct Answer: 78
Show explanation
Explanation

In Miss Asma Jillani vs Government of Pakistan (PLD 1972 SC 139), the key issue was whether the High Court had jurisdiction under Article 98 of the 1962 Constitution to question detention under Martial Law Regulation No. 78 of 1971.

The Supreme Court held that Martial Law was unconstitutional, restoring the jurisdiction of High Courts in such matters.

17

As per the case Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Khan vs Governor General of Pakistan (PLD 1955 F.C 240), the President of the Constituent Assembly Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Khan filed a writ petition in the nature of _____ with a view to determining the validity of appellants appointments as members of the Council of Minister.

Correct Answer: Mandamus and quo warranto
Show explanation
Explanation
Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Khan filed the writ petition in the nature of Mandamus and Quo Warranto.
The petition sought to compel performance of duty and question the authority of office holders.
18

As per Article 204(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, as is regarding contempt of Court, the Court means the _____?

Correct Answer: Supreme Court or a High Court
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 204(1) of the Constitution specifies that for contempt jurisdiction, Court means the Supreme Court or a High Court.

This limits contempt powers to higher judiciary, excluding lower courts.

19

Islamabad is part of which province?

Correct Answer: It is not a part of any province; it's a separate territory
Show explanation
Explanation

Islamabad is the capital city of Pakistan.

It is not part of any province.

It is a separate federal territory known as the Islamabad Capital Territory (ICT).


Islamabad officially became the capital of Pakistan on 14 August 1967, under President Ayub Khan.

The city was constructed starting in 1960.

It is now the country's ninth-most populous city, with a population of over 1.2 million.

Its postcode is 44000.

Before being named the capital, the area was known as Raj Shahi.

20

Which city of Pakistan is called the City of Greenery?

Correct Answer: Islamabad
Show explanation
Explanation

Islamabad is widely considered to be Pakistan's "City of Greenery" due to its abundant parks, gardens, and its setting against the Margalla Hills

Islamabad is the capital city of Pakistan.

It is not part of any province.

It is a separate federal territory known as the Islamabad Capital Territory (ICT).


Islamabad officially became the capital of Pakistan on 14 August 1967, under President Ayub Khan.

The city was constructed starting in 1960.

It is now the country's ninth-most populous city, with a population of over 1.2 million.

Its postcode is 44000.

Before being named the capital, the area was known as Raj Shahi.

21

The land of hospitality is called ____?

Correct Answer: KPK
Show explanation
Explanation

The land of hospitality is called Khyber Pakhtunkhwa

Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, is a province of Pakistan.

It is located in the northwestern region of the country, along the Afghanistan–Pakistan border and close to Tajikistan border.

22

Jalal ud Din Mohammad Akbar was born in ______?

Correct Answer: Sindh
Show explanation
Explanation

Jalal uddin Muhammad Akbar was a Mughal emperor.

He was born in Umarkot (Sindh) in 1542.

He became Emperor in 1556.

He was died in 1605.

23

Which city is also known as the 'Fruit Garden of Pakistan'?

Correct Answer: Quetta
Show explanation
Explanation

Quetta is also known as the fruit garden of Pakistan

The Punjab province is considered the breadbasket of Pakistan.

Peshawar city of Pakistan is called the city of Flowers 

The Multan city is known as mango city in Pakistan.

Land of Five River. Punjab

City of Colleges. Lahore.

City of lightening. Karachi.
ND18-2-2023


Manchester of Pakistan. Faisalabad.

Gateway of Pakistan. Karachi.

Brasilia of Pakistan. Islamabad.

Valley or city of Flowers. Peshawar.

City of Textiles. Faisalabad. 
24

Which city of Pakistan is called the Switzerland of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Swat
Show explanation
Explanation

Swat is called the Switzerland of Pakistan 

Because of its snow-capped mountains and beautiful scenery.


KNOWN ABOUT SWAT

Swat District is a district in the Malakand Division of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa

Swat was merged with Pakistan in 1849

Capital: Saidu Sharif

Mingora is the largest city of Swat Valley

Gwalerai is a famous city for apples in Swat

25

Which of these cities is known for its sports goods production?

Correct Answer: Sialkot
Show explanation
Explanation

Sialkot is known for surgical instruments,sports goods, leather goods, textile goods etc.

Sialkot is a city located in Punjab, Pakistan.
It is the capital of the Sialkot District
Sialkot is the 13th most populous city in Pakistan
Sialkot is located in the north-east Punjab
Sialkot is one of Pakistan's most industrialized regions.
The postal code of Sialkot is  51310

The calling code of Sialkot is 052 

26

Which city is called “Valley of flowers”?

Correct Answer: Peshawar
Show explanation
Explanation

Peshawar is called the Valley of Flowers. 

Peshawar means City of flowers

IMPORTANT QUESTION RELATED TO "PESHAWAR"

Peshawar is the sixth most populous city in Pakistan, with a population of over 2.3 million.

Peshawar was named so by Mughal Emperor Akbar

The modern name of the city "Peshawar" comes from the Persian words 'Pesh' - 'Awardan' meaning 'the first coming city.

Peshawar is situated in the northwest of the country,

Peshawar is close to the International border with Afghanistan.

Peshawar is the capital of the province of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa,

Peshawar Offical Languages Pashto, Hindko, Urdu

27

Who was the first Captain of Pakistan Cricket Team?

Correct Answer: Abdul Hafeez Kardar
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Abdul Hafeez Kardar was the first captain of the Pakistan national cricket team.
  • Pakistan's first Test match was played in Delhi in October 1952, as part of a 5-match series (India won 2–1).
  • Pakistan toured England for the first time in 1954 and drew the series 1–1.
  • Fazal Mahmood took 12 wickets in the Oval victory during the 1954 series.
    • 28

    As per Article 65 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, a person elected to a House shall not sit or vote until he has made before the House oath in the form set out in ______ the schedule.

    Correct Answer: Third
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    According to Article 65 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, a person elected to a House cannot sit or vote until they fulfill a formal requirement.

    They must take an oath before the House in the form set out in the Third Schedule of the Constitution.

    29

    As per Article 70(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, a Bill with respect to any matter in the ______ may originate in either House and shall, if it is passed by the House in which it originated, be transmitted to the other House; and if the Bill is passed without amendment by the other House also, it shall be presented to the President for assent.

    Correct Answer: Federal Legislative List
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 70(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 provides that a Bill relating to matters in the Federal Legislative List can originate in either House (National Assembly or Senate).

    Once passed by one House and agreed without amendment by the other, it is sent to the President for assent.

    30

    As per the Case of Miss Asma Jillani vs Government of Pakistan (PLD 1972 S.C 139), filed against the detention of Malik Ghulam Jillani which was dismissed by the Lahore High Court, one of the questions before the Supreme Court was whether the doctrine enunciated in the case of _____ was correct.

    Correct Answer: State vs Dosso (PLD 1958 SC 533)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In Miss Asma Jillani vs Government of Pakistan (PLD 1972 S.C 139), the Court examined the validity of the doctrine of necessity.

    This doctrine had been enunciated in State vs Dosso (PLD 1958 SC 533), which the Court reviewed and overruled.

    31

    As per Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the term of five years shall commence from the day of ______ and shall stand dissolved at the expiration of its term.

    Correct Answer: Its first meeting
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The 5-year term of the National Assembly starts from the day of its first meeting.

    It automatically dissolves at the end of the term, unless dissolved earlier.

    32

    As per Article 198(4) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, each of the High Courts may have Benches at such other places as the Governor may determine on the advice of the _____ and in consultation with the Chief Justice of the High Court.

    Correct Answer: Cabinet
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 198(4) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 states:

    "Each High Court may have Benches at such other places as the Governor may determine on the advice of the Cabinet and in consultation with the Chief Justice of the High Court."

    33

    As per case "Al-Jehad Trust vs Federation of Pakistan (PLD 1996 S.C. 324"), " consultation " is defined as effective, ______, purposive, consensus oriented, leaving no room for complaint of arbitrariness or unfair play.

    Correct Answer: Meaningful
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In Al-Jehad Trust vs Federation of Pakistan (PLD 1996 SC 324), the Supreme Court held that consultation must be effective and meaningful, not a mere formality.

    The Court emphasized that it should be purposive and consensus-oriented, preventing arbitrariness or unfair play.

    34

    As per Article 32 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the State shall encourage local Government institutions composed of ______ representative of the areas concerned and in such institutions, special representation will be given to peasants, workers and women.

    Correct Answer: Elected
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 32 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 directs the State to encourage local government institutions composed of elected representatives of the concerned areas.

    It also ensures special representation for peasants, workers, and women in these institutions.

    35

    As per the case Khan Asfand Yar Wali and other vs Federation of Pakistan (PLD 2001 S C. 607) the decision of the case was that the effect of ______ was valid retrospectively.

    Correct Answer: National Accountability Bureau Ordinance 1999
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In Khan Asfand Yar Wali vs Federation of Pakistan (PLD 2001 SC 607), the Supreme Court upheld the NAB Ordinance.

    The Court ruled its effect to be valid retrospectively.

    36

    As per Article 183(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the permanent seat of the Supreme Court shall, subjects to clause, be at ______?

    Correct Answer: Islamabad
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 183(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 specifies that the permanent seat of the Supreme Court shall be at Islamabad.

    However, the Court may also sit at other places as the Chief Justice of Pakistan, with approval of the President, may decide.

    37

    As per the Case of Miss Asma Jillani vs Government of Pakistan (PLD 1972 S.C 139), the precise question the Supreme Court was whether the ______ had jurisdiction under Article 98 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1962 to enquire into the validity of detention under the Martial Law Regulation No. 78 of 1971.

    Correct Answer: High Court
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In Miss Asma Jillani vs Government of Pakistan (PLD 1972 SC 139), the key issue was whether the High Court had jurisdiction under Article 98 of the 1962 Constitution to question detention under Martial Law Regulation No. 78 of 1971.

    The Supreme Court held that Martial Law was unconstitutional, restoring the jurisdiction of High Courts in such matters.

    38

    What is the total area of Punjab Province?

    Correct Answer: 205,344 Sq Km,
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    205,344 sq Km, is the total area of the Punjab Province.

    They occupy the Northeastern part of Pakistan.

    They cover 26%  (25.8%) of the total area of Pakistan.

    It is the Second largest area-wise

    There are 36 districts in Punjab.

    There are 9 Divisions in Punjab.


    Additional Information:

    The total area of Pakistan is 881,913 km²

    140,914 sq Km, is the total area of Sindh Province

    13,297 km² is the total area of Azad Kashmir

    347,190 sq Km is the total area of the Balochistan Province

    74,521 Sq Km, is the total area of Khyber Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KPK)

    ND13-5-2023




    39

    Which is the national fruit of Pakistan in summer?

    Correct Answer: Mango
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    National fruit of Pakistan: Mango

    National bird of Pakistan: Chukar

    National animal of Pakistan: Markhor

    National dish of Pakistan: Nihari

    National flower of Pakistan: Jasmine

    National tree of Pakistan: Himalayan Cedar

    National vegetable of Pakistan: Lady Finger

    40

    What is the length of Mangla Dam?

    Correct Answer: 3140 m
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The main Mangla Dam is approximately 3.14 kilometers (3,140 meters or 10,302 feet) long

    The Mangla Dam in the Mirpur District of Azad Kashmir, Pakistan

    It is situated on the Jhelum River 

    It is the sixth-largest dam in the world.

    It was constructed with the help of the World Bank 

    Mangra Dam is named after the nearby village of Mangra.

    Its Construction was started in 1961 and completed in 1967.

    It has 5 tunnels and a power station

    41

    As per Article 177(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the Chief Justice of Pakistan and each of the other Judges of the Supreme Court shall be appointed by the _____ in accordance with Article 175A.

    Correct Answer: President
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 177(1) specifies that the President appoints the Chief Justice and other judges of the Supreme Court.

    The process must follow the procedure laid out in Article 175A, involving the Judicial Commission and Parliamentary Committee.

    42

    As per Article case Nawaz Sharif vs President of Pakistan (PLD 1993 S.C 473), Nawaz Sharif filed the petition in Supreme Court before a full Bench of ______ Permanent Judges, under Article 184(3) of the Constitution of Pakistan, paying that order of dissolution of assembly on 18th April 1993 be declared null and void.

    Correct Answer: 11
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In Nawaz Sharif vs President of Pakistan (PLD 1993 SC 473), a full bench of 11 judges of the Supreme Court heard the case.

    This landmark judgment restored the National Assembly and Nawaz Sharif’s government after being dissolved by the President.

    43

    When was Gwadar re-annexed to Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: 1958
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan purchased Gwadar port from Oman on 8th September 1958.

    Gwadar officially became part of Pakistan on 8th December 1958.

    Gwadar remained under Omani rule for 174 years from 1783 -1958 

    44

    Which Province of Pakistan is called Bab-ul-Islam?

    Correct Answer: Sindh
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Sindh is known as Bab-ul-Islam (the gateway of Islam), as it saw the first spread of Islam into South Asia.

    Sindh is the third-largest province of Pakistan by land area

    Sindh is the second-largest province by population after Punjab

    The Sindhi language is the oldest language

    Sindhi is written in a version of the Arabic script.

    Sindhi is the official language of the Pakistani province of Sindh.

    Sindh has 30 Districts and 7 district

    There are three Barrages in Sindh

    The mountain range located in Sindh is Kirthar Range

    N

    45

    Benazir Bhutto became the first female Prime Minister of Pakistan in _____?

    Correct Answer: 1988
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Benazir Bhutto

    • Born: June 21, 1953, in Karachi.
    • Assassinated: December 27, 2007, in Rawalpindi.
    • Spouse: Asif Ali Zardari (1987–2007).
    • Party: Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP).
    • Children: Bilawal Bhutto Zardari, Bakhtawar Bhutto Zardari, Aseefa Bhutto Zardari.
    • Education: Lady Margaret Hall (1973–1977).

    Political Career:

    • Sworn in as First Woman Prime Minister of Pakistan on December 2, 1988.
    • First term as Prime Minister: 1988 to 1990.
    • Second term as Prime Minister: 1993 to 1996.
    • Farooq Laghari dissolved her second government.
    • Served as the 11th and 13th Prime Minister of Pakistan.

    Education Details:

    • Nursery School: Lady Jennings Nursery School in Karachi.
    • College: Radcliffe College
    • University: University of Oxford
    46

    Taxila University was established by the king

    Correct Answer: Ashoka
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Taxila University was established by King Ashoka

    Taxila University Founded: c. 5th century BCE

    The world's first university is Taxila University

    Ashoka was the third Mauryan Emperor of Magadha

    47

    When did Pakistan blast his first atomic bomb?

    Correct Answer: 28 May 1998
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan conducted a series of nuclear tests on 28 May 1998 at the Chagai test site, in Balochistan.

    5 Pakistan conducted nuclear tests at 15:15 hrs PST on 28 May 1998.

    The tests were performed at Ras Koh Hills in the Chagai District of Balochistan Province.

    Chagai-I was Pakistan's first public test of nuclear weapons.

    while 

    Chagai II

    Chagai-II is the codename assigned to the second atomic test conducted by Pakistan, carried out on 30 May 1998

    These tests followed the Chagai-I series

    The test site was Kharan Desert, Pakistan

    The number of tests is 1

     



    48

    In which of the following city the Pakistan Naval Academy is located?

    Correct Answer: Karachi
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Pakistan Naval Academy is a federal military academy located in Manora Island, Sindh, Pakistan.

    Pakistan Naval Academy Founded: December 8, 1970
    49

    Jinnah Naval Base is located in which province ?

    Correct Answer: Balochistan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Jinnah Naval Base is a strategic naval base of the Pakistan Navy

    Located at Ormara, Balochistan, Pakistan.

    It is named after Pakistan's founder, Mohammad Ali Jinnah.

    It is Pakistan's second-largest naval base.

    On 22 June 2000, the Jinnah Naval Base was inaugurated

    By then-Chief Executive Gen. Pervez Musharraf 



    50

    The Green Revolution in Pakistan was introduced during the rule of?

    Correct Answer: Ayub Khan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Green Revolution was introduced during Ayub Khan’s era in the 1960s.

    It was based on the introduction of the US helping Pakistan with economic Aid for Pakistan

    51

    Green Revolution in Pakistan occurred in?

    Correct Answer: 1960s
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The green revolution in Pakistan began in the 1960s,

    It was based on the introduction of the US helping Pakistan with economic Aid for Pakistan

    52

    When Pakistan Maritime Security Agency Established?

    Correct Answer: 1 January 1987
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Pakistan Maritime Security Agency is a branch of the Pakistan Navy.

    It is a Navy-managed and Navy-controlled Marine law enforcement agency 

    Abbreviation: PMSA

    Motto: Indeed, ALLAH is with us

    Formed: 1 January 1987

    Headquarters: Karachi

    53

    As per Article 175-A (VI) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, for appointment of Judges of Supreme Court, the Commission shall consist of among others, a Senior Advocate of the Supreme Advocate of the Supreme Court of Pakistan nominated by the _____ two years, for a term of.

    Correct Answer: Pakistan Bar Council
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Under Article 175-A(6) of the Constitution, the Judicial Commission includes a senior advocate of the Supreme Court nominated by the Pakistan Bar Council.

    The nominee serves for a non-renewable term of two years in the Commission for appointment of Judges.

    54

    Under which schedule do the National Assembly and Senate take oaths?

    Correct Answer: 3rd
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Third Schedule of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 contains the prescribed forms of oaths for the President, Prime Minister, Governors, Ministers, Members of National Assembly, Senate, and others.

    Therefore, both National Assembly and Senate members take their oaths under the 3rd Schedule.

    55

    In which plateau/range Soan & Haro Rivers are located?

    Correct Answer: Potohar Plateau
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Soan and Haro Rivers are located in the Potohar Plateau (also known as the Potwar Plateau) in Punjab

    The Potohar Plateau mainly includes six river basins

    1. Bhuna
    2. Haro
    3. Kahan
    4. Kanshi
    5. Reshi
    6. Soan

    56

    The archaeological site of "Kot Diji" is located near the city of _____?

    Correct Answer: Khairpur
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Kot Diji Fort formally known as Fort Ahmadabad, is an 18th-century Talpur-era fort located.

    Kot Diji forms a part of the Harappan Civilization.

    It is an ancient flood channel located on the bank of the Indus River.


    57

    The most precious gemstone “Emerald” are found in ____?

    Correct Answer: Swat
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In Pakistan, the most gemstone precious emerald are found in Swat.

    Swat known for its high-quality deposits.

    Emeralds, known for their exotic green tones, are primarily found in Brazil, Colombia, Zimbabwe, and Zambia.

    Gemstone Emerald means سبز فیروزہ ایک قسم کا پتھر

    58

    Siachen Glacier is located in which District?

    Correct Answer: Ghanche
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Siachen Glacier is located in Ghanche District.

    The Ghanche District is located in Gilgit-Baltistan. 

    Pakistan Army's brigade headquarters is located at Goma, Ghanche district.

    -----

    Siachen is glacier.

    Siachen Glacier length is 76KM or 72.4km

    the height of Siachin Glacier is 20,000 feet

    Siachen Glacier is Pakistan largest Glacier

    Siachen Glacier is world 2nd largest Glacier

    “Siachen Glacier” is situated in Karakoru Range

    Siachen Glacier is located in Ghanche District, Gilgit-Baltistan. 

    meaning of Siachen Glacier is Abundance of roses.گلاب کی کثرت۔





    59

    What is the old name of Gujranwala?

    Correct Answer: Khan pur
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan's city of Gujranwala is best known for the industries of rice, utensils, and safes.

    It is the 5th most populous city proper, following Karachi, Lahore, Faisalabad, and Rawalpindi.

    The old name of Gujranwala was Khanpur Shansi.

    It is new name is "City of Wrestlers."

    60

    The area of Islamabad before declaration as the capital of Pakistan was known by the name of____________?

    Correct Answer: Raj Shahi
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Rajshahi is a metropolitan city and a major urban, commercial, and educational centre of Bangladesh.

    Islamabad, Pakistan, was known to be called as Rajshahi Village in Shakarparian as a settlement,


    61

    What is the old name of Bin Qasim?

    Correct Answer: Pepri
    Show explanation
    Explanation
  • Port Qasim was established through an Act of Parliament on 29th June 1973.
  • It is situated in Karachi's Phitti Creek, in the Indus Delta region.
  • Also known as Port Muhammad Bin Qasim, it is a deep-water seaport.
  • The port is located on the Arabian Sea coastline in Karachi, Sindh.
    • 62

    The extinct Volcanic Peak of Koh-i-Sultan is in _______?

    Correct Answer: Pakistan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The extinct volcanic peak of Koh-i-Sultan is in the Chagai District, the Quetta Division of Balochistan

    It is located near the Nokkundi railway station. 

    It is part of the tectonic belt formed by the collision of the Eurasian Plate and the Indian Plate

    63

    Chandragup mud volcano is located in ______?

    Correct Answer: Pakistan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Chandragup is an active mud volcano located in Hingol National Park in Balochistan, Pakistan.

     It is also a sacred site for Hindus and a significant stop for pilgrims on their way to the Shri Hinglaj Mata temple. 

    64

    First agriculture reforms in Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: June 24, 1959
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The first attempts at land reform in Pakistan occurred under Ayub Khan's government, the West Pakistan Land Reforms Regulation 1959

    65

    The first TV station of Pakistan Television was established in which city?

    Correct Answer: Lahore
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    On November 26, 1964, the first official television station in Pakistan commenced broadcasts from Lahore.

    This was followed by 2nd Dhaka in 1965.

    3rd Rawalpindi-Islamabad in 1965.

    4th Karachi in 1966.

    66

    First Museum of Pakistan established in Karachi on?

    Correct Answer: 17 April 1950
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The National Museum of Pakistan was established in Frere Hall on 17 April 1950, replacing the defunct Victoria Museum.

    Location: Dr. Ziauddin Ahmed Road, Karachi

    Established: 1950

    67

    Nawab Abdul Latif found the Muhammadan Literary Society in April 1863 at?

    Correct Answer: Calcutta
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Mohammedan Literary Society was founded by Nawab Abdul Latif in 1863 in Kolkata..

    The society was located at Latif's residence in 16 Taltala, Kolkata.

    68

    PTV was established in _______?

    Correct Answer: Nov 1964
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    PTV was founded on 26 November 1964.

    It is the first official television station.

    PTV started its color transmission in 1976


    Additional information

    PTV started its live transmission on November 26, 1964
    On this date, it began its first transmission broadcasts from Lahore, followed by Dhaka.
    Ayub Khan inaugurated this first official transmission broadcasts 
    A third center was established in Rawalpindi-Islamabad in 1965 

    The fourth center was established in Karachi in 1966.

    Aslam Azhar is widely considered to be the "father of Pakistan Television".

    69

    Chief of Naval Staff Admiral Naveed Ashraf has been awarded which award on occasion of Pakistan Independence Day 2025?

    Correct Answer: Nishan-e-Imtiaz
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    On 14th August 2025, President Asif Ali Zardari awarded Hilal-e-Jurat to Field Marshal Asim Munir and Air Chief Marshal Zaheer Ahmed Babar Sidhu.

    Chairman Joint Chiefs of Staff Committee General Sahir Shamshad Mirza and Chief of Naval Staff Admiral Naveed Ashraf were awarded the Nishan-e-Imtiaz (Military).

    Awarded for their exceptional contributions during Indo Pak war 2025.

     ****

    70

    Chairman Joint Chiefs of Staff Committee General Sahir Shamshad Mirza has been awarded which award on occasion of Pakistan Independence Day 2025?

    Correct Answer: Nishan-e-Imtiaz
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    On 14th August 2025, President Asif Ali Zardari awarded Hilal-e-Jurat to Field Marshal Asim Munir and Air Chief Marshal Zaheer Ahmed Babar Sidhu.

    Chairman Joint Chiefs of Staff Committee General Sahir Shamshad Mirza and Chief of Naval Staff Admiral Naveed Ashraf were awarded the Nishan-e-Imtiaz (Military).

    Awarded for their exceptional contributions during Indo Pak war 2025.

     ****

    71

    Air Chief Marshal Zaheer Ahmed Babar Sidhu has been awarded which award on occasion of Pakistan Independence Day 2025?

    Correct Answer: Hilal-e-Jurat
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    On 14th August 2025, President Asif Ali Zardari awarded Hilal-e-Jurat to Field Marshal Asim Munir and Air Chief Marshal Zaheer Ahmed Babar Sidhu.

    Chairman Joint Chiefs of Staff Committee General Sahir Shamshad Mirza and Chief of Naval Staff Admiral Naveed Ashraf were awarded the Nishan-e-Imtiaz (Military).

    Awarded for their exceptional contributions during Indo Pak war 2025.

     ****

    72

    Name the Army Chief who has been awarded Hilal-e-Jurat on occasion of Pakistan Independence Day 2025?

    Correct Answer: Asim Munir
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    On 14th August 2025, President Asif Ali Zardari awarded Hilal-e-Jurat to Field Marshal Asim Munir and Air Chief Marshal Zaheer Ahmed Babar Sidhu.

    Chairman Joint Chiefs of Staff Committee General Sahir Shamshad Mirza and Chief of Naval Staff Admiral Naveed Ashraf were awarded the Nishan-e-Imtiaz (Military).

    Awarded for their exceptional contributions during Indo Pak war 2025.

     ****

    73

    As per Article 52 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, whenever it is necessary to cause a woman to be searched, the search shall be made by another woman, with strict regard to _____?

    Correct Answer: Decency
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 52 ensures the dignity and modesty of women during any search procedure.

    It requires that the search be conducted by another woman with strict regard to decency.

    74

    As per Article 4(2) of the United Nations Charter, the admission of any such State to membership in the United Nations will be effected by a decision of the ______ upon the recommendation of the Security Council.

    Correct Answer: General Assembly
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • New members are admitted to the UN through a formal process.
    • The Security Council first recommends the applicant state.
    • The final decision is made by the General Assembly.
    • Both organs must agree for admission.
    • This ensures collective approval of new UN membership.


    Last updated by NS on 16-April-2026

    75

    As per Article 193(2) of Constitution Pakistan 1973, a person shall not be appointed a Judge of a High Court unless he is a citizen of Pakistan, is not less than forty- years of age and he has, for a period of not less than ____ years, held a judicial office in Pakistan.

    Correct Answer: 10
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 193(2) sets the eligibility criteria for High Court judges, including 10 years of holding a judicial office in Pakistan (or other qualifying legal experience).

    This ensures sufficient professional and judicial experience before elevation to the High Court.

    76

    As per Article 185(2)(c) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, an appeal shall lie to the Supreme Court from any judgment, decree, final order or sentence of a High Court if the High Court has imposed any punishment on any person for _____?

    Correct Answer: Contempt of the High Court
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 185(2)(c) grants the right to appeal to the Supreme Court in cases where the High Court imposes punishment for contempt of court.

    This ensures judicial oversight and a safeguard against misuse of contempt powers.

    77

    As per case Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Khan vs Governor General of Pakistan (PLD 1955 F.C 240), the President of Constituent Assembly ______ filed a writ petition in the nature of mandamus and quo warranto.

    Correct Answer: Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Khan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Khan, as President of the Constituent Assembly, challenged the Governor General’s dissolution of the Assembly.

    He sought writs of mandamus and quo warranto from the Federal Court.

    78

    As per the case of Zafar Ali Shah and others vs General Pervez Musharraf, Chief Executive of Pakistan (PLD 2000 S.C 869), filed under ______ of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973.

    Correct Answer: Article 184(3)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 184(3) gives the Supreme Court original jurisdiction in matters of public importance relating to enforcement of Fundamental Rights.

    The Zafar Ali Shah case was filed directly in the Supreme Court under this provision.

    79

    As per Article 2A of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the principles and provisions set out in the objective resolution reproduced in the Annex are here by made _____ part of the Constitution and shall have effect accordingly.

    Correct Answer: Substantive
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 2A declares the Objective Resolution as a substantive part of the Constitution.

    This means it has binding legal effect in constitutional interpretation and application.

    80

    As per Article 14(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, no person shall be subjected to _____ for the purpose of extracting evidence.

    Correct Answer: Torture
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 14(2) explicitly prohibits torture for obtaining evidence.

    This provision safeguards human dignity and personal rights.

    81

    As per Article 23 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, every citizen shall have right to acquire, hold and dispose of property in any part of Pakistan, subject to the _____ any reasonable restrictions the imposed by law in the public interest.

    Correct Answer: Constitution
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 23 grants property rights but allows restrictions subject to the Constitution.

    Such restrictions must be reasonable and in the public interest.

    82

    As per Article 55(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, subject to the Constitution, all decisions of the National Assembly shall be taken by _____ of the members present and voting, but the person presiding shall not vote except in the case of equality of votes.

    Correct Answer: Majority
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 55(1) states that decisions in the National Assembly are made by a majority of members present and voting.

    The presiding officer only votes in case of a tie.

    83

    As per Article 77 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, no tax shall be levied for the purposes of the _____ except by or under the authority of Act of Majlis-e-Shoora (Parliament).

    Correct Answer: Federation
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • No tax can be imposed without legal authority.
    • Taxes must be approved by Parliament.
    • It applies to the Federation.
    • Ensures a lawful taxation system.
    • Protects citizens from arbitrary taxation.


    Last updated by NS on 16-April-2026

    84

    As per Article 13(1) of Statute of the International Court of Justice, the members of the Court shall be elected for ______ years and may be re-elected.

    Correct Answer: 9
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The provision is stated in Article 13(1) of the ICJ Statute.
    • Judges of the International Court of Justice are elected members.
    • The term of office is nine (9) years.


    Last updated by NS on 16-April-2026

    85

    Which is the first Islamic atomic nuclear country?

    Correct Answer: Pakistan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan is the first country to possess a nuclear arsenal.

    Pakistan conducted a series of nuclear tests on 28 May 1998 at the Chagai test site, in Balochistan.

    5 Nuclear tests were conducted by Pakistan at 15:15 hrs PST on 28 May 1998.

    The tests were performed at Ras Koh Hills in the Chagai District of Balochistan Province.

    Chagai-I was Pakistan's first public test of nuclear weapons.

    while 

    Chagai II

    Chagai-II is the codename assigned to the second atomic test conducted by Pakistan, carried out on 30 May 1998

    These tests followed the Chagai-I series

    The test site was Kharan Desert, Pakistan

    The number of tests is 1

    86

    As per Article 68 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, No discussion shall take place in Majlis-e-Shoora (Parliament) with respect to the conduct of any _____ in the discharge of his duties.

    Correct Answer: Judge of the Supreme Court or of a High Court
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 68 Constitution 1973 prohibits parliamentary discussion on the conduct of judges of the Supreme Court or High Courts regarding their official duties.

    This provision safeguards judicial independence from legislative interference.

    87

    As per the Judgment of the case of Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Khan vs Governor General of Pakistan (PLD 1955 F.C 240), the application was heard by a Full Bench of ______ Judges of whom Mr. Justice Hassan Ali Agha retired during the hearing and therefore gave no opinion.

    Correct Answer: 5
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    According to the judgment in Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Khan vs. Governor General of Pakistan (PLD 1955 FC 240), the case was heard by a Full Bench of five judges.

    Justice Hassan Ali Agha retired during the hearing and did not contribute to the final decision.

    88

    The boundary line between India and Pakistan demarcated by Sir Cyril Radcliffe is called:

    Correct Answer: Radcliffe Line
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Radcliffe Line was the boundary demarcation line between the Indian and Pakistani portions of the Punjab and Bengal provinces of British India.

    In order to determine exactly which territories to assign to each country, in June 1947,

    Britain appointed Sir Cyril Radcliffe to chair two boundary commissions

    89

    As per Article 205 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the remuneration and other terms and conditions of service of a Judge of the Supreme Court or of a High Court shall be as provided in the ____ Schedule.

    Correct Answer: Fifth
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 205 of the Constitution 1973 specifies that the remuneration and service terms of Supreme Court and High Court judges are provided in the Fifth Schedule.

    This ensures judicial independence and standardized conditions across Pakistan.

    90

    As per Article 189 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, any decision of the Supreme Court shall, to the extent that it decides a ______ or is based upon or enunciates a principle of law, be binding on all other courts in Pakistan.

    Correct Answer: Question of law
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 189 makes Supreme Court decisions binding on all other courts in Pakistan when they decide a question of law or lay down a legal principle.

    This ensures uniformity and consistency in the interpretation of laws across the country.

    91

    Karachi to Lahore direction?

    Correct Answer: Northeast
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Lahore, the second largest city of Pakistan and the capital of Punjab province.

    It lies 811 miles (1,305 km) northeast of Karachi in the upper Indus plain on the Ravi River, a tributary of the Indus.

    92

    Hub Lake is located outside _______?

    Correct Answer: Karachi
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hub Dam is a reservoir on the Hub River.

    It's construction began in September 1963.

    The opening date on June 1981.

    It is situated 56 km from Karachi city in Karachi and Lasbela Districts in Sindh and Balochistan.

    Hub, Haleji, Keenjhar, and Manchhar lakes are located in Sindh province.

    93

    The shortest tenure of Viceroy is _________?

    Correct Answer: Lord Mountbatten
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Lord Mountbatten had the shortest duration, from March 22 to August 15, 1947.

    He was the last viceroy

    Lord Mountbatten through "The Indian Independence Act of 1947"

    This plan is known as the 3 June Plan or Mountbatten Plan.

    This plan was the last plan for independence.


    Additional information

    Lord Linlithgow was longest reign as Viceroy of India.

    Lord Canning was the first viceroy of British India.

    Warren Hastings was the first governor-general of india.


    94

    Saif-ul-Maluk Lake is located in which mountain range?

    Correct Answer: Himalaya
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Himalaya has Mount Everest at the border between Nepal and China.

    In Pakistan the highest range is Nanga parbat.

    The Himalayas include over fifty mountains exceeding 7,200 m (23,600 ft)

    Himalaya has high forest. 

    Saif-ul-Maluk Lake is in Himalaya


    95

    Haleji lake is situated in ___?

    Correct Answer: Sindh
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Haleji Lake is situated in the Thatta District of Sindh 


    It is a perennial freshwater lake


    It is 6.58 km2 (2.54 sq mi) in size


    It was a saline lagoon until the 1930s 








    96

    The Chagai Hills are located _________ .

    Correct Answer: Pak-Afghan border
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Chagai Hills is a range of granite hills in the Chagai District in Balochistan province.

    Location Afghanistan-Pakistan border

    Chagai-I is the code name of five simultaneous underground nuclear tests conducted by Pakistan at 15:15 hrs PKT on 28 May 1998.

    ND18-9-2023


    97

    Where is the tomb of Baba Bulleh shah is located?

    Correct Answer: Kasur
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Syed Abdullah Shah Qadri, known popularly as Baba Bulleh Shah and Bulleya, was a Punjabi philosopher, reformer

    Baba Bulleh Shah was a Sufi poet during the 17th-century Punjab

    Baba Bulleh Shah's Born: in 1680, Uch Sharif

    Baba Bulleh Shah Died: in 1757, in Kasur

    Baba Bulleh Shah's Place of Burial: Kasur

    ND5-5-2023



    98

    How many districts are there in KPK?

    Correct Answer: 38
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The province of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KPK) after Fata Merger consist of 36 districts and 8th divisions.
