    99

    As per Article 178 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, before entering upon office, the Chief Justice of Pakistan shall make before the ______ and any other Judge of the Supreme Court shall make before the Chief Justice, oath in the form set out in the Third Schedule.

    Correct Answer: President
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    According to Article 178 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, before entering office, the Chief Justice of Pakistan shall make an oath before the President, and any other Judge of the Supreme Court shall make an oath before the Chief Justice.

    The form of the oath is outlined in the Third Schedule of the Constitution.

    100

    As per Article 2A of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the principles and provisions set out in the ______ reproduced in the Annex are here by made substantive part of the Constitution and shall have effect accordingly.

    Correct Answer: Objectives Resolution
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 2A makes the Objectives Resolution a substantive part of the Constitution.

    Its principles and provisions are given full effect as part of the constitutional framework.

    101

    As per Article 56(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the ______ may address either House or both Houses assembled together and may for that purpose require the attendance of the members.

    Correct Answer: President
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 56(1) allows the President to address either House or both Houses of Parliament.

    The President can require the attendance of members for this purpose.

    102

    As per Article 190 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, all executive and judicial authorities throughout Pakistan shall act in aid of the ______?

    Correct Answer: Supreme Court
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 190 requires that all executive and judicial authorities act in aid of the Supreme Court.

    This ensures the Supreme Court’s directives and orders are effectively implemented across Pakistan.

    103

    As per Article 35 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the ______ shall protect the marriage, the family, the mother and the child.

    Correct Answer: State
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 35 mandates that the State is responsible for protecting marriage, family, mother, and child.

    It ensures legal and social safeguards for family and maternal welfare.

    104

    As per the case of Zafar Ali Shah and others vs General Pervez Musharraf, Chief Executive of Pakistan (PLD 2000 S.C 869), Pervez Musharraf took ______ steps and proclaimed emergency as Chief Executive.

    Correct Answer: Extra-Constitutional
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In this case, Pervez Musharraf took extra-constitutional steps to assume power.

    He proclaimed emergency and acted as Chief Executive outside the framework of the Constitution.

    105

    As per Article 53(8) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, when the National Assembly is dissolved, the ______ shall continue in his office till the person elected to fill the office by the next Assembly enters upon his office.

    Correct Answer: Speaker
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The provision is given in Article 53(8) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973.
    • It deals with the office of the Speaker of the National Assembly.
    • If the National Assembly is dissolved, the Speaker continues in office.
    • The Speaker remains until the next elected Assembly selects a new Speaker.

    Last updated by NS on 17-April-2026

    106

    As per Article 87(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, each House shall have ______?

    Correct Answer: A separate Secretariat
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 87(1) provides that each House of Parliament shall have its own Secretariat.

    The Secretariat manages administrative and procedural functions of the respective House.

    107

    As per Case of Begum Nusrat Bhutto vs Chief of Army Staff (PLD 1977 S.C. 657), _____ the learned Attorney General for Pakistan, appearing as the law officer of the court.

    Correct Answer: Mr. Sharifuddin Pirzada
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Mr. Sharifuddin Pirzada appeared as the Attorney General for Pakistan in this case.

    He acted in the capacity of the law officer of the Court during the proceedings.

    108

    As per Article 25(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, there shall be no discrimination on the basis of ______?

    Correct Answer: Sex
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    According to Article 25(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, there shall be no discrimination on the basis of sex.
    Article 25 also states that all citizens are equal before the law and entitled to equal protection of the law.
    109

    As per Article 194 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, before entering upon office, the Chief Justice of a High Court shall make before the _____ and any other Judge of the Court shall make before the Chief Justice, oath in the form set out in the Third Schedule.

    Correct Answer: Governor
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    Article 194 states the Chief Justice of a High Court takes oath before the Governor of the province.
    Other High Court judges take oath before the Chief Justice of that court.
    110

    As per Judgment of the case "Begum Nusrat Bhutto vs Chief of Army Staff and Federation of Pakistan (PLD 1977 S.C 657)", the petition seeks to challenge the detention of Mr. Zulifqar Ali Bhutto, and _____ other leaders of the Pakistan People's Party under Marital Law Order No. 12 of 1977.

    Correct Answer: 10
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In Begum Nusrat Bhutto vs Chief of Army Staff, the petition challenged the detention of Zulfikar Ali Bhutto and 10 other PPP leaders.

    The detention was under Martial Law Order No. 12 of 1977 after the military takeover.

    111

    As per case "Khan Asfand Yar Wali and Others vs Federation of Pakistan (PLD 2001 S.C 607)", the writ petition was filed under _____ of the Constitution of Pakistan, wherein vires of National Accountability Bureau Ordinance, 1999, was challenged.

    Correct Answer: Article 184(3)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The petition in Khan Asfand Yar Wali vs Federation was filed under Article 184(3) directly in the Supreme Court.

    It challenged the constitutionality of the NAB Ordinance, 1999, on grounds of public importance and fundamental rights.

    112

    As per Article 53(6) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the Deputy Speaker may, by writing under his hand addressed to the _____, resign his office.

    Correct Answer: Speaker
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 53(6) states the Deputy Speaker can resign by writing to the Speaker.

    This maintains procedural hierarchy within the National Assembly.

    113

    As per Article 22(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, in respect of any ______ institution, there shall be no discrimination against any community in the granting of exemption or concession in relation to taxation.

    Correct Answer: Religious
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • It is mentioned in Article 22(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973.
    • It relates to taxation exemptions or concessions.
    • It ensures no discrimination among communities.
    • The protection applies specifically to religious institutions.


    Last updated by NS on 16-April-2026

    114

    As per Article 57 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the Prime Minister, a Federal Minister, a Minister of State and the _____ General shall have the right to speak and otherwise take part in the proceedings of either House, or a joint sitting or any committee thereof.

    Correct Answer: Attorney
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 57 grants the Prime Minister, ministers, and the Attorney General the right to speak in Parliament or its committees.

    This right exists even if they are not members of that particular House.

    115

    As per Article 13(b) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, no person _____, when accused of an offence, be compelled to be a witness against himself.

    Correct Answer: Shall
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 13(b) states that no person shall be compelled to be a witness against themselves.

    This protects individuals from self-incrimination in legal proceedings.

    116

    As per Islamic Jurisprudence by Imran Ahsan Khan Niazi, the Fiqh is used in the literal sense means " ______ " and "discernment".

    Correct Answer: Understanding
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In its literal sense, Fiqh means "understanding" and "discernment" in Islamic jurisprudence.

    It refers to deep comprehension of religious rulings and principles.

    117

    As per Article 37(g) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the ____ shall prevent prostitution, gambling and taking of injurious drugs, printing, publication, circulation and display of obscene literature and advertisements.

    Correct Answer: State
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 37(g) mandates the State to prevent social evils like prostitution, gambling, and harmful drug use.

    It also includes stopping the circulation and display of obscene literature and advertisements to protect public morality.

    118

    As per Article 78(2)(a) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, all other moneys received by or on behalf of the _____; shall be credited to the Public Account of the Federation.

    Correct Answer: Federal Government
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 78(2)(a) mandates that all moneys received by or on behalf of the Federal Government be credited to the Public Account of the Federation.

    This ensures centralized management of public funds at the federal level.

    119

    As per Article 10(8) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the appreciate _____ shall determine the place of detention of the person detained and fix a reasonable subsistence allowance for his family.

    Correct Answer: Review Board
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 10(8) of the Constitution assigns the Review Board the duty to decide the detention place and subsistence allowance.

    This protects detainees’ rights and supports their families during detention.

    120

    As per case "Muhammad Nawaz Sharif vs President of Pakistan (PLD 1993 S.C 473)", President ______ dismissed Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif with his cabinet.

    Correct Answer: Ghulam Ishaq Khan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In Muhammad Nawaz Sharif vs President of Pakistan (PLD 1993 S.C 473), Ghulam Ishaq Khan, then President, dismissed Nawaz Sharif’s government.

    This was under the controversial use of constitutional powers at that time.

    121

    As per case "Al-Jehad Trust vs Federation of Pakistan (PLD 1996 S.C. 324"), " ______ " is defined as effective, meaningful, purposive, consensus oriented, leaving no room for complaint of arbitrariness or unfair play.

    Correct Answer: Consultation
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In Al-Jehad Trust vs Federation of Pakistan, consultation is defined as a process that is effective, meaningful, and consensus-oriented.

    It emphasizes fairness and absence of arbitrariness in decision-making.

    122

    As per Article 193(2) of Constitution Pakistan 1973, A person shall not be appointed a Judge of a High Court unless he is a citizen of Pakistan, is not less than ______ years of age.

    Correct Answer: Forty-five
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 193(2) of the 1973 Constitution requires a High Court judge to be at least 45 years old.

    This ensures adequate legal experience and maturity before appointment.

    123

    As per case The State vs Dosso and other (PLD 1958 S.C. 533), by the Proclamation of October 7, 1958, the President of Pakistan annulled the Constitution of 2nd March and dissolved the ______?

    Correct Answer: National and Provincial Assemblies
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In The State vs Dosso (PLD 1958 SC 533), the Court validated the annulment of the 1956 Constitution and dissolution of National and Provincial Assemblies.

    This case applied the "Doctrine of Necessity" for the first time in Pakistan.

    124

    In the case Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Khan vs Governor General of Pakistan (PLD 1955 F.C. 240, ______ was the Chief Justice.

    Correct Answer: Justice Muhammad Munir
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Khan vs Governor General of Pakistan (PLD 1955 FC 240), Justice Muhammad Munir was the Chief Justice.

    This case is notable for upholding the Governor General’s powers, leading to controversy over constitutional interpretation.

    125

    As per Article 198(3) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the Lahore High Court Shall have a Bench each at Bahawalpur, Multan and Rawalpindi, the High Court of Sindh shall have a Bench at ______?

    Correct Answer: Sukkur
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 198(3) of the 1973 Constitution provides that the Sindh High Court shall have a Bench at Sukkur.

    This facilitates easier access to justice for residents of upper Sindh.

    126

    As per Article 53(4) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the Speaker or the Deputy Speaker shall NOT preside at a meeting of the Assembly when a/an ______ for his removal from office is being considered.

    Correct Answer: Resolution
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 53(4) states that the Speaker or Deputy Speaker cannot preside over an Assembly meeting when a resolution for their removal is under consideration.

    This prevents any conflict of interest during such proceedings.

    127

    As per Article 5(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, obedience to the ______ is the inviolable obligation of every citizen wherever he may be and of every other person for the time being within Pakistan.

    Correct Answer: Constitution and Law
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 5(2) of the 1973 Constitution makes obedience to the Constitution and the law an inviolable obligation for all citizens and persons in Pakistan.

    This ensures the supremacy of the Constitution and rule of law.

    128

    As per Provision of Article 54(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the National Assembly shall meet for NOT less than _____ working days in each year.

    Correct Answer: One hundred and thirty
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 54(2) of the 1973 Constitution mandates that the National Assembly must meet for at least 130 working days each year.

    This ensures regular legislative activity and parliamentary oversight.

    129

    As per Article 36 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the State shall safeguard the ______ rights and interests of minorities, including their due representation in the Federal and Provincial services.

    Correct Answer: Legitimate
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Article 36 of the Constitution of Pakistan, 1973, deals with the protection of minorities.
    • It states that the State shall safeguard the legitimate rights and interests of minorities.
    • This includes ensuring their due representation in Federal and Provincial services.

    Last updated on NS-16-April-2026

    130

    As per provision of Article 194 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the Chief Justice of the Islamabad High Court shall make oath before the ______ and other Judges of that Court shall make oath before the Chief Justice of the Islamabad High Court.

    Correct Answer: President
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 194 states that the Chief Justice of the Islamabad High Court takes the oath before the President of Pakistan.

    Other judges of the Islamabad High Court take their oath before its Chief Justice.

    131

    As per Article 11(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, all forms of forced labor and traffic in human beings are _____?

    Correct Answer: Prohibited
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 11(2) of the 1973 Constitution explicitly states that all forms of forced labor and human trafficking are prohibited.

    This provision safeguards human dignity and fundamental rights.

    132

    As per Article 1(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, Pakistan shall be Federal Republic to be known as the _______ Republic of Pakistan.

    Correct Answer: Islamic
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Article 1(1) of the 1973 Constitution declares Pakistan as the Islamic Republic of Pakistan.

    This reflects the country’s identity as both a federal state and an Islamic nation.

    133

    Dr. Abdul Salam won the Nobel Prize for the subject of _____?

    Correct Answer: Physics
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In 1979,  Dr. Abdul Salam won the Nobel Prize for physics.

    He received the Nobel Prize for combining two forces:

    • Electromagnetic 
    • weak forces

     He was the second Muslim and first Pakistani scientist to get the Nobel Prize.


    Additional information:

    Dr. Abdus Salam was Born in Jhan Punjab: Jan. 29, 1926

    Dr. Abdus Salam was Died: Nov. 21, 1996

    He completed his Ph.D. in theoretical physics from the University of Cambridge  In 1952 

    Awards

    • Copley Medal: 1990
    • Royal Medal: 1978
    • Hughes Medal: 1964
    • Pride of Performance: 1958
    • Nishan-e-Imtiaz
    • Adams Prize: 1958
    • Lomonosov Gold Medal: 1983
    • Atoms for Peace Award
    • Gold Medal for Outstanding Contributions to Physics: 1981
    • Smith's Prize for Essay: 1950
    • Jožef Stefan Medal: 1980
    • Cristoforo Colombo Prize: 1992
    134

    The ideology of Pakistan is based on the ______?

    Correct Answer: Two-Nation Theory
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    The Two-Nation Theory states that Muslims and Hindus of the Indian subcontinent are two distinct nations, with their own religion, culture, and identity.
    It was the ideological foundation for the creation of Pakistan in 1947.
    135

    Which of the following countries has not been recognized by Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Armenia
    Show explanation
    Explanation
  • Pakistan does not recognize Armenia due to its conflict with Azerbaijan over Nagorno-Karabakh.
  • Pakistan is the only country that has not established diplomatic relations with Armenia.
    • 136

    The following country does not share its border with Pakistan? Iran,India, Uzbekistan, Afghanistan

    Correct Answer: Uzbekistan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan shares borders with India, Afghanistan, Iran, and China, but not with Uzbekistan.

    Uzbekistan is separated from Pakistan by Afghanistan’s Wakhan Corridor.

    137

    Who among the following has become the second Army Chief to be promoted to the rank of Field Marshal in the history of Pakistan Army?

    Correct Answer: General Asim Munir
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    On 20 May 2025, the federal cabinet approved the promotion of Chief of Army Staff (COAS) General Asim Munir to Field Marshal.

    For ensuring the security of the country and defeating the enemy based on the high strategy and courageous leadership of the Battle of Truth, Operation Bunyan-um-Marsoos.


    Additional Information:

    The rank of Field Marshal is the highest rank of armies built on the patron of the British Army.

    It may be noted that after his promotion, General Asim Munir will become the country’s second field marshal only after Field Marshal Ayub Khan.

    138

    Who is the Current Chairman of Senate of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Yousaf Raza Gillani
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The current Chairmen of the Senate is Yousaf Raza Gillani.

    **

    Yousaf Raza Gillani is the 9th and current Chairman of the Senate of Pakistan.  

    He was elected as Chairman Senate on 09 April 2024

    Preceded by: Sadiq Sanjrani

    Last verified & updated 28-05-2024

    اس سوال کو وضاحت کے ساتھ ضرور پڑھیں

    The current seats of senate are:


    139

    The Headquarters of Air Force is located in?

    Correct Answer: Islamabad
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Air Force  HQ Initially, it was established at Peshawar on 15 August 1947.

    Later it was moved to Karachi on 1 June 1948 and back to Peshawar in 1960.

    In 1983 construction of the Air headquarters was started in Islamabad after it was decided to have all the armed forces headquarters in the capital city.

    ND5-07-2023


    140

    What is the rank of Pakistan in the production of sugarcane?

    Correct Answer: 5
    Show explanation
    Explanation

     The country is ranked 5th in the world cane acreage and 9th in sugar cane production.



    141

    Which from the following countries is not bordered by Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Bangladesh
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Pakistan, Nepal, and Sri Lanka are Countries of South Asia

    Bangladesh is bordered by the Indian states of West Bengal to the west and north


    *****

    Pakistan shares its border with four countries


    Pakistan borders India 2100 KM long in the east,

     Afghanistan border is 2,611 KM long in the west (northwest)

    Iran border is 909 KM long also in the west

    China 599 KM long border situated in the northeast.

      The beach of the Arabian Sea 1058KM forms its southern border.

    *****


    142

    When Muhammad Ali Jinnah gave 14 points?

    Correct Answer: 1929
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • In March 1929, Muhammad Ali Jinnah presented his 14 Points during a Muslim League session in Delhi to protect Muslim rights and interests.
      • He had left for England in May 1928.
      • He had returned after six months.
      • The Nehru Report of 1928 influenced these points, which were consolidated with help from Muhammad Ali Jauhar.
      • Jinnah’s Fourteen Points (1929) aimed to safeguard Muslim rights in British India.

      They included:

      1. Federal constitution with provincial powers.
      2. Provincial autonomy.
      3. No constitutional changes without state agreement.
      4. Adequate Muslim representation in legislatures.
      5. Muslim representation in services and self-governing bodies.
      6. 1/3rd Muslim representation in the Central Legislature.
      7. 1/3rd Muslim members in central and state cabinets.
      8. Separate electorates.
      9. No bill passed if 3/4th of a minority opposes it.
      10. Reorganisation should not affect Muslim majorities in Bengal, Punjab, and NWFP.
      11. Separation of Sindh from Bombay Presidency.
      12. Reforms in NWFP and Balochistan.
      13. Full religious freedom.
      14. Protection of Muslim religious, cultural, educational, and linguistic rights.
      143

      Kalam and Behrin are important tourist points of ___?

      Correct Answer: Swat Valley
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Kalam and Bahrain are important tourist points of the Swat Valley,

      It is located in the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province of Pakistan.

      This region is known for its breathtaking natural beauty, 

      144

      How many seats are reserved for women in the National Assembly of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: 60
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The National Assembly has a total of 342 seats.

      Of these, 266 are filled through direct elections.

      10 seats are reserved for religious minorities.

      60 seats are reserved for women, allocated through proportional representation to parties with more than 5% of the vote.

      ProvinceGeneralWomenNon-MuslimsTotal
      Punjab1413310174
      Sindh611475
      KPK45954
      Balochistan16420
      Islamabad3-3
       2666010

      145

      The Iran Pakistan gas pipeline is also called ____?

      Correct Answer: Peace Pipeline
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Peace pipeline is an under-construction 2,775-kilometre pipeline

      This delivers natural gas from Iran to Pakistan.

      Inaugurated by the president of Pakistan, Asif Ali Zardari, andthe  President of Iran Mahmoud Ahmadinejad

      Founded on March 11, 2013

      146

      Who becomes the first Pakistani man climbed 7 summits in 7 continents?

      Correct Answer: Mirza Ali Baig
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Mirza Ali Baig is a Pakistani high-altitude mountaineer and the first Pakistani man to summit all seven highest peaks in seven continents.

      Samina Baig is the first Pakistani to climb the Seven Summits, which are the seven highest peaks on each of the world's seven continents.
      147

      Who has become first Pakistani woman to climb Mount Manaslu of Nepal?

      Correct Answer: Naila Kiani
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Naila Kiani became the first Pakistani woman to climb Mount Manaslu in Nepal.

      She achieved this feat in September 2023,

      Summiting the world's eighth-highest mountain.

      Awards: Received the Sitara-i-Imtiaz, Pakistan's third-highest civilian award, for her mountaineering accomplishments.

      148

      Which city is the provincial capital of Balochistan?

      Correct Answer: Quetta
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Capital of Baluchistan is Quetta.

        They cover 42 percent of the area of Pakistan.

        They became a province in 1970.

        It is the biggest province of Pakistan by area.

        It is located on the Iranian plateau.

        35 districts are there in Balochistan

        There are many rivers in Balochistan but 7 are the main.

        Hingol is the largest river in the Balochistan.

        Baluchistan province has 7 divisions.

                    1. Kalat
                    2. Makran
                    3. Nasirabad
                    4. Quetta
                    5. Sibi
                    6. Zhob
                    7. Rakshan
                    149

                    Hala Haveli is known as birthplace of ______?

                    Correct Answer: Hazrat Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai, a renowned Sindhi Sufi poet, lived in the 18th century (1689–1752).

                    His poetry, particularly in Shah Jo Risalo, reflects deep spirituality and the culture of Sindh.

                    Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai, was a Sindhi Sufi mystic and poet from Pakistan.

                    Greatest poet of the Sindhi language. 

                    Born: November 18, 1689, Hala

                    Died: December 21, 1752, Bhit Shah

                    Place of burial: Bhit Shah

                    Nationality: Indian, Pakistani

                    150

                    Sindh should be separated from Bombay Presidency, was one of the points in _____?

                    Correct Answer: The fourteen (14) points of the Muslim League
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                                   List of Jinnah fourteen points (presents in 1929)

                    Title                               Description
                    Point 1:Federal System
                    Point 2:Provincial Autonomy
                    Point 3:Representation of Minorities
                    Point 4:Number of Muslim Representative
                    Point 5:Separate Electorates
                    Point 6:Muslim Majority Provinces
                    Point 7:Religious Liberty
                    Point 8:Three-Fourth Representation
                    Point 9:Separation of Sindh
                    Point 10:Introduction of Reforms in N.W.F.P and Baluchistan
                    Point 11:Government Services
                    Point 12:Protection of Muslim’s culture and Language
                    Point 13:One-Third Muslim Ministers
                    Point 14:Constitution
                    151

                    Bhakhar is the old name of _____?

                    Correct Answer: Sukkur
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    The official name of Sukkur Barrage is Lloyd Barrage.
                    The barrage was built during the British Raj from 1923 to 1932 

                    Sukkur Barrage Located in Sindh Province

                    Latest built: Rehabilitated in November 2004

                    Length: Approximately 2 km (1 mi)

                    Sukkur is the oldest barrage in Pakistan.

                    7 canals take off from Sukkur Barrage

                    Sukkur Multan motorway is known as M5

                    152

                    What does NICHD stand for?

                    Correct Answer: National Institute of Child Health and Human Development
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    National Institute of Child Health and Human Development (NICHD) is a part of the National Institutes of Health (NIH).

                    It's mission is to support research and programs that improve the health and well-being of children, adults, and families.

                    NICHD supports research on various topics, including developmental biology, reproductive health, and child health and development.

                    153

                    In Pakistan, what is the maximum term duration for a local government elected through direct elections?

                    Correct Answer: 5 years
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    In Pakistan, the maximum term duration for a local government elected through direct elections is 5 years, as per the local government laws.

                    This allows for the elected representatives to serve their full term before new elections are held.

                    154

                    Khewra is name associated with _____?

                    Correct Answer: Salt Mine
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    It is Pakistan’s largest and oldest salt mine and the world’s second-largest mine.

                    The Khewra Salt Mine (or Mayo Salt Mine) is in Khewra, north of Pind Dadan Khan, an administrative subdivision of Jhelum District, Punjab Region, Pakistan.

                    The Khewra salt mine was discovered by Alexander emperor in 326 BC 


                    Production

                    Products Rock salt

                    Production 3,50,000 MT

                    The financial year 2019–20

                    History

                    Opened 1872

                    Active 150 years

                    155

                    Female Judge of the Supreme Court of Pakistan is/are _____?

                    Correct Answer: Both A and B
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Justice Ayesha Malik the first female Judge of the Supreme Court of Pakistan since March 2012.

                    In June 2023, Judicial Commission of Pakistan approved the elevation of Justice Musarrat Hilali to the Supreme Court.

                    17 judges are there in the Supreme Court of Pakistan.

                    Retirement age of Supreme Court Judges: 65

                    Retiring age of High Court Judge: 62

                    *****

                    Justice Hilali became only the second female judge to rise to the apex court after Justice Ayesha  Malik

                    Justice Musarrat Hilali is set to become the first female chief justice of the Peshawar High Court (PHC) on April 1, 2023

                    Syeda Tahira Safdaris a Pakistani jurist served as the Chief Justice of the Balochistan High Court.

                    Mian Abdul Rasheed  was the 1st Chief Justice of the Supreme Court of Pakistan

                    156

                    What is the total number of judges of the supreme court of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: 17
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Chief Justice of Pakistan and each of other judges of the Supreme Court shall be appointed by the President in accordance with Article 175A, inserted through 18th and 19th Constitutional amendments.

                    In the light of this provision “The Supreme Court (Number of Judges) Act, 1997” has determined that the number of the Judges of the Supreme Court of Pakistan other than the Chief Justice shall be sixteen.

                    157

                    Who is called Baba-e-Urdu?

                    Correct Answer: Molvi Abdul Ul Haq
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The title of Baba-e-Urdu was given to Molvi Abdul Haq in 1935.


                    Molvi Fazal Ul Haq is also called "Sher E Bengal".

                    He was born on 16 November 1872 in Hapur.

                    He Died on August 16, 1961, in Karachi

                    The place of burial is Federal Urdu University Abdul Haq Campus Karachi.

                    158

                    Muslim League was established on the 30th of December 1906. Which city was decided to be its headquarters?

                    Correct Answer: Both A and B
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Important Note: According to STS IBA Aligarh is right answer because: The Muslim League's headquarters were initially located in Aligarh from 1906 until 1910 then move to Lucknow.


                    All India Muslim League Founded on 30 December 1906 at Dhaka, Bangladesh.


                    It was Founder by Nawab Khwaja Salimullah

                    Its Headquarters is located in Lucknow

                    They were Dissolved on 14 August 1947

                    1st President of All India Muslim League is Sir Agha Khan



                    جب 1947 میں مسلم لیگ ختم ہوئی تو یہ تین پارٹیاں بن گئی

                    1- Muslim League in Pakistan

                    2- Awami League in Bangladesh

                    3- Indian Union Muslim League in India


                    Note: If Aligarh appears as an option in any paper, then Aligarh is the correct answer.

                    If Aligarh is not listed and Lucknow is given, then Lucknow is the correct answer.

                    159

                    Pakistan's Peshawar was once known as _____?

                    Correct Answer: City of Flowers
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Peshawar is called the Valley of Flowers. 
                    • Sixth most populous city in Pakistan
                    • Peshawar was named so by the Mughal Emperor Akbar
                    • Peshawar" comes from the Persian words 'Pesh' - 'Awardan' meaning 'the first coming city.
                    • Situated in the northwest of the country,
                    • Cose to the International border with Afghanistan.
                    • Capital of the province of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa,
                    • Offical Languages Pashto, Hindko, Urdu

                    Last updated question by NA on 07 May 2026

                    160

                    Benazir Bhutto was assassinated on _____?

                    Correct Answer: December 27, 2007
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    "All about Benazir Bhutto and important question"

                    BB was Born on June 21, 1953, in Karachi

                    BB was Assassinated on December 27, 2007, in Rawalpindi

                    BB Spouse: Asif Ali Zardari (1987–2007)

                    BB Party: Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP)

                    BB Education: Lady Margaret Hall (1973–1977),

                    BB Children: Bilawal Bhutto Zardari, Bakhtawar Bhutto Zardari, Aseefa Bhutto Zardari.

                    Prime Minister

                    She was sworn in as the Prime Minister of Pakistan on 2 December 1988.

                    As Prime Minister first time from 1988 to 1990 

                     As Prime Minister second time from 1993 to 1996.

                    Farooq Laghari dissolved Benazir Bhutto's second government.

                    She was the 11th and 13th Prime Minister of Pakistan.

                    education :

                    Which Nursery School did Benazir Bhutto attend? Lady Jennings Nursery School in Karachi.

                    Which college did Benazir Bhutto attend? Radcliffe College

                    Which University did Benazir Bhutto attend? University of Oxford,

                    Karoonjhar Mountains are located in ______?

                    Correct Answer: Tharparkar

                    Explanation

                    Tharparkar, also known as Thar, is a district in Sindh province in Pakistan headquartered at Mithi.

                    It has the lowest Human Development Index rating of all the districts in Sindh.

                    As per 2023 census, what is the population of Sindh province?

                    Correct Answer: 55.7 million
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    2023 national census (released on 5 August 2023)

                    Growth rate: 2.55 per cent

                    Total Pakistan population: 241.49 million

                    Punjab: 127.68 million

                    Punjab growth rate: 2.53 percent

                    Sindh population: 55.69 million

                    Sindh growth rate: 2.57 percent

                    Balochistan population: 14.89 million

                    Balochistan growth rate: 3.2 percent (highest of all)

                    KPK population: 40.85 million

                    KPK growth rate: 2.38 percent

                    Islamabad population: 2.36 million

                    Islamabad growth rate: 2.81 percent

                    Pakistan rural population: 61.18 percent

                    Pakistan Urban population: 38.82 percent

                    33 million population increased between 2017 and 2023 

                    110 million population increased between 1998 and 2023 

                    *****

                    Who wrote the famous poem "The Neem Tree"?

                    Correct Answer: Elsa Kazi
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Elsa Kazi wrote the famous poem "The Neem Tree."

                    She was a German writer who married Allama I.I. Kazi.

                    Elsa Kazi's poem reflects themes of strength and resilience using the neem tree as a symbol.

                    164

                    CPEC has how many routes in Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Three
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    CPEC has the following 3 routes:

                    1. Western route

                    2. Central route

                    3. Eastern route


                    CPEC stands for China-Pakistan economic corridor.

                    China–Pakistan Economic Corridor is a 3000 km framework of regional connection between China and Pakistan.

                    Type of project------->Economic corridor

                    Location-------> Pakistan: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Gilgit-Baltistan, Punjab, Balochistan, Sindh & Azad Kashmir, China: Xinjiang

                    Established---->20 April 2015

                    Which is the largest cantonment of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Kharian Cantt
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Kharian Cantonment is one of the largest cantonments (Army bases) in Pakistan.

                    ....

                    Kharian Cantonment is located near Kharian city, in the Gujrat district of Punjab province.

                    The Cantonment was built in the late 1950s with US support.

                    ..

                    The oldest cantonment of Pakistan is Sialkot Cantonment 

                    42 Cantonment Boards are in Pakistan

                    Six military cantonments are there in Karachi

                    There are 56 Cantonments in Pakistan

                    The largest Cant in the world is Kanpur Cantonment

                    Sitar-e-Imtiaz: Third highest civilian award

                    In the Dadu District, floodwater reportedly submerged 300 villages.

                    171

                    What is the National color of Pakistan ?

                    Correct Answer: Green and White
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                     The national colors of Pakistan are green and white

                    White color represents peace and honesty & symbolizing the role of religious minorities

                    Green color represents – hope, joy, and love

                    Additional information 

                    The national bird of Pakistan Chukar

                    The national animal of Pakistan markhor
                    The national fruit of Pakistan                     Mango

                    172

                    Who is the great Leader of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Quaid e Azam
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    1st Governor-General of Pakistan 

                    Quaid e Azam born on: 25 December 1876 in Karachi

                    Additional information:

                    Muhammad Ali Jinnah joined the league in 1913.

                    He was 37 years old when he joined Muslim League.

                    He joined the Indian National Congress in 1906 and left in 1920

                    He was Born on December 25, 1876, in Karachi

                    He died on September 11, 1948, in KarachiOn 11th August 1947, Quaid-i-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah was elected as the President of the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan.

                    173

                    Lieutenant General Sarfraz Soomro was martyred during ______ in Balochistan.

                    Correct Answer: Flood Relief Operation
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Lieutenant General Sarfraz Ali, also known as Sarfraz Soomro, was martyred on August 1, 2022.

                    He died in a helicopter crash during a flood relief operation in Balochistan.

                    174

                    Which of the following Vice Chancellors of Sindh University is buried at the main campus of Sindh University Jamshoro?

                    Correct Answer: Both A and B
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Allama I. I. Kazi – A revered scholar and the first Vice Chancellor of the University of Sindh.

                    He is buried along with his wife Elsa Kazi at the main campus in Jamshoro.

                    Professor Dr. Abida Tahirani – Former Vice Chancellor of Sindh University, also buried at the main campus in Jamshoro.

                    175

                    Bilquis Bano Edhi passed away at the age of 74 on which date?

                    Correct Answer: April 15th 2022
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Bilquis Bano Edhi HI was a Pakistani nurse

                    save the lives of over 16,000 children

                    Bilquis Bano Edhi was: the wife of Abdul Sattar Edhi (married in 1966)

                    Born: August 14, 1947, Bantwa, India

                    Bilquis Bano Edhi passed away at the age of 74 on April 15th, 2022 

                    Awards: Hilal-i-Imtiaz, Ramon Magsaysay Award, Lenin Peace Prize

                    Resting place: Mewa Shah Graveyard, Karachi

                    176

                    Madr-e-Millat is the title of _____?

                    Correct Answer: Fatima Jinnah
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Madr-e-Millat is the title given to Fatima Jinnah
                    • Title: My Brother
                    • Publication Date: 1987
                    • Publisher: Quaid-i-Azam Academy, Karachi


                    Last updated by NA on 20-April-2026

                    177

                    Which mountain range is located in Sindh province?

                    Correct Answer: Kirthar Mountains
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Sindh is a province of Pakistan.

                    Sindh is known as Bab-ul-Islam.

                    The mountain range located in Sindh is Kirthar Range.

                    The Kirthar Mountains are a mountain range that marks the boundary between the provinces of Balochistan and Sindh.

                    Elevation of Kirthar mountain 2,260 m (7,410 ft).

                    The highest peak of the Kirthar mountains is Zardak Peak at 7,430 ft (2,260 m).

                    Kirthar Range, located along the Balochistan and Sindh provincial border.

                    The Hill Station of Sindh at Gorakh, in Kirthar Mountains Range, off Dadu.
                    178

                    Niaz Cricket Stadium is located in which city?

                    Correct Answer: Hyderabad, Sindh
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    Niaz Cricket Stadium is located in Hyderabad, Sindh, Pakistan.
                    It is an important first-class cricket venue and has hosted international matches as well.
                    179

                    The "Rooplo Kolhi Resort" is located in _________?

                    Correct Answer: Nagarparkar
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Established by: Sindh Tourism Development Corporation (STDC) in 2017.

                    Location: Foothills of the Karoonjhar Mountains, near Nagarparkar, Tharparkar District.

                    Named after: Rooplo Kolhi, a legendary freedom fighter of Sindh.

                    Attractions nearby: Ancient Jain temples, old mosques, and the arid Thar landscape.

                    Facilities: Offers lodging and amenities for tourists and visitors.

                    Scenery: Known for scenic views and cultural heritage sites.

                    180

                    SIUT stands for ______?

                    Correct Answer: Sindh Institute of Urology and Transplantation
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Dr. Adeeb Rizvi is the founder of the Sindh Institute of Urology and Transplantation (SIUT).

                    It established in 1975 in Karachi, Pakistan.

                    SIUT provides free medical care for urological diseases and promotes organ donation and transplantation.

                    181

                    What is the percentage of total population of Pakistan lives in rural areas?

                    Correct Answer: 63.66%
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    According to World Bank and the Pakistan Institute of Development Economics (PIDE)

                    Approximately 61.3% to 64% of Pakistan's total population lives in rural areas

                    "Urban area" can refer to towns, cities, and suburbs.

                    Rural areas, often called "the country," have low population density and large amounts of undeveloped land.

                    182

                    Khunjerab pass connects ______?

                    Correct Answer: Pakistan with China
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Khunjrab pass connects Pakistan with China.

                    Location: Hunza Pakistan - administered Gilgit–Baltistan and Xinjiang, China

                    Road name: N-35

                    Range: Karakoram

                    183

                    Ibn Battuta was born in which country?

                    Correct Answer: Morocco
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Battutah, commonly known as Ibn Battuta

                    Born: February 24, 1304, Tangier, Morocco

                    Died: 1369, Marrakesh, Morocco

                    Place of burial: Tomb of Ibn Battuta, Tangier, Morocco

                    Ibn Battuta visited India during the reign of Muhammad bin Tughlaq


                    Ibn Battuta was: a traveler

                    Ibn Battuta belonged: Morocco

                    Over a period of thirty years, Ibn Battuta visited most of the:

                    North Africa,

                    the Middle East,

                    Central Asia,

                    South Asia,

                    Southeast Asia,

                    China,

                    and the Iberian Peninsula

                    184

                    Who is responsible for conducting the census in Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Pakistan Bureau of Statistics
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Pakistan Bureau of Statistics (PBS) is responsible for conducting censuses in Pakistan.

                    It is an attached department of the Ministry of Planning, Development & Special Initiatives.

                    It works for collecting statistics in the country.

                    Formed: July 14, 1950

                    Headquarters:  G-9/1, Islamabad

                    185

                    The Police Martyrs Day is observed on which date in Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: August 4
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                  • The Police Martyrs Day is observed on August 4th across Pakistan to honor the services of the police department.
                  • It marks the death anniversary of Shaheed Safwat Ghayur.
                  • He was martyred in a suicide attack on August 4th, 2010.
                    • 186

                    National juice of Pakistan is ______?

                    Correct Answer: Sugarcane juice
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The sugarcane juice is the "national drink" of Pakistan.

                    It was declared the national drink in 2019.

                    Additional information 

                    National fruit of Pakistan: Mango

                    National bird of Pakistan: Chukar

                    National animal of Pakistan: Markhor

                    National dish of Pakistan: Nihari

                    National flower of Pakistan: Jasmine

                    National tree of Pakistan: Himalayan Cedar

                    National vegetable of Pakistan: Lady Finger

                    187

                    Guddu Barrage was constructed on the river _______?

                    Correct Answer: River Indus
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Constructed: 1962

                    Foundation of Gudu Barrage laid by: President Iskander Mirza (February 1957)

                    Completed: 1962 (cost 474.8 million rupees)

                    188

                    Who wrote book “If I am assassinated”?

                    Correct Answer: Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    "If I Am Assassinated" was written by Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto in 1979 while he was imprisoned.

                    The book reflects his political views, defense, and criticism of the judicial system that sentenced him.

                    189

                    Chotiari Dam is another name of ______?

                    Correct Answer: Baqar Lake
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Chotiari Dam is another name for Baqar Lake.

                    It is also known as the "Jungle of Lakes".

                    It is a prominent feature of the Chotiari reservoir, which is an artificial water reservoir in the Sanghar district of Sindh, Pakistan.

                    The reservoir is fed by the Nara Canal.

                    190

                    According to the World Bank, the urban population of Pakistan in 2020 was _______?

                    Correct Answer: 38.10%
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    According to data from the World Bank, the urban population of Pakistan in 2020 was 38.10% of the total population.

                    The rural population of Pakistan in 2020 was 61.90% of the total population.

                    191

                    The word Majlis Shoora means _______?

                    Correct Answer: Parliament
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Majlis-e-Shoora is the Parliament of Pakistan, consisting of the National Assembly and the Senate.

                    The term "Majlis" means assembly, and "Shoora" refers to consultation, indicating a legislative body.

                    192

                    Nathia Gali is situated in _______?

                    Correct Answer: KPK
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Nathia Gali is a famous hill station located in the Abbottabad District of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KPK), Pakistan.

                    It is part of the Galyat range.

                    It is known for its cool weather, pine forests, and scenic hiking trails.

                    193

                    The "Baqar Lake Resort" is located in the district _______?

                    Correct Answer: Sanghar
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Baqar Lake Resort is situated on the Chotiari Reservoir, close to Achro city at the tail end of the Nara Canal, located in Sanghar District.

                    The resort is part of the Chotiari ecosystem—which includes several natural lakes like Akanwari, Tajar, Phuleli, Seri, Sao Naro, and Baqar—popularly referred to as a “wonderland of Sindh,” all within Sanghar District.

                    194

                    Which pass connects Pakistan and Afghanistan?

                    Correct Answer: Khyber Pass
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Pass connecting Pakistan and Afghanistan is the Khyber Pass

                    Connects Landi Kotal to the Valley of Peshawar.

                    Elevation: 1,070 m (3,510 ft)

                    Traversed by: N-5 National Highway; Khyber Pass Railway

                    Location: in Landi Kotal and Jamrud

                    Range: White Mountains (Spin Ghar, Safed Koh)

                     


                    Additional information 

                    Bolan Pass 

                    Connects: JCB and Sibi with Quetta 

                    Height: 1793 meters, 5884 feet


                     

                    Lowari Pass 

                    Connects: Chitral with Dir 

                    Height: 3118 meters, 10230 feet 

                     

                    Broghil Pass 

                    Location: Pakistan & Afghanistan border 

                    Height: 3798 meters, 12460 feet 

                     

                    Malakand Pass 

                    Connects: Dir with Chitral 

                    Height: 1362 meters, 4468 feet 

                     

                    Shandur pass

                    Connects: Gupis/Gilgit with Chitral 

                    Height: 3700 meters, 12139 feet 

                     

                    Khanjerab Pass (top most in Pakistan) 

                    Other name: Friendship Gate

                    Connects: China with Pakistan 

                    Height: 4693 meters, 15397 feet 

                     

                    Dorah pass

                    Connects: Badakhshan province of Afghanistan with Chitral 

                    Height: 4300 meters, 14108 feet 

                     

                    Babusar Pass 

                    Connects: Mansehra/Abbotabad with Gilgit


                    Kohat Pass

                    Connects: Kohat with Peshawar 


                    Khojak Pass

                    Connects: Qilla Abdullah with Chaman

                     

                    Tochi Pass

                    Connects: Ghazni with Bannu


                    Karakar Pass

                    Connects: Mingora with Buner 

                     




                    Last updated by NA on 23-April-2026

                    195

                    Which country won Cricket Champions Trophy 2017?

                    Correct Answer: Pakistan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The 2017 ICC Champions Trophy was the eighth ICC Champions Trophy,

                     It was held in England and Wales from 1 to 18 June 2017.

                                        List of Champions Trophy Winners & Host 
                    YEARHOSTWINNERRUNNER
                    1998Dhaka, BangladeshSouth AfricaWest Indies
                    2000Nairobi, KenyaNew ZealandIndia
                    2002Sri LankaIndia and Sri Lanka (final was washed out)
                    2004EnglandWest IndiesEngland
                    2006IndiaAustraliaWest Indies
                    2009South AfricaAustraliaNew Zealand
                    2013EnglandIndiaEngland
                    2017EnglandPakistanIndia
                    2025Pakistan
                    2029India

                    ***


                    196

                    What was the original name of Hazrat Lal Shahbaz Qalandar?

                    Correct Answer: Syed Usman Marwandi
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The full name of Hazrat Lal Shahbaz Qalandar: Syed Shah Hussain Marwandi

                    Lal Shahbaz Qalandar wrote several books in Persian and Arabic

                    Lal Shahbaz Qalandar was a Sufi saint and poet of present-day Pakistan and Afghanistan

                    Born: 1177, Marand, Iran

                    Died: February 19, 1274, Sehwān

                    Place of burial: Darbar Hazrat Usman Marvandi Lal Shahbaz Qalandar, Sehwān

                    Title: Qalandar

                    The shrine of Hazrat Laal Shahbaz Qalandar is situated in Sehwan Sindh

                    197

                    According to the World Bank, the population of Pakistan in 2020 was _______?

                    Correct Answer: 220 Millions
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Based on various sources, including Statista, Dawn, and the UN, the population of Pakistan in 2020 was 220 million. 

                    The current population of Pakistan is 255,500,337 as of Wednesday, July 30, 2025, based on Worldometer's elaboration of the latest United Nations data.

                    198

                    The largest source of electricity generation in Pakistan is __________?

                    Correct Answer: Thermal
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    Major Source Of Electricity In Pakistan
                    1. Thermal:         58% share
                    2. Hydro:             30% share
                    3. Nuclear:         10% share
                    4. Renewables: 1% share
                    ***********

                    Major Source Of Electricity/Energy In World

                          1.  Oil & Gas     53% share  
                          2. Coal               27% share     
                          3. Renewable     13% share
                          4. Nuclear             7% share
                          199

                          The Largest Island of Pakistan is ________?

                          Correct Answer: Astola Island
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Astola is Pakistan's largest offshore island at approximately 6.7 km (4.2 mi) long)

                          It is also known as Jezira Haft Talar, Satadip, or 'Island of the Seven Hills

                          Location:  Balochistan, Gwadar District

                          Astola is famous for its rich marine biodiversity.


                          AGM 02.05.2023


                          200

                          Which constitution named Pakistan as Islamic Republic of Pakistan?

                          Correct Answer: 1956
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          After nine years of efforts, Pakistan was successful in framing a constitution.

                          The Constituent Assembly adopted it on 29 February 1956

                          it was enforced on 23 March 1956 proclaiming Pakistan to be an Islamic republic.

                          This constituent consists of:

                          234 Articles divided into 13 parts and 6 schedules.


                          23rd March became our Republic day

                          In 1957 Pakistan officially renamed the Islamic Republic of Pakistan


                          201

                          Who is the Current Prime Minister (PM) of Pakistan?

                          Correct Answer: Shahbaz Sharif
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Shahbaz Sharif became the 24th Prime Minister of Pakistan. 

                          Appointed on 03 March 2024

                          Party affiliation: PML-N

                          Predecessor: Anwar ul Haq Kakar 



                           Last updated and verified on 10-05-2024

                          202

                          Where is Shalimar Garden located?

                          Correct Answer: Lahore
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan built Shalimar Gardens in Lahore.

                          It was built in the year 1641-42.

                          It is included in World Heritage Site: 1981


                          Sr#

                          Built by

                          Name of Places

                          1

                          Jahangir​

                          Shalimar Bagh, Kashmir

                          2

                          Shah Jahan

                          1. Shalimar Garden, Pakistan,

                           

                          2. Taj Mahal,

                          3. the Jama Masjid,

                          4. the Red Fort
