                          Akbar

                          1.    Buland Darwaza,

                          2.    Fatehpur Sikri

                          4

                          Aurangzeb

                          Moti Masjid, Delhi

                          203

                          Which country is located west of Pakistan?

                          Correct Answer: Iran
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Pakistan shares its western border with Iran, which is approximately 909 km long.

                          It is also bounded by Afghanistan to the northwest and north.

                          China to the northeast.

                          India to the east and southeast.

                          These borders define Pakistan's geostrategic location in South Asia.

                          204

                          When did Pakistan join OIC?

                          Correct Answer: 1969
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Pakistan has been an active Member of the Organization.

                          Pakistan join the OIC for the first time in 1969

                          Pakistan hosted the Second OIC Summit Conference on 22-24 February 1974 in Lahore

                          on September 25, 1969, was OIC founded
                          ***
                          25 founding members of OIC at the time of its establishment.

                          OIC headquarters is located in Jadda.

                          OIC Stands for Organization of Islamic Cooperation

                          It is the largest international organization outside of the United Nations.

                          The official languages of the OIC are Arabic, English, and French.

                          It changed its name on 28 June 2011 from the Organization of the Islamic Conference to its current name.

                          Total Current member= 57

                          The first summit of OIC was held in Rabat

                          205

                          The International border between Pakistan and Afghanistan is known as ______?

                          Correct Answer: Durand line
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The common border between Pakistan and Afghanistan is called the Durand Line

                          The length of the border is 2611 km

                          It was drawn on November 12, 1893, in Kabul Afghanistan

                          It was an agreement between Mortimer Durand and Abdul Rahman.

                          There are two significant ethnic groups near the Durand line:

                          • Punjabis: biggest ethnic group in Pakistan
                          • Pashtuns: are the largest ethnic group in Afghanistan

                          ********

                          According to the Survey of Pakistan, the border between Pakistan and Afghanistan is 2611 km

                          اچھی تیاری کرنا چاہتے تو یہ سوالات ضرور پڑھیں

                          Click here

                          206

                          Oyster Rocks, a series of Islands, are located in ______?

                          Correct Answer: Karachi
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Oyster Rocks are a group of small islands located off the coast of Karachi, near Clifton.

                          They fall within the territorial waters of Sindh province in southern Pakistan.

                          207

                          Which of the following is known as "Data Ganj Bakhsh"?

                          Correct Answer: Shaikh Ali Bin Usman Al-Hajveri
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Hazrat Sheikh Ali Bin Usman Hajweri (Hazrat Data Ganj) came to Lahore in 1041 AD with Masud Ghazni, the son of Mahmud, a Turko-Afghan invader.

                          He was born in Ghazni, Afghanistan in 1009 AD.

                          He was a Persian Sufi mystic and Islamic scholar.

                          He wrote a famous book on mysticism.

                          He died in Lahore in 1072 AD at the age of 63.

                          208

                          Karachi is located in the _______?

                          Correct Answer: South of Pakistan
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Karachi is situated along the southern coast of Pakistan, bordering the Arabian Sea.

                          It is the country’s largest city and a major seaport in southern Pakistan.

                          209

                          Taj Mahal was completed in 1648 by Emperor Shah Jahan in memory of his wife named _____?

                          Correct Answer: Mumtaz Mahal
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Taj Mahal

                          Construction started

                          1632

                          Construction completed

                          1647

                          Built by

                          Mughal Emperor Shah Jehan

                          Built in the memory of

                          Mumtaz Mahal

                          Height

                          73 M

                          Taj Mahal

                          Most visited monument of India as of July 2022

                          Architecture

                          Ustad Ahmed Lahori

                          210

                          The author of the book "Whither Pakistan: Dictatorship or Democracy" is _______?

                          Correct Answer: Benazir Bhutto
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          "Whither Pakistan: Dictatorship or Democracy?" was written by Benazir Bhutto in 1988.
                          The book discusses Pakistan’s political landscape and advocates for democracy.
                          211

                          Shah Jo Risalo was first translated by _____?

                          Correct Answer: Ernest Trumpp
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Shah Jo Risalo is a poetic compendium of famous Sindhi Sufi poet Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai

                          Language: Sindhi

                          Subject: Sufism Culture History

                          The Risalo was first published in 1866 by the German philologist Ernest Trumpp.

                          It contains thirty chapters, called Sur.

                          212

                          The mosque located on the foothills of Margalla Hills in Islamabad is _____?

                          Correct Answer: Shah Faisal Masjid
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Shah Faisal Masjid is located at the foothills of Margalla Hills in Islamabad.

                          It is one of the largest and most iconic mosques in Pakistan.


                          • The largest Mosque in Pakistan is Shah Faisal Mosque
                          • **
                          • Shah Faisal Mosque is the largest Mosque in Pakistan.
                          • Shah Faisal Mosque Architect by Vedat Dalokay. (Turkish)
                          • Shah Faisal Mosque Date established in 1986
                          • Shah Faisal Mosque Capacity of 300,000 worshippers
                          • Shah Faisal Mosque Minaret height 90 m (300 ft)
                          213

                          Aligarh Movement was started by _______?

                          Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmed Khan
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation


                          The Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental (MAO) School was established by Sir Syed Ahmed Khan in 1875 as part of the Aligarh Movement.

                          It later evolved into Aligarh Muslim University, aiming to modernize Muslim education.

                          The major objective of the Aligarh movement was to promote Education

                          Sir Syed Ahmed Khan started Ali Garh School in 1875

                          Ali Garh school converted into College 1877

                          Ali Garh College was converted into a University in 1920


                          Additional information:

                          M.A.O college was to provide education to Muslims & Non-Muslims

                          Henry Siddons was the headmaster of the M.A.O school

                          Lord Lytton inaugurated M.A.O College in 1877

                          In MAO Intermediate classes were started in 1878

                          in 1881 B.A. degree classes were added.


                          214

                          Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai invented a musical instrument called ______?

                          Correct Answer: Tamboora
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai invented and played the Tamboora, a stringed musical instrument.

                          It is closely associated with his poetry and Sufi music in Sindh.

                          Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai, was a Sindhi Sufi mystic, and poet.

                          He is widely considered to be the greatest poet of the Sindhi language. 

                          Born: November 18, 1689, Hala

                          Died: January 1, 1752, Bhit Shah

                          215

                          What part of the world is Pakistan in?

                          Correct Answer: South Asia
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Pakistan is located in South Asia, situated in the southwestern part of the Asian continent.

                          It is strategically positioned at the confluence of South Asia, the Middle East, and Central Asia.

                          216

                          Which of the following is the largest airline in Pakistan?

                          Correct Answer: PIA
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Pakistan International Airlines (PIA) is the largest airline in Pakistan

                          They operate a fleet of 32 aircraft.

                          It was established as Orient Airways in 1946 and renamed PIA in 1955

                          It is an international airline that serves as the national flag carrier of Pakistan.

                          Founded: 1946

                          Headquarters: Karachi

                          217

                          Which of the following died before the independence of Pakistan?

                          Correct Answer: Allama Muhammad Iqbal
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Allama Iqbal passed away on 21st April 1938, 9 years before Pakistan's independence in 1947.

                          Quaid-e-Azam died in 1948, and Liaquat Ali Khan was assassinated in 1951 — both after independence.

                          218

                          The Shrine (Tomb) of Sufi Shah Inayat Shaheed is in district _____?

                          Correct Answer: Thatta
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The shrine of Sufi Shah Inayat Shaheed is located in Jhok Sharif, District Thatta, Sindh.

                          He was a famous Sufi saint and social reformer who stood for peasant rights.

                          He was born in 1655/56 AD in Miranpur (now part of Jhok Sharif).

                          He died in 1718.

                          219

                          Neelum Valley is located in _______?

                          Correct Answer: Azad Kashmir
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The Neelum River is a river in the Kashmir region of India and Pakistan.

                          Neelum River originates in the Bandipora district of northern Jammu and Kashmir in India

                          Neelum River flows through the Neelam Valley in Pakistan's Azad Kashmir

                          The river has traditionally been known as the Kishanganga River

                          The river was renamed the Neelum River in Pakistan in 1956


                          220

                          In which of the following district the shrine of Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai Located in?

                          Correct Answer: Matiari
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai, was a Sindhi Sufi mystic, and poet.

                          He is widely considered to be the greatest poet of the Sindhi language. 

                          Born: November 18, 1689, Hala

                          Died: January 1, 1752, Bhit Shah

                          221

                          Zarb-e-Kaleem was written by _____?

                          Correct Answer: Allama Iqbal
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Zarb-e-Kaleem is a philosophical poetry book of Allama Iqbal in Urdu.

                          It was published in 1936, two years before his death.

                          222

                          On which date is Death Anniversary of Quaid-e-Azam observed?

                          Correct Answer: 11th September
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Muhammad Ali Jinnah born on 25 December 1876  died on 11 September 1948

                          He was the Pakistan's first Governor-General until his death.

                          223

                          Who is the first lady federal minister of Pakistan?

                          Correct Answer: Vikarun Nisa Noor
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          First Lady, Federal Minister Vikarun Nisa Noor (Tourism). 

                          Additional information 

                          First Lady Governor Rana Liaquat Ali (Sindh) 1973-1976.

                          First lady governor Begum Rana Liaquat Ali (Sindh) 1973-1976

                          224

                          Which was the first medical college established in Pakistan?

                          Correct Answer: King Edward Medical College
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          King Edward Medical University is a public medical university located in Lahore.

                          Founded in 1860, the university is named after King Edward VII.

                          Established by the British Raj, named as Lahore Medical School.

                          confusion about the oldest University of Pakistan

                          • Punjab University( founded in 1882) 

                          King Edward University (History)

                          • First established as Lahore Medical School was established in 1860
                          • Renamed King Edward Medical College in 1911
                          • Got university status in 2005
                          225

                          How many stories does the Habib Bank Plaza in Karachi have?

                          Correct Answer: 22 stories
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Habib Bank Plaza, also known as HBL Plaza,

                          It is located on I. I. Chundrigar Road in Karachi, Sindh, Pakistan,

                          It is the head office of Habib Bank Limited. 

                          It was once the tallest building in Asia

                          Height: 101 m

                          Floors: 22

                          Construction started: 1963

                          Opened: 1971

                          226

                          What is the duration of the National Anthem of Pakistan?

                          Correct Answer: 80 Seconds
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          About National Anthem of Pakistan:
                          • Written by: Hafeez Jalandhari in 1952
                          • Music by: Ahmad G. Chagla in 1949
                          • Adopted on: August 13, 1954
                          • First recorded by: Ahmed Rushdi in 1954
                          • Re-recorded after 68 years
                          Key Facts:
                          • 15 lines in the National Anthem of Pakistan
                          • 48 words in the National Anthem of Pakistan
                          • First played in front of Raza Shah Rahalvi of Iran
                          • "Jan-i-Istaqbal" means Symbol of ambitions
                          • First played on national radio on August 14, 1947
                          • Duration of the National Anthem of Pakistan: 80 seconds (1 minute 20 seconds)
                          • Pakistan's National Anthem is written in: Persian
                          227

                          The highest Building of Pakistan is ______?

                          Correct Answer: Icon Tower
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The current tallest building in Pakistan is the Bahria Icon Tower in Karachi.

                          At 62 floors, and a height to its spire tip of 273 m

                          Construction started: 2009

                          Topped-out 2017

                          Completed: March 2023

                          228

                          The largest Glacier of Pakistan is _____?

                          Correct Answer: Siachian
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Siachen Glacier is Pakistan's largest Glacier

                          The Siachen Glacier is located in the eastern Karakoram range.

                          where the Line of Control between India and Pakistan ends.

                          ***

                          Siachen Glacier length is 76KM or 72.4km

                           کچھ ذرائع کے مطابق یہ لمبائی 72.4 کلومیٹر یعنی 45 میل ہے۔

                          پیپر میں ان دونوں میں جو بھی آئے اس کے مطابق جواب دیں

                          the height of Siachin Glacier is 20,000 feet

                          Siachen Glacier is the world 2nd largest Glacier

                          It is located in Ghanche District, Gilgit-Baltistan. 

                          The meaning of Siachen Glacier is Abundance of Roses.گلاب کی کثرت۔


                          229

                          The Lansdowne Bridge over Indus river connects ______?

                          Correct Answer: Sukkur – Rohri
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          The Lansdowne Bridge, built in 1889–1897, connects the cities of Sukkur and Rohri across the Indus River in Sindh, Pakistan.
                          It was one of the longest rigid girder bridges in the world at the time of its completion.
                          230

                          KANUPP is the abbreviation of ______?

                          Correct Answer: Karachi nuclear power plant
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          KANUPP stands for Karachi Nuclear Power Plant

                          it is a large commercial nuclear power plant located at Paradise Point in Karachi Sindh 

                          it is Pakistan's first nuclear energy plant

                          it is composed of three commercial nuclear power plants

                          K1: 1 August 1966 (support from Canada) 

                          K2: 20 August 2015 (investment provided by China and IAEA)

                          K3: 31 May 2016 (investment provided by China and IAEA)


                          231

                          The Largest Coal Reservoir are found in ___?

                          Correct Answer: Thar, Sindh
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Pakistan's largest coal reserves are found in Tharparkar Sindh

                          approximately 175 billion tonnes found in Tharparkar

                          The deposits 16th-largest coal reserves in the world, were discovered in 1991

                          Coal resources of Pakistan

                          RegionCoal
                          (billion tonnes)
                          Sindh:184.623
                          Punjab:0.235
                          Balochistan: 0.217
                          KPK:0.092
                          AJK:0.009
                          Grand total185.175
                          232

                          Shah Jahan Mosque is located in _____?

                          Correct Answer: Thatta
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The Shah Jahan Mosque is also known as the Jamia Masjid of Thatta.

                          It was built by Shah Jahan.

                          It's construction started in 1647 and completed in 1659.

                          Shah Jahan also constructed the Shalimar Bagh.

                          233

                          What is the real name of 'Begum Maulana Muhammad Ali Jauhar' or of Bi-Amma (mother of Jauhar brothers)?

                          Correct Answer: Abadi Bano Begum
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          Abadi Bano Begum, the widow of the great Muslim leader Maulana Mohammad Ali Jauhar.
                          She is also the only woman whose signature is affixed on the Pakistan Resolution document.
                          234

                          Which of the following battle laid the foundation of Mughal rule in India?

                          Correct Answer: Battle of Panipat
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The First Battle of Panipat (1526) was fought between Babur and Ibrahim Lodhi.

                          Babur's victory in this battle laid the foundation of Mughal rule in India.

                          235

                          What did Subuktigin, the Turkic slave, do?

                          Correct Answer: Founded the Ghaznavid Empire and opened path to Indian subcontinent
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Subuktigin was a Turkic slave who rose to power and founded the Ghaznavid Empire in the 10th century.

                          His military campaigns in present-day Afghanistan and India paved the way for future invasions, especially by his son Mahmud of Ghazni.

                          236

                          Which country is situated in the east of Pakistan?

                          Correct Answer: India
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          ·        Pakistan borders India 2100 KM long in the east.


                          237

                          How would Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 affect Pakistan?

                          Correct Answer: All of these
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Vision 2030 aims to diversify Saudi Arabia’s economy, opening doors for Pakistani workers and professionals.

                          It encourages foreign investment, including from and into Pakistan.

                          It strengthens bilateral ties through joint projects and strategic cooperation.

                          238

                          After how many years is Pakistan hosting an ICC event in 2025?

                          Correct Answer: 29 years
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Pakistan is hosting an ICC event after 29 years in 2025, as the last ICC tournament hosted by Pakistan was the 1996 Cricket World Cup


                          Know about ICC Champions Trophy 2025

                          Edition: 9th

                          Venues: Pakistan/United Arab Emirates

                          Participating teams: 8

                          Total Matches: 15

                          Starting date: 19 Feb 2025

                          Closing date: 09 March 2025

                          Administrator: International Cricket Council (ICC)

                          ****

                          239

                          TTP ended its ceasefire with the Pakistani government on which date?

                          Correct Answer: September 4, 2022
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          On September 4, 2022, Tehrik-i-Taliban Pakistan (TTP) officially ended its ceasefire agreement with the Pakistani government.

                          The group resumed attacks, citing lack of progress in peace talks.

                          240

                          What is the correct definition of a district?

                          Correct Answer: A division of a country marked for administrative purposes
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          A district is a defined area within a country, organized for administrative or governmental purposes.

                          It may include towns, villages, or cities.

                          It is overseen by local authorities like a Deputy Commissioner.

                          241

                          Which drama is based on autobiography of Malala Yousafzai?

                          Correct Answer: Gul Makai
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Gul Makai is a drama based on the autobiography of Malala Yousafzai.

                          242

                          What does SIFC stand for?

                          Correct Answer: Special Investment Facilitation Council
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          SIFC: Special Investment Facilitation Council

                          Formation: 17th June, 2023

                          Founded by: Shahbaz Sharif

                          Headquarter: Islamabad

                          Purpose: Economic revival of Pakistan, attract Foreign Direct Investment

                          243

                          One unit was dissolved on?

                          Correct Answer: 1st July 1970
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                           The One Unit scheme was dissolved on July 1, 1970.

                          General Yahya Khan imposed Legal Framework Order No. 1970 to end the program

                          and reinstate the provisional status of the Four Provinces as of August 1947.


                          Additional information:

                          The One Unit was the reorganization of the provinces of Pakistan 

                          Prime Minister Muhammad Ali Bogra led it on 22 November 1954

                          and passed on 30 September 1955

                          This program merged the four provinces of West Pakistan into a single province to parallel the province of East Pakistan

                          • West Punjab
                          • Sind
                          • NWFP
                          • Baluchistan
                          244

                          ______ is father of Pashto literature.

                          Correct Answer: Khushal Khan Khattak
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Khushal Khan Khattak (1613–1689) is widely regarded as the father of Pashto literature due to his vast contributions in poetry, prose, and philosophy.

                          He promoted nationalism, unity, and resistance through his powerful Pashto poetry, influencing generations of Pashtun thinkers and writers.

                          245

                          The 2023 Pakistan census revealed the largest population growth in which province?

                          Correct Answer: Sindh
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          As per Pakistan's 2023 Census, Sindh province recorded an annual population growth rate of 2.57%, slightly higher than Punjab’s 2.53% and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa’s 2.38%.

                          Based on that growth rate ranking, Sindh experienced the largest population growth among the listed provinces in 2023.

                          246

                          A proper definition of Muslim is incorporated in the constitution?

                          Correct Answer: 1973
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The 1973 Constitution of Pakistan is the first to define a "Muslim" officially in legal and constitutional terms.

                          Article 260 of the 1973 Constitution includes the definition of a Muslim as someone who believes in the Oneness of Allah and the finality of Prophethood of Muhammad (PBUH).

                          247

                          Kabul, Swat, Kunhar, Panjkora, Bara, Kurram and Gomal are the rivers of ________?

                          Correct Answer: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Khyber Pakhtunkhwa often abbreviated as KPK.

                          It was formerly known as the North-West Frontier Province, is one of the four provinces of Pakistan.

                          It is located in the northwestern region of the country, along the Afghanistan–Pakistan border.

                          248

                          The Abbasids Governor ______ came to Sindh in 140 AH. / 757 AD.

                          Correct Answer: Hisham
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          In 140 AH / 757 AD, Hisham ibn Amr al-Taghlibi, the Abbasid governor of Iraq, was sent to Sindh by the Abbasid Caliphate.
                          He is known for consolidating Abbasid control in Sindh after the decline of Umayyad rule.
                          249

                          Who was the first Muslim Head / C-in-C of Pakistan Army?

                          Correct Answer: Field Marshal Muhammad Ayoub
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Muhammad Ayub Khan 2nd President of Pakistan from 27 October 1958 to 25 March 1969.

                          Muhammad Ayub Khan Commander-in-Chief (C-in-C) of the Pakistan Army from 23 January 1951 to 26 October 1958
                          250

                          In 2024, the highest foreign direct investment (FDI) into Pakistan came from ______?

                          Correct Answer: China
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          In fiscal year 2024 (FY24), Pakistan received approximately USD 1.9 billion in foreign direct investment (FDI), marking a 17% year-on-year increase from FY23.

                          China was the largest investor, contributing around USD 568 million—about 30% of the total FDI—followed by Hong Kong (USD 359 m) and the UK (USD 268 m).

                          251

                          Who was the first Pakistani woman to scale all eight peaks above 8000 meters in 2023?

                          Correct Answer: Naila Kiani
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          • In July 2023, Naila Kiani scaled Broad Peak, making her the first woman from Pakistan to climb all eight-thousanders in the country.
                          • Naila Kiani  is also the first Pakistani woman to summit eight peaks above 8,000m in the world.
                          252

                          The China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC) entered which phase in 2023?

                          Correct Answer: Industrialization
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          In 2023, CPEC entered Phase II, focusing on industrialization, socio-economic development, and agriculture after completing Early Harvest Projects.

                          This phase emphasizes high-quality growth through special economic zones, industrial parks, and science & technology initiatives.

                          253

                          Pakistan faced a major electricity blackout in January 2023 during its _____?

                          Correct Answer: Faulty grid synchronization
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          On 23 January 2023, a national grid frequency fluctuation caused a massive blackout affecting 220 million people in Pakistan.

                          The outage was due to faulty grid synchronization during energy-saving shutdowns, leading to unstable frequency (~50.75 Hz) and a 12-hour power collapse.

                          254

                          What is the main theme of Pakistan’s updated National Security Policy (2022 to 2026)?

                          Correct Answer: Economic security
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Pakistan’s first-ever National Security Policy (NSP) for 2022–2026 places economic security at its core, marking a shift from the traditional focus solely on military strength.

                          It emphasizes building a strong economy to bolster overall national resilience.

                          255

                          Pakistan signed a long-term agreement with which country for discounted oil in 2023?

                          Correct Answer: Russia
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          In 2023, Pakistan entered into a long‑term commercial agreement with Russia to import crude oil at a discounted rate (approximately $60/barrel, around $10 under market price).

                          The first agreed cargo of ~100,000 metric tonnes began arriving in late 2023, with Pakistani refineries.

                          Such as Cnergyico and Pakistan Refinery Limited—receiving the shipment and refining the oil locally.

                          256

                          Which section of the Election Act pertains to the allotment of symbols to political parties?

                          Correct Answer: Article 217
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Section 217 of the Elections Act 2017. The Constitution has articles, and acts have sections






                          257

                          Kerry lugar bill was passed for the period

                          Correct Answer: 2010-2014
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The 'Enhanced Partnership with Pakistan Act of 2009

                          also known as the Kerry-Lugar-Berman Act

                          It was passed by the US Congress on 24 September and signed by President Obama on 15 October.

                          This bill provides Pakistan with a non-military aid package of US$1.5 billion annually, for the next five years

                          It was proposed by Senators John Kerry (D-Massachusetts) and Richard Lugar (R-Indiana).

                          258

                          Pakistan Olympic Gold Medalist in 2024?

                          Correct Answer: Arshad Nadeem
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Arshad Nadeem set a new Olympic record by clinching the coveted gold medal in the men’s javelin final

                          This ended Pakistan’s 32-year wait for success at the Games 

                          Throw: 92.97-m throw

                          Winning date: 8 Aug 2024

                          259

                          The council that resolves disputes between the Federation and Provincial Assemblies is?

                          Correct Answer: Council of Common Interest
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The Council of Common Interest (CCI) is a constitutional body in Pakistan,

                          Established under Article 153 of the Constitution.

                          Formed: 1973

                          Headquarters: Islamabad

                          Its members include:

                          • The Prime Minister (Chairman)
                          • Chief Ministers of the Provinces
                          • Three members nominated by the Prime Minister from the Federal Government

                          Until 2010 the body worked under Cabinet Division

                          After the passing of the 18th amendment the body was transferred to the Ministry of Inter Provincial Coordination on 4 March 2010.

                          260

                          What was a significant development in Pakistan-Iran relations in January 2024?

                          Correct Answer: Border clashes
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The most significant development in Pakistan-Iran relations in January 2024 was the border clashes


                          Date: 16 January – 18 January 2024 (2 days)

                          Location: Balochistan, Pakistan, Sistan and Baluchestan, Iran

                          Result:

                          Inconclusive

                          Both sides agree to a ceasefire

                          261

                          How many members are elected to the National Assembly of Pakistan for a term of five years?

                          Correct Answer: 272/342 (266/366)
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          According to Article 51 (1) and 3(a) of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan

                          Article 51. (1) There shall be [three hundred and thirty-six] seats for members in the National Assembly, including seats reserved for women and non-Muslims.

                           

                           Province / AreaGeneral SeatsWomen SeatsNon-MuslimTotal Seats
                          Balochistan16420
                          Khyber Pakhtunkhwa451055
                          Punjab14132173
                          Sindh611475

                          1010
                          Total2666010336
                          262

                          The book "Modern Muslim India and the Birth of Pakistan (1858-1951)" was written by _____?

                          Correct Answer: S.M. Ikram
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The book “Modern Muslim India and the Birth of Pakistan, 1858–1951” was written by S. M. Ikram.

                          It was first published in 1965 as an expanded English version of his earlier Urdu works.

                          263

                          According to 2024-2025 reports, which is Pakistan's most polluted city?

                          Correct Answer: Lahore
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          From 2024 and early 2025, Lahore, Pakistan, has consistently been identified as one of the most polluted cities in the world

                          Lahore is becoming the world's most polluted city with an AQI of 394 in October 2024

                          Pakistan ranked the third most polluted globally in 2024 by IQAir, with an average PM2.5 concentration of 73.7 μg/m3. 

                          264

                          Who wrote the book "Christmatic Leader and Creator of Pakistan"?

                          Correct Answer: Sikandar Hayat
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The book "The Charismatic Leader: Quaid-i-Azam Mohammad Ali Jinnah and the Creation of Pakistan" was written by Sikandar Hayat.

                          He is a historian who has extensively written on Jinnah and the Pakistan Movement.

                          The book examines Jinnah's leadership through the lens of Weber's concept of charisma. 

                          265

                          What was the main reason for Pakistan’s worst economic crisis in 2023?

                          Correct Answer: Balance-of-payments crisis and foreign reserve depletion
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          In 2023, Pakistan faced a severe balance-of-payments crisis due to rising imports and low reserves.

                          This caused currency devaluation, inflation, and debt repayment issues—crippling the economy.

                          266

                          Which is the highest court of justice in Pakistan?

                          Correct Answer: Supreme Court of Pakistan
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The highest court of justice in Pakistan is the Supreme Court of Pakistan.

                          It has the ultimate authority in judicial matters and oversees the constitutionality of laws and decisions.

                          267

                          Construction of Taj Mahal was completed in ______?

                          Correct Answer: 1647
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Taj Mahal

                          Construction started

                          1632

                          Construction completed

                          1647

                          Built by

                          Mughal Emperor Shah Jehan

                          Built in the memory of

                          Mumtaz Mahal

                          Height

                          73 M

                          Taj Mahal

                          Most visited monument of India as of July 2022

                          Architecture

                          Ustad Ahmed Lahori
                          268

                          In Pakistan Monsoon rain constitute about how much percent of the annual rainfall that occurs in the months from June to September?

                          Correct Answer: 70%
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Monsoon rains contribute approximately 70% of Pakistan’s total annual rainfall, mainly during the months of June to September.

                          These rains are crucial for agriculture but can also cause flooding due to intense downpours.

                          269

                          After the official visit of prime minister Liaquat Ali Khan to Iran. When did Shah of Iran visited Pakistan for the first time?

                          Correct Answer: 1950
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The Shah of Iran, Mohammad Reza Pahlavi, visited Pakistan for the first time in March 1950.

                          This visit strengthened Pakistan-Iran diplomatic relations, leading to future alliances like RCD (1964) and ECO (1985).

                          270

                          After independence, who were in majority among teachers in East Pakistan?

                          Correct Answer: Hindu Teachers
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          After independence in 1947, Hindu teachers were still in majority in East Pakistan, especially in educational institutions.

                          This was due to the historical dominance of Hindus in the education sector before partition, and many continued until migration increased later.

                          271

                          What was the title of the pamphlet in which Chaudhry Rahmat Ali proposed the name "Pakistan"?

                          Correct Answer: Now or Never
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The word was first introduced now or never in 1933 by Chaudhry Rahmat Ali.

                          The "Pakistan Declaration" titled Now or Never means Are We to Live or Perish Forever?

                          In this pamphlet, the word Pakistan was used for the first time 

                          This pamphlet was derived from the two-nation theory of Muslim reformer Syed Ahmed Khan

                          it was a pamphlet, referring to the names of the five northern regions of British India: 

                          1. Punjab
                          2. Afghanistan
                          3. Kashmir
                          4. Sindh
                          5. Baluchistan. (Pakistan)

                          ***


                          272

                          Who was the first female member of the All India Muslim League?

                          Correct Answer: Begum Jahan Ara Shahnawaz
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The first female member of the All India Muslim League was Begum Jahanara Shahnawaz.

                          She also founded the Punjab Provincial Women's Muslim League in 1935.

                          273

                          Which group helped Indian forces during the attacks in East Pakistan?

                          Correct Answer: Mukti Bahani
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Mukti Bahini was a Bengali guerrilla force that actively supported Indian forces during the 1971 war in East Pakistan.

                          They played a key role in the creation of Bangladesh, fighting against Pakistani military control.

                          274

                          When was the caliphate officially abolished by Turkey?

                          Correct Answer: March 1924
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          The Ottoman Caliphate was officially abolished on 3rd March 1924 by Mustafa Kemal Atatürk, the founder of modern Turkey.
                          This marked the end of the centuries-old Islamic Caliphate, which had been symbolically held by the Ottoman Sultans.
                          275

                          What initial belief did Sir Syed Ahmad Khan originally hold regarding the relationship between Hindus and Muslims in India?

                          Correct Answer: They are one united nation
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Sir Syed Ahmad Khan initially believed that Hindus and Muslims were "two eyes of the same beautiful bride" — India, and could coexist peacefully.

                          Over time, due to political and social developments, his views evolved toward recognizing their distinct identities.

                          276

                          What was one of Babur's major contributions after founding the empire?

                          Correct Answer: Developing an efficient administration
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Babur laid the foundation of a centralized administrative system in the Mughal Empire after its establishment in 1526.

                          He introduced organized revenue collection and governance practices that were later refined by his successors.

                          277

                          What is the Power generation capacity of Tarbela Dam?

                          Correct Answer: 3478 MW
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Tarbela Dam is located in Pakistan and is the world's largest fill-type dam.

                          It is built over the River Indus near the small town of Tarbela in the Haripur District.

                          278

                          The Indus river falls into the _______?

                          Correct Answer: Arabian sea
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Indus River Falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi

                          Indus River flows into the Arabian Sea called Indus Delta (southern Sindh province of Pakistan)

                          Indus Delta Designated on 5 November 2002

                          All important question "INDUS RIVER"

                          The Indus River starts in Tibet

                          The river Indus originates from the Mansarovar Lake

                          The Indus River flows through India, Pakistan,

                          The Indus River finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.

                          River Indus starts making a delta near the city of Thatta

                          The old name of the Indus River was: Sindhu

                          National River of Pakistan: Indus River

                          6 Barrages constructed in the Indus River

                          Indus River Length: 3,180 km

                          Indus River 2nd source: Gar Tsangpo

                          The upper Indus Plain ends at Mithankot 

                          279

                          Hazrat Lal Shahbaz Qalandar was born in _____?

                          Correct Answer: Marwand
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The full name of Hazrat Lal Shahbaz Qalandar: Syed Shah Hussain Marwandi

                          Lal Shahbaz Qalandar wrote several books in Persian and Arabic

                          Lal Shahbaz Qalandar was a Sufi saint and poet of present-day Pakistan and Afghanistan

                          Born: 1177, Marand, Iran

                          Died: February 19, 1274, Sehwān

                          Place of burial: Darbar Hazrat Usman Marvandi Lal Shahbaz Qalandar, Sehwān

                          Title: Qalandar

                          The shrine of Hazrat Laal Shahbaz Qalandar is situated in Sehwan Sindh

                          280

                          Which of the following place is associated with the famous romance of Sasui-Punhu love story?

                          Correct Answer: Banbhore
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Banbhore is traditionally associated with the famous Sindhi folktale of Sasui-Punhu.

                          Sasui wandered through the desert in search of her lover Punhu, and Banbhore is linked to her journey.

                          Sassi Punnu is a legendary tale from Sindhi folklore.

                          Hashim Shah was a sufi poet and Punjabi writer.

                          Sassi is known as Princess of Hope.

                          281

                          The tallest tree in Pakistan is _____?

                          Correct Answer: Deodar Tree
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The national tree of Pakistan is the deodar tree.

                          It also known as the Himalayan cedar.

                          The deodar’s scientific name is Cedrus deodara.

                          The deodar is a tall tree that can grow to be 40–50 meters tall, with trunks up to 3 meters in diameter.

                          The wood of the deodar is light, close-grained, and rot-resistant.

                          The deodar’s name comes from Sanskrit and means “wood of the god”.

                          282

                          Who resigned as a President of Pakistan to become the Prime Minister of Pakistan?

                          Correct Answer: Zulifqar Ali Bhutto
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Zulfikar Ali Bhutto served as the President of Pakistan from 1971 to 1973.

                          After the 1973 Constitution made the Prime Minister the chief executive, he resigned as President and became the Prime Minister of Pakistan in August 1973.

                          283

                          The oldest University of Sindh province is ______?

                          Correct Answer: Sindh University Jamshoro
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The oldest University of Sindh province is Sindh University Jamshoro.

                          It was initially established in Karachi in 1947 under the University of Sindh Act, 1947.

                          Later relocated to Jamshoro in 1951, becoming a full-fledged teaching university.

                          While Sindh Madressatul Islam was founded as a school in 1885 and became a college in 1943, it was granted university status much later in 2012.

                          284

                          Shahi Qila is located in which city of Pakistan?

                          Correct Answer: Lahore
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Lahore Fort was built by Emperor Akbar.

                          It is also known as Shahi Qila.

                          Initiated the construction in the 1560s and completed in 1566.

                          It is located in the northern part of Lahore's old walled city.

                          The fort's Alamgiri gate is part of an ensemble of buildings, which along with the:

                          • Badshahi Mosque
                          • Roshnai Gate
                          • and Samadhi of Ranjit Singh

                           History:

                          The Shah Jahan period is characterized by luxurious marble.

                          Alamgri Gate Built by Aurangzeb

                          Badshahi mosque Is in Lahore Built by Aurangzeb in 1673

                          285

                          Hunza is in which division of Gilgit Baltistan?

                          Correct Answer: Gilgit
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Hunza Valley: Mountainous valley in Gilgit-Baltistan.

                          Location: Situated along the Hunza River.

                          Attraction: Known for its snow-capped, rugged mountains.

                          Administrative Division: Located in the Gilgit Division of Gilgit-Baltistan (specifically, Hunza District).

                          286

                          Urs Mubarak of Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai is celebrated in the month of _____?

                          Correct Answer: Safar
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Urs Mubarak of Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai is celebrated in the month of Safar.

                          It begins on the 14th day of Safar, the second month of the Islamic (Hijra) calendar, and spans three days.

                          Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai's father was Shah Habib.

                          The shrine of Shah Abdul Latif Bhitai is located in Bhit Shah.

                          Bhit Shah is a small town located in Matiari District, Sindh.

                          Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai was the greatest Muslim poet of the Sindhi language.

                          He is also known as the poet of Sindh.

                          Born on 18 November 1689.

                          Died on 1 January 1752.

                          Main Ghulam Shah Kalhoro built the tomb of Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai.

                          287

                          In which year Muslim league was established in Balochistan?

                          Correct Answer: 1939
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          In 1939 a local lawyer, Qazi Muhammad Isa, created the Balochistan Muslim League.

                          In Pishin at a mosque meeting.

                          288

                          When did Arfa Karim die?

                          Correct Answer: January 14, 2012
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Arfa Karim (1995-2012) was a Pakistani student and computer prodigy.

                          She became the youngest Microsoft Certified Professional (MCP) at the age of 9 in 2004.

                          She was invited to visit Microsoft headquarters in Redmond, Washington, and met with Bill Gates.

                          289

                          The Khyber Pass links which two countries?

                          Correct Answer: Afghanistan & Pakistan
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Karakar pass is located in: Hindu Kush, KPK

                          Important Passes of Pakistan:

                          1. Khyber Pass 

                          connects: Peshawar to Kabul

                          connects: Pakistan to Afghanistan

                          Height 1070 meters, 3510 feet 


                          2. Bolan Pass 

                          Connects: JCB and Sibi with Quetta 

                          Height: 1793 meters, 5884 feet 


                          3. Lowari Pass 

                          Connects: Chitral with Dir 

                          Height: 3118 meters, 10230 feet 


                          4. Broghil Pass 

                          Location: Pakistan & Afghanistan border 

                          Height: 3798 meters, 12460 feet 


                          5. Malakand Pass 

                          Connects: Dir with Chitral 

                          Height: 1362 meters, 4468 feet 


                          6. Shandur pass

                          Connects: Gupis/Gilgit with Chitral 

                          Height: 3700 meters, 12139 feet 


                          7. Khanjerab Pass (top most in Pakistan) 

                          Other name: Friendship Gate

                          Connects: China with Pakistan 

                          Height: 4693 meters, 15397 feet 


                          8. Dorah pass

                          Connects: Badakhshan province of Afghanistan with Chitral 

                          Height: 4300 meters, 14108 feet 


                          9. Babusar Pass 

                          Connects: Mansehra/Abbotabad with Gilgit


                          10. Kohat Pass

                          Connects: Kohat with Peshawar 


                          11. Khojak Pass

                          Connects: Qilla Abdullah with Chaman


                          12. Tochi Pass

                          Connects: Ghazni with Bannu


                          13. Karakar Pass

                          Connects: Mingora with Buner 


                          290

                          How many times Pakistan won the hockey World Cup?

                          Correct Answer: 4
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Pakistan is the most successful national field hockey team in the Hockey World Cup.

                          Pakistanis the four-time champion: 

                          1. 1971
                          2. 1978
                          3. 1982
                          4. 1994
                          291

                          Who was Amir Khusro?

                          Correct Answer: All of these
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Amir Khusrau was a poet, historian, and musician.

                          He is also known as the "Parrot of India".

                          He is widely regarded as one of the greatest Persian-language poets of medieval India.

                          Amir Khusrau was a disciple of the Sufi saint Hazrat Nizamuddin Auliya, of the Chishti Order. 

                          292

                          The largest district of the Punjab by population is ____?

                          Correct Answer: Lahore
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          **********

                          Total District in Pakistan 136

                          Facts about Pakistan Azla/Districts

                          1. How many districts are in Punjab?

                          36✔

                          And 9 Divisions

                           

                          2. How many districts in KPK?

                          35 (after 25th amendment 1973- FATA merge)

                          And 8 Divisions

                           

                          3. How many districts in Baluchistan?

                          35✔

                          And 7 Divisions

                           

                          4. How many districts in Sindh?

                          30✔

                          And 7 Divisions


                          5. How many districts in Gilgit Baltistan?

                          14✔

                           

                          6. How many districts are in Azad Kashmir?

                          10✔

                           

                          7. The largest district of Pakistan by population?

                          Lahore✔

                           

                          8. The smallest districts of Pakistan by area?

                          Tor Ghar✔ (KPK)

                          9. The largest districts of Pakistan by area?

                          Chagai✔(Balochistan)

                            

                          10. The largest districts of Punjab by area?

                          Bhalwarpur✔

                          293

                          How many Round Table Conferences were attended by Allama Iqbal?

                          Correct Answer: 2
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          • First round table conference: 1930
                          • 2nd round table conference: 1931
                          • 3rd round table conference:1932
                          • All round table conference held in London
                          • ***
                          • The three Round Table Conferences of 1930–32 were a series of conferences to formulate the future constitution of India in the light of suggestions given by the Indian Leaders
                          • ***
                          • Gandhi a leader does not attend the first round table conference 

                          • The three Round Table Conferences of 1930–32 were a series of conferences to formulate future constitution of India in the light of suggestions given by the Indian Leaders

                            Sir Agha Khan also first president of Muslim League.

                          The Largest Radio Station found in Pakistan is _____?

                          Correct Answer: Islamabad
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Largest Radio Station Found In Pakistan Is Islamabad.


                          ******

                          The following three radio stations existed at the time of independence of Pakistan on 14 August 1947.

                          1.      Peshawar Radio Station was established in 1935

                          2.      Lahore radio station established in 1937

                          3.      Dhaka radio station established in 1939

                          295

                          شاعر مشرق کے نام سے کون سے شاعر پہچانے جاتے ہیں؟

                          Correct Answer: علامہ اقبال
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          ڈاکٹر سر علامہ محمد اقبال (ولادت: 9 نومبر 1877ء - وفات: 21 اپریل 1938ء)

                          ۔ کونسی کتا ب علامہ اقبال کی وفات کے بعد شائع ہوئی؟ ارمغان حجاز

                          ۔ اردو نثر میں علامہ اقبال کی پہلی باقاعدہ تصنیف کا کیا نام ہے؟ علم الا قتصاد

                          The shrine of Shah Abdul Latif Bhitai is located in Bhit Shah.

                          Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai was the greatest Muslim poet of the Sindhi language.

                          He is also known as the poet of Sindh.

                          297

                          Who delivered the Presidential address at Allahabad in 1930?

                          Correct Answer: Allama Iqbal
                          Show explanation

                          Allama Iqbal Join Muslim League in 1908 in London and in subcontinent in 1922

                          Sir Muhammad Iqbal's 1930 Presidential Address to the 25th Session of the All-India Muslim League Allahabad, 29 December 1930.

                          Allama Iqbal is a National Poet of Pakistan

                          Allama Iqbal Born: November 9, 1877, Sialkot

                          Allama Iqbal Died: April 21, 1938, in Lahore

                          Allama Iqbal Place of burial: Tomb of Allama Iqbal, Lahore

                          298

                          Who is the youngest professional boxer in Pakistan?

                          Correct Answer: Ali Abbas Jeddy
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          The youngest professional boxer in Pakistan is Ali Abbas Jeddy, who is 19 years old.
                          He is also the youngest Pakistani fighter to achieve a 6-0 record, defeating Chris Wong of Great Britain.
                          Ali is also a skilled footballer, playing as a striker for his school team.
                          299

                          In which City (District) Manchhar Lake is situated?

                          Correct Answer: Jamshoro
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Manchhar Lake is the largest natural freshwater lake in Jamshoro Sindh Pakistan.

                          It collects water from the Kithar mountain range.

                          It is one of South Asia's largest.

                          Manchhar Lake is the largest natural freshwater lake in Pakistan.

                          Manchhar Lake covers an area of  23.5 km (14.6 mi).

                          It is part of Indus River basin.

                          300

                          The real name of Hazrat Sachal Sarmast is _____?

                          Correct Answer: Abdul Wahab
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The real name of Hazrat Sachal Sarmast was Abdul Wahab.

                          Hazrat Sachal Sarmast born 1152 Hijri (1739 CE).

                          Hazrat Sachal Sarmast died 1242 Hijri (1827 CE).

                          Hazrat Sachal Sarmast is known as: a poet of seven languages (شاعر ہفت زبان)

                          301

                          Mohenjo Daro means __________?

                          Correct Answer: Mound of the Dead
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Mohenjo-Daro means Mound of the Dead

                          This is because after it had declined, the remains started to have layers over them.

                          it was first recognized in 1922

                          It was discovered by R. D. Banerji

                          It is located in Larkana upper Sindh

                           it was the largest settlement of the ancient Indus Valley Civilisation



                          302

                          As Prime Minister, Imran Khan first visited officially which country?

                          Correct Answer: Saudi Arabia
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Inaugural two-day visit to meet King Salman of Saudi Arabia in Jeddah.

                          to discuss matters of regional and bilateral interest.

                          Khan also performed the Umrah in Mecca and visited the Prophet's Mosque in Medina.

                          Date: 18–19 September 2018

                          303

                          Muhammad bin Qasim Mosque is located at _____?

                          Correct Answer: Aror
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The mosque is located in Aror city, opposite Govt. Elementary Campus School Aror, Taluka Rohri, District Sukkur.

                          Muhammad bin Qasim is said to have built this mosque in Aror, which was the capital of Sindh at the time of his conquest.

                          304

                          سر سید احمد خان کس تعلیمی ادارے کے بانی تھے؟

                          Correct Answer: علی گڑھ کالج
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          سر سید احمد خان نے 1875 میں علی گڑھ کالج (بعد میں علی گڑھ مسلم یونیورسٹی) کی بنیاد رکھی۔
                          اس ادارے کا مقصد مسلمانوں کو جدید تعلیم سے آراستہ کرنا تھا۔
                          305

                          K-2 is also known as ______?

                          Correct Answer: Godwin Austen
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          K-2 is the second largest mountain after Mount Everest in the world.

                          K-2 is the highest mountain in Pakistan.

                          306

                          When the first constitution was abrogated and the first martial law was enforced/proclaimed?

                          Correct Answer: 7th October 1958
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Ø  Iskandar Ali Mirza who was the first president of Pakistan abrogated the constitution of 1956 on 07-October-1958.

                          Ø  Ayoub Khan was made chief martial law after this abrogation.

                          Ø  Feroz Khan Noun was the prime minister at the time of the first Martial Law in Pakistan.

                          ******

                          List of Martial Laws Held in Pakistan from 1947 to 2022

                          Sr#

                           Martial Law Number

                          Date of Martial Law

                          Martial Law Implemented by

                          1

                          First Martial Law

                          7th October 1958

                          Iskandar Mirza

                          2

                          2nd Martial Law

                          25th March 1969

                          General Yahya Khan

                          3

                          3rd Martial Law

                          25th July 1977

                          General Zia ul Haq

                          4

                          4th Martial Law

                          12th October 1999

                          General Pervaiz Musharraf

                           

                          There are total four martial laws Implemented in Pakistan from 1947 to 2022

                          پاکستان کا قومی ترانہ کس نے لکھا تھا؟

                          حفیظ جھالندری 1982 میں لاہور میں انتقال کرگئے۔

                          308

                          Port Qasim is near the which city in Pakistan?

                          Correct Answer: Karachi
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Port Qasim is located in Karachi, Sindh,  Pakistan.

                          It is one of the busiest ports in Pakistan.

                          It is situated on the coast of the Arabian Sea.

                          It is approximately 35 kilometers east of Karachi's city center.

                          Opened: September 1980

                          309

                          Which Russian leader supported the Two-Nation Theory?

                          Correct Answer: Stalin
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Joseph Stalin supported the idea of the Two-Nation Theory as a principle of national self-determination.

                          He recognized that Muslims and Hindus in India were distinct nations with separate identities.

                          310

                          The ruler of Junagarh at the time of Independence was ____?

                          Correct Answer: Muslim
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The ruler of Junagarh at the time of independence was Nawab Muhammad Mahabat Khanji III.

                          He was a Muslim ruler.

                          He initially opted to join Pakistan.

                          This decision was made despite the majority Hindu population in the state.

                          311

                          Who was the first ruler among these to accept the title of Khalifa (Caliph's delegate) in the Delhi Sultanate?

                          Correct Answer: Iltutmish
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Iltutmish (Reign: 1211–1236 CE):

                          The first Delhi Sultan to formally accept the title of "Naib-i-Amir-ul-Momineen" (Deputy of the Caliph) from the Abbasid Caliph in Baghdad (1229 CE).

                          This legitimized his rule under Islamic law.

                          312

                          Tulaja Fort is located in which of the following places?

                          Correct Answer: Soan Valley
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Tulaja Fort is a ruined fort in Soan Valley, Punjab, Pakistan.

                          In October 2021, a team of researchers found that the site is two thousand years old.


                          313

                          Who is popularly known as the "Saviour of Pakistan"?

                          Correct Answer: Dr. Abdul Qadeer Khan
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                           Dr. Abdul Qadeer Khan is widely regarded as the "Saviour of Pakistan"

                          "Mohsin-e-Pakistan" (Savior of Pakistan).

                          He is colloquially known as the father of Pakistan's atomic weapons program.


                          Science Advisor to the Presidential Secretariat:In office 1 January 2001 – 31 January 2004

                          Born: 1 April 1936, Bhopal, Bhopal State, India

                          Died: 10 October 2021 (aged 85) Islamabad

                          Political party: Tehreek-e-Tahaffuz-e-Pakistan (2012–2013)

                          Awards

                          • Nishan-i-Imtiaz (1996; 1999)
                          • Hilal-i-Imtiaz (1989)

                           It was first published in 2011.

                          حفیظ خان کا کون سا ناول بہاولپور کی تحریک پر مبنی ہے؟

                          Correct Answer: ادھ ادھورے لوگ
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          محمد حفیظ خان نے ادھ ادھورے لوگ میں سرزمین بہاول پور کی صدیوں سے پسی ہوئی عوام کے کرب اور درد کو زبان دی۔

                          وہاں کا متروک کلچر، زبان، محاورہ، تہذیب وثقافت ...


                          316

                          "تنقید کی آزادی" کس کی کتاب ہے؟

                          Correct Answer: مظفر علی سید
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          مظفر علی سید 6 دسمبر، 1929ء کو امرتسر، برطانوی ہندوستان میں پیدا ہوئے۔

                          ان کی تصانیف میں تنقید کی آزادی، احمد ندیم قاسمی کے بہترین افسانے، یادوں کی سرگم اور تراجم میں فکشن، فن اور فلسفہ اور پاک فضائیہ کی تاریخ کے نام شامل ہیں۔


                          317

                          انجم خیالی کا تعلق کس شہر سے تھا؟

                          Correct Answer: جہلم
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          انجم خیالی کا تعلق جہلم شہر سے تھا۔

                          وہ جہلم میں پیدا ہوئے اور بعد میں میرپور میں بھی رہے۔

                          بعد ازاں وہ انگلستان چلے گئے اور بریڈفورڈ میں بس کنڈکٹر کے طور پر کام کیا، بتایا گیا ہے


                          318

                          صحیفۂ امام زین العابدینؓ کس کے متعلق ہے؟

                          Correct Answer: دعاؤں کا مجموعہ
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
