                           صحیفۂ امام زین العابدینؓ، جسے صحیفہ کاملہ یا صحیفہ سجادیہ بھی کہا جاتا ہے،

                          حضرت امام زین العابدین علیہ السلام کی دعاؤں کا مجموعہ ہے۔

                          یہ کتاب اہل تشیع کے نزدیک بہت اہمیت کی حامل ہے اور اسے زبور آل محمد اور انجیل اہل بیت کے نام سے بھی جانا جاتا ہے۔

                          اس میں امام سجاد علیہ السلام کی 45 دعائیں اور مناجاتیں شامل ہیں جو دینی معارف اور تعلیمات کا خزانہ ہیں۔ 


                          319

                          مجلس ترقی ادب کا دفتر کہاں واقع ہے؟

                          Correct Answer: لاہور
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          مجلس ترقی ادب کا دفتر لاہور میں واقع ہے،

                          ٹھیک ٹھیک 2-کلب روڈ پر. یہ ادارہ حکومت پنجاب کے ماتحت کام کرتا ہے اور اس کا قیام جولائی 1954ء میں عمل میں آیا. 


                          320

                          Who established the Tahreek-e-Niswan (Women's Movement) organization?

                          Correct Answer: Sheema Kermani
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Tahreek-e-Niswan:

                          Founded in 1979 by Sheema Kermani, a renowned Pakistani dancer, activist, and feminist.

                          Focuses on women’s rights, gender equality, and cultural activism through theater and grassroots campaigns.

                          321

                          First school for transgenders in Punjab inaugurated in the city of ______?

                          Correct Answer: Lahore
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The Punjab School Education Department (SED) on December 7, 2022 inaugurated first transgender school in Lahore to educate and teach skills to transpersons.

                           

                          322

                          Gawadar is a part of which province?

                          Correct Answer: Baluchistan
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Pakistan purchased Gwadar port from Oman on 8th September 1958.

                          Gwadar officially became part of Pakistan on 8th December 1958.

                          Gwadar remained under Omani rule for 174 years from 1783 -1958 

                          Gwadar Port is situated on the Arabian Sea at Gwadar in Balochistan province.

                          Opened: 2002 

                          while 

                          Pakistan has three big sea ports  It was purchased from Oman in 1958.

                          Karachi Port,

                          Muhammad Bin Qasim port

                          and Gwadar port.

                          323

                          The Kirthar Range is near to _____?

                          Correct Answer: Dadu
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          • The elevation of the Kirthar Mountains is 2,260 meters (7,410 feet).
                            • The highest peak in the Kirthar Range is Zardak Peak.
                            • It stands at 7,430 feet (2,260 meters).
                            • The Kirthar Range is situated along the border of Balochistan and Sindh provinces.
                            • The hill station of Gorakh, located in the Kirthar Mountains, is situated off Dadu in Sindh.
                            324

                            “APP” news agency belongs to ______?

                            Correct Answer: Pakistan
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            “APP” news agency belongs to Pakistan

                            APP stands for Associated Press of Pakistan

                            It was Founded in 1949

                            Its Headquarters is located in Islamabad



                            News Agencies of Important Countries

                            China: Xinhua

                            Russia: TASS (Telegrafnoye agentstvo Sovetskogo Soyuza) 

                            India: Press Trust of India (PTI)

                            France: AFP (Agence France-Presse) (also the oldest news agency)

                            Italy: ANSA (Agenzia Nazionale Stampa Associata)

                            USA: Associated Press (AP)

                            Britain: Reuters 

                            Malaysia: Bernama 

                            Israel: ITIM

                            Bahrain: BNA (Bahrain News Agency)

                            Iran: FARS

                            Syria: SANA(Syrian Arab News Agency)

                            Yemen: SABA

                            Indonesia: Antara 

                            Kuwait: KUNA (Kuwait News Agency)

                            Kenya: KNA (Kenya News Agency)


                            325

                            Nadvat ul Ulema started working in ______?

                            Correct Answer: 1898
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            Nadwat-ul-Ulama (Darul Uloom Nadwatul Ulama) was founded in Lucknow.

                            Start of Functioning: September 26, 1898, at Khatoon Manzil, Lucknow.

                            Ceremonial Inauguration: October 1898.

                            Purpose: Reform madrasa education to align with modern needs.

                            First Manager: Muhammad Ali Mungeri.

                            Current Manager: Bilal Abdul Hai Hasani Nadwi.

                            326

                            When was the Grand Jamia Mosque in Karachi built?

                            Correct Answer: 2015
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            The Grand Jamia Mosque in Bahria Town, Karachi, was started in 2015.

                            Construction was completed in nearly 2022.

                            Famous Pakistani architect Nayyar Ali Dada designed the Grand Jamia Masjid Karachi.

                            Grand Jamia Mosque, Karachi also known as Bahria Town Jamia Masjid Complex is a cultural complex under construction in Bahria Town Karachi, Pakistan.

                            When completed, the complex will include Pakistan’s largest and world’s third largest mosque according to capacity.

                            327

                            Sidhnai Headworks has been constructed on river?

                            Correct Answer: Ravi
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            Sidhnai Headworks has been constructed on the Ravi River.

                            The new Sidhnai Barrage was constructed 31000 ft upstream of old Sidhnai weir with design capacity of 150,000 Cs

                            Downstream straight reach was excavated and widened to increase the capacity from 100,000 Cs to 150,000 Cs.

                            It is the last Headworks over Ravi River before its confluence into Chanab River about 15 KM ahead.
                            It is situated about 2 KM from Abdul Hakeem.
                            328

                            Bhangra is the dance of _____?

                            Correct Answer: Punjab
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            Bhangra is a folk dance of Punjab.

                            ***

                            Khattak dance is also the National dance of Pakistan.

                            Khattak Dance is a swift martial dance usually performed by the tribesmen from the agile Khattak tribe of Pashtuns

                            Dhamal is one of the most popular folk dances in Sindh

                            Attan is a famous Pakistani folk dance performed in the areas of KPK and Baluchistan.

                            Luddi is also one of the most popular folk dances in Punjab.

                            Jhumar is also one of the many popular traditional dances of Punjab.


                            329

                            Who proposed the 4-Point Formula for Kashmir?

                            Correct Answer: Pervez Musharraf
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            Pervez Musharraf (former President of Pakistan) introduced the 4-Point Formula in December 5, 2006 during India-Pakistan peace talks. Key elements included:

                            1. Self-governance for Kashmiris.
                            2. Demilitarization on both sides of the LoC.
                            3. Joint management (India-Pakistan cooperation).
                            4. No change in borders (but softening the LoC).
                            330

                            When was Quaid-e-Azam first appointed as Third Presidency Magistrate in Bombay?

                            Correct Answer: 1900
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            In 1900, P. H. Dastoor, a Bombay presidency magistrate, left the post temporarily and Jinnah succeeded in getting the interim position.

                            After his six-month appointment period, Jinnah was offered a permanent position on a 1,500 rupee per month salary. But he refuses

                            331

                            مرزا غالب کی وفات کس سن میں ہوئی؟

                            Correct Answer: 1869
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            مرزا غالب 27 دسمبر 1797 کو آگرہ میں پیدا ہوئے۔

                             مرزا غالب 15 فروری 1869ء کو انتقال کر گئے تھے۔

                            332

                            Dr. Umar bin Daudpoto was born at _____?

                            Correct Answer: Talti
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            Dr. Umar bin Muhammad Daudpota was born on 25 March 1896 (or 1 June 1897) in the village of Talti.

                            It is located in Sehwan Sharif, Dadu District (now District Jamshoro), British India (present‑day Sindh, Pakistan).

                            333

                            Two-Nation theory is the basis of creation of _____?

                            Correct Answer: Pakistan
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation


                            Syed Ahmad Khan who presented the theory of the two nations 

                            He presented this theory In a speech at Meerut in 1866 

                            The two-nation theory is the basis of the creation of Pakistan.


                            The theory advocated by the All India Muslim League is the founding principle of the Pakistan Movement.

                            The state of India officially rejected the two-nation theory


                            Aga Khan

                            He called on the British Raj to consider Muslims to be a separate nation from India,

                            the so-called 'Two Nation Theory"

                            Abdul

                            Ghaffar

                            khan

                            He founded the Khudai Khidmatgar commonly known as the RED SHIRTS,

                            during the 1920s

                            Awarded Bharat Ratna in 1987

                            Abbas 

                            Tyabji

                            He is also known as the Grand Old Man of Gujarat

                            Served as the Chief Justice of Baroda State

                            Tyabji launched the Dharasana Satyagraha. in 1930



                            334

                            علیگڑھ مسلم یونیورسٹی کا بنیاد کس نے رکھا؟

                            Correct Answer: سر سید احمد خان
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            علیگڑھ مسلم یونیورسٹی کی بنیاد سر سید احمد خان نے 1875 میں محمدن اینگلو اورینٹل کالج کے طور پر رکھی۔

                            بعد میں یہ ادارہ 1920 میں یونیورسٹی کے درجے پر فائز ہوا، اور مسلمانوں کی تعلیمی ترقی میں اہم کردار ادا کیا۔

                            335

                            Which country won second ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2009?

                            Correct Answer: Pakistan
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            Know about ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2009

                            Edition: Second

                            Hosted by: England

                            Held: 5 June – 21 June 2009

                            Winner: Pakistan

                            Pakistan won: First time

                            Runner-up: Sri Lanka

                            Most wickets taken by: Umar Gul (13 wickets)

                            Pakistani captain: Younas Khan

                             

                            336

                            Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah died in ____?

                            Correct Answer: Karachi
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            On 11 September 1948. Quaid-e-Azam died

                            ***

                            Mohammed Ali Jinnah died on September 11, 1948, in Karachi, Pakistan.

                            Mohammed Ali Jinnah was the founder and first governor-general (1947–48) of Pakistan

                            Mohammad Ali Jinnah Born: December 25, 1876, Karachi

                            Spouse: Rattanbai Jinnah (m. 1918–1929), Emibai Jinnah (m. 1892–1893)

                            Siblings: Fatima Jinnah, Shireen Jinnah, Maryam Bai Jinnah, Ahmad Ali Jinnah, Bunde Ali Jinnah, Rahmat Ali Jinnah

                            Children: Dina Wadia

                            Parents: Poonja Jinnah, Mithibai Jinnah

                            Quaid-e-Azam Residency also known as Ziarat Residency

                            Quaid-e-Azam Residency is located in Ziarat, Balochistan, Pakistan.

                            337

                            Gorakh Hill is located in _____?

                            Correct Answer: District Dadu
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            Gorakh Hill is a popular tourist hill station located in District Dadu, Sindh.

                            It lies in the Kirthar Mountain Range.

                            It is known as the "Murree of Sindh."

                            Elevation of kirthar mountain 2,260 m (7,410 ft) 

                            The highest peak of the Kirthar mountains is Zardak Peak at 7,430 ft (2,260 m).

                            Kirthar Range, located along the Balochistan and Sindh provincial border.

                            The Hill Station of Sindh at Gorakh, in Kirthar Mountains Range, off Dadu.

                            338

                            A famous poet of Urdu Mir Taqi Mir was born in 1723 in city _____?

                            Correct Answer: Agra
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            Mir Taqi Mir, a great classical Urdu poet, was born in 1723 in Agra.

                            He is known as “Khuda-e-Sukhan” (God of poetry) in Urdu literature.

                            He was passed away in Lucknow on September 20, 1810.

                            He died due to a purgative overdose. 

                            339

                            Mirza Dabeer and Mir Anees are famous poets of ______?

                            Correct Answer: Marsiyah
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            Mirza Dabeer and Mir Anees are renowned for writing Marsiyah — elegiac poetry mourning the tragedy of Karbala.

                            They elevated Marsiyah to a high literary form in Urdu literature.

                            340

                            The world's youngest Microsoft certified professional at _____?

                            Correct Answer: Pakistani
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            Arfa Abdul Karim Randhawa achieved the MCP title in 2004 at age 9, becoming the youngest ever at that time.

                            She held this record until 2008.

                            She represented Pakistan at global forums, even visiting Microsoft HQ in the U.S.

                            341

                            Fort Munro is located in which province?

                            Correct Answer: Punjab
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            Fort Munro, formerly known as Loilaresir, is a hill station which is located at a height of 6,470 feet.

                            It is located in Sulaiman Range.

                            It is located in Dera Ghazi Khan District, Punjab.

                            342

                            Name the neighboring countries of Pakistan?

                            Correct Answer: India, China, Afghanistan and Iran
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            Neighboring Countries of Pakistan (with direction):

                            1. India – East
                            2. China – Northeast
                            3. Afghanistan – Northwest
                            4. Iran – West
                            343

                            The first session of constituent Assembly of Pakistan held at ______ on 10th August 1947.

                            Correct Answer: Karachi
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation
                            • The first session of the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan was held on 10th August 1947.
                              • It was held at the Sindh Assembly Building Karachi.
                              • The period of the First Constituent Assembly of Pakistan was from 10 August 1947 to 24 October 1954.
                              344

                              The 3rd June Plan was worked out/announced by _____?

                              Correct Answer: Lord Mountbatten
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The 3rd June Plan announced the partition of British India into 2 states:

                              • India
                              • Pakistan


                              Punjab and Bengal to be divided according to 3rd June Plan

                              This Plan also known as the Mountbatten Plan or Partition Plan 

                              It was the last plan for independence.

                               Lord Mountbatten assassinated: August 1979



                              345

                              Who was the Prime Minister of Pakistan when General Musharraf overthrew the government?

                              Correct Answer: Nawaz Sharif
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              General Pervez Musharraf overthrew the government on 12 October 1999 through a military coup.

                              At that time, Nawaz Sharif was serving as the Prime Minister of Pakistan.

                              346

                              After the secession of East Pakistan, who became the head of government in Pakistan?

                              Correct Answer: Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              After the secession of East Pakistan in December 1971, General Yahya Khan resigned, and Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto became the President and later Prime Minister.

                              He assumed power on 20 December 1971 and began rebuilding Pakistan politically and economically.

                              347

                              Who led the group of Deoband scholars that supported Quaid-e-Azam?

                              Correct Answer: Shabir Ahmad Usmani
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Shabir Ahmad Usmani led the group of Deoband scholars who broke away and supported Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah and the Pakistan Movement.

                              He also played a key role in framing Pakistan’s first Objectives Resolution.

                              He was a respected Islamic scholar.

                              348

                              Which superpowers interfered in East Pakistan during its internal political crisis?

                              Correct Answer: Soviet Union and America
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              During the 1971 East Pakistan crisis, the Soviet Union supported India, while the United States supported West Pakistan (now Pakistan).

                              Their involvement reflected Cold War rivalries, making it an internationalized conflict.

                              349

                              What was the name of the group that supported Ayub Khan?

                              Correct Answer: Convention Muslim League
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Convention Muslim League was formed in 1962 by supporters of President Ayub Khan.

                              It backed Ayub's Basic Democracies system and became the government party during his rule.

                              350

                              What script was used in Urdu that Hindus disliked?

                              Correct Answer: Persian-Arabic
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Urdu is written in the Persian-Arabic script, which was disliked by Hindus, who favored Devanagari (Hindi) script.

                              This script-based difference became a major cultural and political divide during British India.

                              351

                              When are federal and provincial governments formed in Pakistan?

                              Correct Answer: After general elections
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              In Pakistan, federal and provincial governments are formed after general elections, held every 5 years.

                              These elections determine the members of the National Assembly and Provincial Assemblies, who then elect the Prime Minister and Chief Ministers.

                              352

                              Which of the following is NOT a part of the structure of the government?

                              Correct Answer: Military
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Pakistan has three branches of government:

                              1. The Legislative Branch makes the laws.
                              2. The Executive Branch carries out the laws.
                              3. The Judiciary Branch evaluates and interprets the laws.

                              The military is a part of the state apparatus but not a formal branch of government.

                              The Legislative Branch, or Parliament, has two houses: the National Assembly and the Senate

                              1. National Assembly: It makes laws for the federation and keeps a check over the Executive. 

                              2. The Senate is the body that represents the provinces and promotes a feeling of equality, peace, and harmony.

                              353

                              How many essential elements does a state have?

                              Correct Answer: Four
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              A state has four essential elements:

                              1. Population
                              2. Territory
                              3. Government
                              4. Sovereignty

                              These elements are necessary for a political entity to be recognized as a state under international law.

                              354

                              What position did Zulifqar Ali Bhutto hold from 1971 to 1973?

                              Correct Answer: President of Pakistan
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Zulfikar Ali Bhutto returned to Pakistan on 18 December 1971.

                              On 20 December, he took over as President and Chief Martial Law Administrator from Yahya Khan.

                              Bhutto served as President from December 1971 to August 1973.

                              Bhutto served as Prime Minister from August 1973 to July 1977.

                              355

                              What did the military ceasefire line in Kashmir come to be known as _____?

                              Correct Answer: Line of Control
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The military ceasefire line in Kashmir, established after the 1949 UN-mediated ceasefire, is known as the Line of Control (LoC).

                              It separates Indian-administered and Pakistani-administered parts of Jammu and Kashmir.

                              356

                              Which power project is an example of Japanese economic cooperation with Pakistan?

                              Correct Answer: Ghazi Brotha Power Project
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The Ghazi Brotha Power Project received economic and technical assistance from Japan, showcasing bilateral cooperation.

                              Ghazi-Barotha Hydropower Project is a 1,450 MW un-of-the-river hydropower, connected to the Indus River.

                              Ghazi-Barotha  Location Between Attock, Punjab and Haripur, KP

                              Construction of the project began in 1995. 

                              Opening date: 2002

                              The Official name of Ghazi-Barotha Hydropower Project is غازى بروتھا

                              357

                              When did the Simla Deputation took place?

                              Correct Answer: 1st October 1906
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • Sir Agha Khan led the Simla Deputation.
                                • Indian Muslim leaders organized it.
                                • The delegation met the Governor-General and Viceroy, Lord Minto, in Simla on 1 October 1906.

                                شملہ وفد 1906

                                1. شملہ وفد یکم اکتوبر 1906 کو وائسرائے ہند سے ملا.

                                2. شملہ وفد کا صدر سر آغا خان تھا.

                                3. اس میں 35 لوگ / ارکان شامل تھے.

                                4. سب سے پہلے شملہ وفد ایم اے علی گڑھ کالج کے پرنسپل سے ملا.

                                5. پر. نسپل نے سیکرٹری وائسرائے کرنل ڈونلپ سے یہ میٹنگ طے کر دیا، ان ہی کےذریعے وفد وائسرائے لارڈ منٹو سے ملا.

                                6. چونکہ مسلمانوں کا یہ وفد وائسرائے ہند سے شملہ کے مقام پر ملا، اس وجہ سے اسکا نام شملہ وفد بن گیا.

                                358

                                The Indian National Congress was a ______?

                                Correct Answer: Political party
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Indian National Congress was founded on December 28, 1885, at Gokuldas Tejpal Sanskrit College in Bombay as a political party.

                                Its headquarters are in New Delhi.

                                Allan Octavian Hume was appointed as General Secretary.

                                Womesh Chunder Bonnerjee was elected the first President.

                                359

                                Who adopted the name "Pakistan Resolution" after it was used by the Hindu Press?

                                Correct Answer: Quaid-e-Azam and the Muslim League
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Hindu press first mockingly used the term "Pakistan Resolution" after the 1940 Lahore Resolution.

                                Quaid-e-Azam and the Muslim League later officially adopted the name, giving it political identity and significance.

                                360

                                In 1971 the 7th American Marine Craft came to save _______?

                                Correct Answer: East Pakistan
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                In 1971, during the Indo-Pak war, the 7th American Fleet (Marine Craft) moved toward East Pakistan (now Bangladesh).

                                Its purpose was to show support for Pakistan, but it did not engage militarily in the conflict.

                                361

                                Where was an art gallery built for Gulgee on public demand?

                                Correct Answer: Clifton, Karachi
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                An art gallery was built in Clifton, Karachi in honor of the renowned Pakistani artist Ismail Gulgee.

                                It was constructed due to public admiration and demand to preserve and showcase his artistic contributions.

                                362

                                In which city did Anna Molka Ahmad settle after moving to the subcontinent?

                                Correct Answer: Lahore
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                Anna Molka Ahmad, a pioneer of fine arts in Pakistan, settled in Lahore after moving to the subcontinent.
                                She played a vital role in establishing the Department of Fine Arts at the University of the Punjab, Lahore.
                                363

                                What was the status of the Mughal Empire after the death of Aurangzeb Alamgir?

                                Correct Answer: Declining
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                During Aurangzeb's reign (1658–1707), the Mughal Empire began its decline due to:
                                • Military Overextension: Constant wars (e.g., Deccan campaigns) drained resources.
                                • Religious Policies: Aurangzeb’s reversal of Akbar’s tolerance alienated Hindus and Sikhs, sparking rebellions (e.g., Sikh uprisings, Rajput conflicts).
                                • Economic Strain: Heavy taxation and famine weakened the agrarian economy.
                                • Administrative Breakdown: Corruption grew, and regional governors (like the Nizam of Hyderabad) gained autonomy.
                                364

                                Who became the President of Pakistan after General Yahya Khan in 1971?

                                Correct Answer: Zulfikar Ali Bhutto
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Zulfikar Ali Bhutto returned to Pakistan on 18 December 1971.

                                On 20 December, he took over as President and Chief Martial Law Administrator from Yahya Khan.

                                Bhutto served as President from December 1971 to August 1973.

                                Bhutto served as Prime Minister from August 1973 to July 1977.

                                365

                                Which Viceroy gave a positive response to the Simla Deputation?

                                Correct Answer: Lord Minto
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Simla Deputation (1906) was led by Aga Khan.

                                He presented Muslim demands to the British, especially for separate electorates.

                                Lord Minto, then Viceroy of India, welcomed the deputation positively.

                                It was later led to the Minto-Morley Reforms of 1909 granting Muslims separate electorates.

                                366

                                The British considered which community primarily responsible for the 1857 War of Independence?

                                Correct Answer: Muslims
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                After the 1857 War of Independence, the British held Muslims primarily responsible due to their leadership role and historical resentment from the fall of the Mughal Empire.

                                As a result, Muslims faced harsher repression, including loss of property, jobs, and educational opportunities during British rule.

                                367

                                When was Maulana Mufti Mahmood elected as Chief Minister of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa?

                                Correct Answer: 1972
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Maulana Mufti Mahmood was elected as the Chief Minister of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa on March 1, 1972 during the Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto government.

                                He held this position until February 15, 1973.

                                368

                                Who was the ruler of Bengal before the Battle of Buxar?

                                Correct Answer: Mir Qasim
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Mir Qasim was the Nawab of Bengal before the Battle of Buxar in 1764.

                                He had replaced Mir Jafar with British support but later clashed with them.

                                He allied with Shuja-ud-Daula (Oudh) and Shah Alam II (Mughal Emperor) against the British East India Company.

                                The English Army in the Battle of Buxar was led by Hector Munro.

                                369

                                Which hospital in Islamabad was developed with Japanese support?

                                Correct Answer: Pakistan Institute of Medical Sciences
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Pakistan Institute of Medical Sciences (PIMS) was established with significant financial and technical assistance from Japan, making it Islamabad’s flagship public medical facility.

                                This international collaboration included contributions from the Government of Japan for infrastructure, equipment, and training initiatives.

                                370

                                Tashkent Declaration with Pakistan was signed by Indian Prime Minister?

                                Correct Answer: Lal Buhadur Shastari
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Tashkent Declaration was signed president of Pakistan Ayub Khan and by India’s Prime Minister Lal Bahadur Shastri 

                                It was signed between India and Pakistan on 10 January 1966 to resolve the Indo-Pakistani War of 1965

                                The Tashkent Declaration was a peace agreement between India and Pakistan. 

                                Through the mediation of the Soviet Union Tashkent Agreement held between India and Pakistan 

                                371

                                The Cripps proposals were rejected by _______?

                                Correct Answer: Both Congress and Muslim league
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Cripps Mission (1942) offered dominion status after WWII, but was rejected by both the Indian National Congress and the Muslim League.

                                Gandhi called it a "post-dated cheque," and Jinnah also opposed it as it did not recognize the demand for Pakistan.

                                372

                                How long was the interim period before the enforcement of the 1962 Constitution?

                                Correct Answer: Three years and four months
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The interim period before the enforcement of Pakistan's 1962 Constitution,

                                They followed the abrogation of the 1956 Constitution and the imposition of martial law in 1958

                                Lasted approximately 3 years and 4 months, from October 7, 1958, to March 1, 1962,

                                Finally enforced on June 8, 1962. which martial law was in place under Ayub Khan.

                                373

                                How many medals did Pakistan win in Asian Athletics Championships 2025?

                                Correct Answer: One Medal
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Olympic champion Arshad Nadeem threw a massive 86.40m to win gold in the men’s javelin final at the Asian Athletics Championship

                                Winning date: 31 May 2025

                                Venue: Gumi, South Korea

                                Nadeem’s gold is Pakistan’s first and only medal at the championship.

                                 

                                Additional Information:

                                The 2025 Asian Athletics Championships was the 26th edition of the Asian Athletics Championships.

                                Being held from 27 to 31 May 2025 in Gumi, South Korea.

                                 ****

                                374

                                Which medal did Pakistan athlete Arshad Nadeem win in Asian Athletics Championship 2025 held in South Korea?

                                Correct Answer: Gold Medal
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Olympic champion Arshad Nadeem threw a massive 86.40m to win gold in the men’s javelin final at the Asian Athletics Championship

                                Winning date: 31 May 2025

                                Venue: Gumi, South Korea

                                Nadeem’s gold is Pakistan’s first and only medal at the championship.

                                 

                                Additional Information:

                                The 2025 Asian Athletics Championships was the 26th edition of the Asian Athletics Championships.

                                Being held from 27 to 31 May 2025 in Gumi, South Korea.

                                 ****

                                375

                                Who is the Current Director-General (DG) of Inter-Services Intelligence (ISI)?

                                Correct Answer: Lt. Gen. Muhammad Asim Malik
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                ð Lieutenant General Muhammad Asim Malik is the current and 26th Director-General (DG) of Inter-Services Intelligence (ISI) since 30 Sep 2024.
                                ð He replaced  Lt. Gen. Nadeem Anjum.
                                ð DG ISI tenure term: 3 years

                                Last update on 01.09.2023
                                        Updated & verified on 30-09-2024
                                        Click here for reference 
                                376

                                Which of the following is true about the Faraizi Tehreek?

                                Correct Answer: It was a political movement for independence
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Faraizi Movement (founded by Haji Shariatullah) emphasized reviving Islamic obligations (Faraiz).

                                It was not focused on modernization but on purifying Muslim society from un-Islamic practices.

                                377

                                What type of system of government does Pakistan have?

                                Correct Answer: Federal parliamentary system
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Pakistan has a federal parliamentary system, where powers are shared between the federal government and provinces.

                                The Prime Minister is the head of government, elected by the National Assembly.

                                378

                                The Pakistani athlete who became the first-ever individual gold medalist in the 2024 Olympic Games for Pakistan is ______?

                                Correct Answer: Arshad Nadeem
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Arshad Nadeem set a new Olympic record by clinching the coveted gold medal in the men’s javelin final

                                This ended Pakistan’s 32-year wait for success at the Games 

                                Throw: 92.97-m throw

                                Winning date: 8 Aug 2024

                                Arshad belongs to Mian Channu.

                                 Nadeem’s throw was Pakistan’s first individual gold medal

                                Note: It also shattered the previous Olympic record of 90.57m, set by the Netherlands’ Andreas Thorkildsen at the Beijing 2008 Olympics.

                                The throw now stands as the sixth longest throw ever, and the best in the world this year.

                                 ****

                                379

                                The main cause of the 2022 floods in Pakistan was ______?

                                Correct Answer: Heavy monsoon rains
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The 2022 floods in Pakistan were primarily caused by exceptionally heavy monsoon rains, far above average.

                                These rains led to massive flooding across all provinces, affecting millions.

                                380

                                The Twenty-Sixth (26th) Amendment to Pakistan’s Constitution was assented by President _______?

                                Correct Answer: Asif Ali Zardari
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Signed by President Asif Ali Zardari on 21 October 2024.

                                About 26th amendment of the constitution of Pakistan.

                                Approved by Senate on 20 October 2024 (65 votes).

                                Approved by National Assembly on 21 October 2024 (225 votes).

                                381

                                The Prime Minister of Pakistan is _______?

                                Correct Answer: Elected by the National Assembly
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Zulifqar Ali Bhutto introduced the 1973 constitution in Pakistan on August 14, 1973.

                                According to the 1973 constitution, the Provincial Assembly, National assembly, and senate elect the President.

                                Whereas the Prime Minister is elected by the only National Assembly.

                                According to the constitution 1973, the minimum age for the President of Pakistan: are 45 Years

                                The President is elected indirectly for a term of five years.

                                382

                                The President of Pakistan is elected ______?

                                Correct Answer: By the members of the Parliament and provincial assemblies
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Zulifqar Ali Bhutto introduced the 1973 constitution in Pakistan on August 14, 1973.

                                According to the 1973 constitution, the Provincial Assembly, National assembly, and senate elect the President.

                                Whereas the Prime Minister is elected by the only National Assembly.

                                According to the constitution 1973, the minimum age for the President of Pakistan: are 45 Years

                                The President is elected indirectly for a term of five years.

                                383

                                The higher legal authority in Pakistan is _______?

                                Correct Answer: The Supreme Court
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Supreme Court is the highest judicial authority in Pakistan.

                                It has the power to interpret the Constitution and ensure laws follow constitutional principles.

                                Under the 1973 Constitution of Pakistan, the Prime Minister is the Head of Government.

                                The President serves as the Head of State with a ceremonial role.

                                384

                                The name of the tree plantation campaign started by Imran Khan in Pakistan was ______?

                                Correct Answer: Billion Tree Tsunami
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                On December 22, 2020, PM Imran Khan launched the "Billion Tree Honey Initiative" to boost honey production and tree plantation under the same program.

                                "10 Billion Tree Tsunami" was launched in 2018 as a five-year nationwide project to plant 10 billion trees.

                                It was an expansion of the earlier "Billion Tree Tsunami" campaign in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KP).

                                The campaign was also known as "Plant for Pakistan".

                                385

                                The province in Pakistan reported the highest number of polio cases in 2024 is _____?

                                Correct Answer: Baluchistan
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Balochistan reported the highest number of polio cases in Pakistan in 2024:

                                Balochistan: Reported the most cases with 27 infections

                                Sindh: Reported 23 cases

                                Khyber Pakhtunkhwa: Reported 22 cases

                                Islamabad: Reported one case

                                Punjab: Reported one case


                                386

                                On 27 January 2025, which of the following team beat Pakistan to win Test Match for the first time in 35 years?

                                Correct Answer: West Indies
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                On 27 January 2025, West Indies won a Test match in Pakistan for the first time in nearly 35 years.

                                The West Indies won the second Test in Multan by 120 runs on day three to draw the series 1-1.

                                The last time the West Indies won a Test in Pakistan was in Faisalabad in November 1990.

                                 ****

                                387

                                Under 1973 Constitution the president of Pakistan is elected by?

                                Correct Answer: All above mentioned
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Zulifqar Ali Bhutto introduced the 1973 constitution in Pakistan on August 14, 1973.

                                According to the 1973 constitution, the Provincial Assembly, National assembly, and senate elect the President.

                                Whereas the prime minister is elected by the only national assembly.

                                According to the constitution 1973, the minimum age for the President of Pakistan: are 45 Years

                                The president is elected indirectly for a term of five years.


                                388

                                After the separation of East Pakistan in December 1971, Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto came into power by being appointed as ______?

                                Correct Answer: Civilian Martial Law Administrator & President
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                In December 1971, after the fall of East Pakistan, Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto was appointed as both the President of Pakistan and the Civilian Chief Martial Law Administrator.

                                He assumed the presidency on December 20, 1971, following the resignation of Yahya Khan.

                                Later, he also served as the Prime Minister of Pakistan from 1973 to 1977 after the post was recreated by a new constitution.

                                389

                                The land reforms introduced by Ayub Khan fixed the size of irrigated land at _____?

                                Correct Answer: 500 acres
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Introduced by Ayub Khan’s government as the first major land reform in Pakistan.

                                Regulation 64 of 1959 limited land ownership to:

                                • 500 acres of irrigated land, or
                                • 1,000 acres of unirrigated land, or
                                • A maximum of 36,000 Produce Index Units (PIU) — whichever was greater.
                                390

                                Mahatma Gandhi is an important historical figure because ______?

                                Correct Answer: He led India's non-violent independence movement
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Mahatma Gandhi is renowned for leading the Indian independence movement using non-violent civil disobedience (Ahimsa).

                                He inspired millions of Indians to peacefully resist British rule through protests, marches, and boycotts.

                                It earned him the title "Father of the Nation" in India.


                                Additional Information:

                                1915: Gandhi returned to India from South Africa and began working for social reforms.

                                1919: Launched opposition to the Rowlatt Act, marking the start of mass movements.

                                1930: Led the Dandi March (Salt March) against British salt laws.

                                1942: Launched the Quit India Movement, demanding an end to British rule.

                                15 August 1947: India gained independence.

                                Gandhi was assassinated on 30 January 1948.

                                391

                                Pakistan faces severe floods in 2022 because _____?

                                Correct Answer: Due to excessive rainfall and poor infrastructure
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Primary Cause of 2022 Floods:

                                Record Monsoon Rains: Pakistan received 190% more rainfall than average (NDMA data), submerging 1/3 of the country.

                                Glacial Melt: Heatwaves accelerated melting, adding to water volume (UN report).

                                Poor Infrastructure: Weak dams, drainage systems, and urban planning worsened flooding.

                                392

                                The local government system is important for Pakistan ______?

                                Correct Answer: To decentralize power and promote grassroots development
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The local government system brings governance closer to the people, allowing for faster and more responsive service delivery.

                                It helps decentralize power from federal/provincial levels and encourages community participation in development.

                                393

                                The 1973 Constitution is significant for Pakistan because ______?

                                Correct Answer: It is the foundational legal document defining Pakistan’s governance
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The 1973 Constitution provides the framework for Pakistan’s political system, including the roles of the legislature, executive, and judiciary.

                                It declares Pakistan an Islamic republic.

                                It ensures fundamental rights, and establishes a parliamentary form of government.

                                394

                                The judiciary is important in Pakistan because ______?

                                Correct Answer: It interprets laws and ensures justice
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The judiciary in Pakistan plays a key role in interpreting the Constitution and laws of the country.

                                It ensures justice, protects fundamental rights, and maintains checks and balances on the executive and legislative branches.

                                395

                                As of February 2025, which country is the largest foreign investor in Pakistan’s infrastructure projects?

                                Correct Answer: China
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                As of February 2025, China contributed 40% of Pakistan’s total FDI in infrastructure during FY 2024–25.

                                China invested $790 million in sectors like power, energy, and transport, making it the top foreign investor.

                                396

                                In February 2025, which international financial institution conducted a Governance and Corruption Diagnostic Assessment in Pakistan?

                                Correct Answer: International Monetary Fund
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                In February 2025, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) conducted a Governance and Corruption Diagnostic Assessment in Pakistan.

                                The IMF dispatched a mission to Islamabad from February 6-14, 2025, to assess governance and corruption risks in Pakistan at the request of the Pakistani government.

                                The assessment was part of the Extended Fund Facility (EFF) 2024 program. 

                                397

                                A plateau is a flat elevated landform that rises sharply above the surrounding area on at least one side; in Pakistan, the number of Plateaus is/are ______?

                                Correct Answer: Two
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Pakistan has two main plateaus: the Pothohar Plateau in Punjab and the Balochistan Plateau.

                                Both are elevated landforms with distinct geographical and strategic importance.

                                398

                                The total requirement of oil produced by Pakistan is ______?

                                Correct Answer: 20%
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                Pakistan produces only about 20% of its total oil requirement.
                                The remaining 80% is met through imports, making the country heavily dependent on foreign oil.
                                399

                                When did General Pervez Musharraf assume control of the government by overthrowing Nawaz Sharif?

                                Correct Answer: October 12, 1999
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                In October 1999, General Pervez Musharraf led a military coup to overthrow Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif.

                                The military seized control of the government on October 12, 1999.

                                By October 14, 1999, Musharraf was acting as the Chief Executive of Pakistan.

                                400

                                Name the empire ruled by Genghis Khan?

                                Correct Answer: Mongol Empire
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Genghis Khan founded and led the Mongol Empire in the early 13th and 14th centuries.

                                It was the largest contiguous land empire in history.

                                Founded: 1206

                                Date dissolved: 1368

                                401

                                The function of Senate in Pakistan's political system is to _______?

                                Correct Answer: Make laws and represent provinces
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Senate is the upper house of Pakistan’s Parliament.

                                It ensures equal representation of all provinces and participates in law-making.

                                Pakistan has three branches of government:

                                1. The Legislative Branch makes the laws.
                                2. The Executive Branch carries out the laws.
                                3. The Judiciary Branch evaluates and interprets the laws.

                                The Legislative Branch, or Parliament, has two houses: the National Assembly and the Senate

                                1. National Assembly: It makes laws for the federation and keeps a check over the Executive. 

                                2. The Senate is the body that represents the provinces and promotes a feeling of equality, peace, and harmony.

                                402

                                Urdu is considered a national language of Pakistan because ______?

                                Correct Answer: It unites people from different linguistic backgrounds
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Urdu serves as a lingua franca, helping unite people across Pakistan's diverse regional languages.

                                It was chosen to promote national unity.

                                Urdu was declared Official Language of Pakistan under 251 article of the constitution 1973.

                                403

                                The Khyber Pass is important for Pakistan because ______?

                                Correct Answer: It is a strategic trade and invasion route
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Khyber Pass connects Pakistan with Afghanistan and Central Asia.

                                Historically used for trade and by invading armies, making it strategically vital.

                                Important Passes of Pakistan

                                1. Khyber Pass 

                                connects: Peshawar to Kabul

                                connects: Pakistan to Afghanistan

                                Height 1070 meters, 3510 feet 


                                2. Bolan Pass 

                                Connects: JCB and Sibi with Quetta 

                                Height: 1793 meters, 5884 feet 


                                3. Lowari Pass 

                                Connects: Chitral with Dir 

                                Height: 3118 meters, 10230 feet 


                                4. Broghil Pass 

                                Location: Pakistan & Afghanistan border 

                                Height: 3798 meters, 12460 feet 


                                5. Malakand Pass 

                                Connects: Dir with Chitral 

                                Height: 1362 meters, 4468 feet 


                                6. Shandur pass

                                Connects: Gupis/Gilgit with Chitral 

                                Height: 3700 meters, 12139 feet 


                                7. Khanjerab Pass (topmost in Pakistan) 

                                Other name: Friendship Gate

                                Connects: China with Pakistan 

                                Height: 4693 meters, 15397 feet 


                                8. Dorah pass

                                Connects: Badakhshan province of Afghanistan with Chitral 

                                Height: 4300 meters, 14108 feet 


                                9. Babusar Pass 

                                Connects: Mansehra/Abbottabad with Gilgit


                                10. Kohat Pass

                                Connects: Kohat with Peshawar 


                                11. Khojak Pass

                                Connects: Qilla Abdullah with Chaman


                                12. Tochi Pass

                                Connects: Ghazni with Bannu


                                13. Karakar Pass

                                Connects: Mingora with Buner 

                                404

                                Allama Iqbal is considered an important figure in Pakistan's history because _____?

                                Correct Answer: He was a poet and philosopher who inspired the Pakistan Movement
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Allama Iqbal envisioned a separate homeland for Muslims of the subcontinent.

                                His poetry and philosophy deeply influenced the ideology behind Pakistan’s creation.

                                Additional Information:

                                • Delivered the Allahabad Address (1930) proposing a separate Muslim state.
                                • Regarded as the "Spiritual Father of Pakistan."
                                • His ideas laid the foundation for the Two-Nation Theory.
                                • National poet of Pakistan; his birth (Nov 9) is celebrated as Iqbal Day.
                                405

                                Pakistan is a major producer of which agricultural product?

                                Correct Answer: Wheat
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Pakistan's largest food crop is wheat.

                                As of 2018, According to the Ministry of Agriculture, Pakistan's wheat output reached 26.3 million tonnes.

                                The most important crops which together account for nearly more than 75% of the value of total crop output are:

                                • Wheat
                                • Sugarcane
                                • Cotton
                                • Rice
                                406

                                The national flag of Pakistan features a crescent moon and star coloured with ______?

                                Correct Answer: White
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The crescent moon and star on Pakistan’s flag are white.

                                They are placed on a green background symbolizing progress and peace.


                                Additional Information:

                                White color represents – peace and honesty & symbolizing the role of religious minorities

                                Green color represents – hope, joy, and love, and in many cultures has a sacred significance as in Pakistan where the color green is associated with Islam.

                                The crescent in the Pakistani Flag represents progress

                                Pakistan flag's green and white color explains the Muslim majority in Pakistan and various religious minorities.

                                The Dark green field in the flag of Pakistan represents Muslim Majority

                                The star in the Pakistani flag represents light and knowledge

                                The white strip in the flag of Pakistan represents Minority

                                407

                                The branch that makes the laws is _______?

                                Correct Answer: Legislative Branch
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Pakistan has three branches of government:

                                1. The Legislative Branch makes the laws.
                                2. The Executive Branch carries out the laws.
                                3. The Judiciary Branch evaluates and interprets the laws.

                                The Legislative Branch, or Parliament, has two houses: the National Assembly and the Senate

                                1. National Assembly: It makes laws for the federation and keeps a check over the Executive. 

                                2. The Senate is the body that represents the provinces and promotes a feeling of equality, peace, and harmony.

                                408

                                The role of Executive in Pakistan is ______?

                                Correct Answer: To enforce laws
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Pakistan has three branches of government:

                                1. The Legislative Branch makes the laws.
                                2. The Executive Branch carries out the laws.
                                3. The Judiciary Branch evaluates and interprets the laws.

                                The Legislative Branch, or Parliament, has two houses: the National Assembly and the Senate

                                1. National Assembly: It makes laws for the federation and keeps a check over the Executive. 

                                2. The Senate is the body that represents the provinces and promotes a feeling of equality, peace, and harmony.

                                409

                                The branch which evaluates and interprets law is _______?

                                Correct Answer: Judiciary Branch
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Pakistan has three branches of government:

                                1. The Legislative Branch makes the laws.
                                2. The Executive Branch carries out the laws.
                                3. The Judiciary Branch evaluates and interprets the laws.

                                The Legislative Branch, or Parliament, has two houses: the National Assembly and the Senate

                                1. National Assembly: It makes laws for the federation and keeps a check over the Executive. 

                                2. The Senate is the body that represents the provinces and promotes a feeling of equality, peace, and harmony.

                                410

                                Who was the first Hindu woman judge in Pakistan?

                                Correct Answer: Suman Kumari Bodani
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Suman Kumari Bodani, Pakistan's first Hindu woman judge in 2019.

                                She lives in Qambar Shahdadkot District. 

                                She is currently serving in her native district, Qambar Shahdadkot

                                411

                                Ghulam Ishaq Khan served as Chairman of the Senate of Pakistan during which years?

                                Correct Answer: 1985–1988
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Ghulam Ishaq Khan's held offices:

                                • Chairman WAPDA: 1961-1966
                                • Governor SBP: 22nd December 1971 – 30th November 1975
                                • Chairman Senate: 21st March 1985 – 12th December 1988
                                • President of Pakistan: 17th August 1988 – 18th July 1993
                                412

                                Faisal Masjid was constructed in 1986. When was the first prayer held there?

                                Correct Answer: 1988
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                On 18th June 1988, the first prayer  was held in the Faisal mosque

                                Construction of the mosque began in 1976 

                                The mosque was completed in 1986

                                It was opened to the public in 1986 OR 1987

                                **

                                The Faisal Mosque is the largest in Pakistan,

                                Located in the  Islamabad foothills of Margalla Hills.

                                It is the sixth-largest mosque in the world

                                The unconventional design by Turkish architect Vedat Dalokay

                                It is named after the late King Faisal of Saudi Arabia (r. 1964–75)

                                413

                                In which year was the All India Muslim Students Federation established?

                                Correct Answer: 1937
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The All-India Muslim Students Federation (AIMSF) was founded in Calcutta, India on September 1, 1937.

                                Headquarters located in Chennai.

                                Mother party is the Indian Union Muslim League.

                                The AIMSF was a student union that was affiliated with the All-India Muslim League.

                                It was organized by Fatima Jinnah under the patronage of Muhammad Ali Jinnah in 1941.

                                414

                                Tehqeeqi Majalla Bunyad is published by which university?

                                Correct Answer: National University of Modern Languages (NUML)
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Tehqeeqi Majalla “Bunyād” is the scholarly research journal of the National University of Modern Languages (NUML).

                                It is published by NUML’s dedicated research and publication division

                                Reflecting the university’s commitment to advancing academic inquiry and linguistic studies.





                                415

                                Pakistan has no diplomatic relations with _____?

                                Correct Answer: Israel
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Pakistan has no diplomatic relations with Israel.

                                Israel and Pakistan have had a history of tensions, and Pakistan has been a long-standing supporter of the Palestinian cause.

                                Currently, 31 countries do not recognize and/or have foreign relations with Israel.

                                416

                                When President Mamnoon Hussain signed the FATA merger bill?

                                Correct Answer: 31 May 2018
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                President Mamnoon Hussain signed the FATA merger bill on May 31, 2018.

                                The bill was passed by the National Assembly on May 24, 2018, and by the Senate on May 25, 2018.


                                FATA will have 21 seats within the KP Assembly:

                                • 16 general
                                • 4 reserved for women
                                • 1 reserved for non-Muslims

                                The incumbent senators for FATA will be allowed to complete their six-year terms.

                                The Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Assembly will have 145 seats

                                • 115 general
                                • 26 reserved for women
                                • 4 reserved for minorities

                                FATA was merged with KPK in May 2018 under the 25th Amendment. 

                                417

                                The State Bank Museum & Art Gallery established in year?

                                Correct Answer: 2004
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The State Bank Museum & Art Gallery was established in 2004

                                It is a museum on Ibrahim Ismail Chundrigar Road in Karachi

                                In 2004, the State Bank of Pakistan decided to adapt the building as a museum

                                Work on the projects started in 2006 

                                History

                                The first Monetary Museum of Pakistan.

                                Previously, it was the Imperial Bank of India

                                That was a Greco-Roman building in Jodhpuri Red Sandstone, constructed in the 1920s by the British government.


                                418

                                The average height of Sub-Himalayas?

                                Correct Answer: 600 – 1200 meters
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Their average height varies between 600 and 1200 meters,

                                Sub-Himalayan range of the northern Indian subcontinent.

                                Sub-Himalayas are also known as Siwalik or Outer Himalayas.

                                 The range spans the modern-day countries of :

                                1. Pakistan,
                                2. India,
                                3. Nepal,
                                4. and Bhutan.
                                419

                                The Second Constituent Assembly of Pakistan was formed in?

                                Correct Answer: 1955
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The second Constituent Assembly of Pakistan was created on 28th May 1955 under Governor General's Order No. 12 of 1955.

                                The strength of this Assembly was 80 Members, half each from East Pakistan and West Pakistan.

                                First assembly consisted of 69 members while second assembly consisted of 80 members.
