                                On 26th July 1947 in which the first Constituent Assembly of Pakistan was given shape with 69 Members (later increased to 79), including one female Member.

                                420

                                Who was the founder of Arya Samaj?

                                Correct Answer: Pandit Dayanand
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Dayanand Saraswati was an Indian philosopher, social leader and founder of the Arya Samaj.

                                Arya Samaj founded in 1875

                                421

                                Dharmanrajaikastupa in taxila was built in the era of _____?

                                Correct Answer: Kushans
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Dharmarajika Stupa, also referred to as the Great Stupa of Taxila.

                                It is a Buddhist stupa near Taxila, Pakistan.

                                It dates from the 2nd century CE.

                                It was built by the Kushans to house small bone fragments of the Buddha.

                                422

                                Which bank controls all the functions of the banking system in Pakistan?

                                Correct Answer: State Bank
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The State Bank of Pakistan is the central regulatory authority for the country’s banking sector.

                                It manages monetary policy and oversees all banking functions.

                                423

                                Haripur was named after which Sikh ruler?

                                Correct Answer: Hari Singh Nalwa
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Haripur was founded and named after the Sikh general Hari Singh Nalwa.

                                He established it as a fort city in 1822.

                                424

                                Who recalled Muhammad bin Qasim from Sindh?

                                Correct Answer: Caliph Sulaiman
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Muhammad bin Qasim was recalled from Sindh by Caliph Sulayman.

                                This happened after the death of Caliph al-Walid I in 715 CE.

                                Caliph Sulayman had a different political agenda.

                                He ordered Muhammad bin Qasim’s arrest and execution.

                                425

                                What percent share of industry enterprises did Pakistan have at the time of partition?

                                Correct Answer: 3.7%
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                At the time of the partition of British India, Pakistan inherited approximately 3.7% of the industrial enterprises.

                                Specifically, Pakistan received 34 out of the 921 industrial units present in the subcontinent. 

                                426

                                Which Indian leader first took the Kashmir issue to the UN Security Council?

                                Correct Answer: Jawaharlal Nehru
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Jawaharlal Nehru, India's first Prime Minister, was the Indian leader who initially brought the Kashmir issue to the UN Security Council.

                                He formally lodged a complaint against Pakistan with the UN on January 1, 1948, following the outbreak of the Indo-Pakistani War of 1947. 

                                427

                                Which percentage correctly represents Pakistan’s arms imports from China?

                                Correct Answer: 81%
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                According to data from SIPRI, 81% of Pakistan’s weapon imports in 2020–24 came from China.

                                This marks an increase from the previous period (2015–19), when 74% of Pakistan’s arms were sourced from China.

                                428

                                Pakistan and China are coupled by land in the north of Pakistan due to an agreement in the year 1963, which was named?

                                Correct Answer: Sino-Pak Agreement
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Negotiations between the nations officially began on October 13, 1962, and resulted in an agreement being signed on 2 March 1963.

                                It was signed by foreign ministers Chen Yi for the Chinese and Zulfikar Ali Bhutto for the Pakistani.

                                The eastern tip of the Wakhan Corridor starts the Sino-Pak border between the People's Republic of China and Pakistan spanning about 559 km (347.3 mi).

                                Zulfikar Ali Bhutto, the Minister of External Affairs of Pakistan.

                                429

                                On July 2, 1972, the Simla Agreement ceasefire line as a Line of Control was signed by ______?

                                Correct Answer: Zulifqar Ali Bhutto
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Simla Agreement was signed on July 2, 1972 between Zulifqar Ali Bhutto (Pakistan) and Indira Gandhi (India).

                                It converted the ceasefire line in Kashmir into the Line of Control (LoC).

                                430

                                The turquoise waters of LOTUS LAKE are a sight to behold, very calm and peaceful and it is located in ______?

                                Correct Answer: Islamabad
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Lotus Lake is located in Islamabad, Pakistan.

                                It is known for its serene environment and blooming lotus flowers during the season.

                                It attracting visitors for its natural beauty.

                                431

                                Pakistan's location plays an essential role in international trade because _____?

                                Correct Answer: It connects South Asia to the Middle East and Central Asia
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Pakistan’s strategic location links key regions — South Asia, Central Asia, and the Middle East.

                                This makes it a vital route for international trade, energy transit, and regional connectivity.

                                432

                                The Constitution of 1973 defines Pakistan is ______?

                                Correct Answer: As an Islamic Republic
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The 1973 Constitution of Pakistan declares the country as the Islamic Republic of Pakistan.

                                It ensures that laws align with Islam while functioning under a democratic parliamentary system.

                                433

                                Pakistan has federal government system _____?

                                Correct Answer: To allow provinces some autonomy
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                Pakistan follows a federal system to ensure that provinces have constitutional powers and autonomy.
                                This system helps manage regional diversity and distribute power between the center and provinces.
                                434

                                The Noble Peace Prize in 2014 was won by a Pakistani ______?

                                Correct Answer: Malala Yousafzai
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                In October 2014, Malala, along with Indian children's rights activist Kailash Satyarthi .

                                At age 17, she became the youngest person to receive this prize.

                                About Malala Yousafzai

                                Born: 1997

                                Belongs: Mingora, Swat

                                Received Noble Prize in 2014

                                She is the world's youngest Nobel Prize laureate.

                                International Malala Day is observed every year on July 12.

                                435

                                Which Pakistani Cricket player has breaks Kohli’s record and becomes fastest player to reach 1,000 runs in T20 International?

                                Correct Answer: Babr Azam
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The fastest Pakistani player to score 1000 T20 runs is Babar Azam.

                                He reached the milestone in 26 innings. He is also the fastest to 1000 runs in T20 Internationals. 

                                Born: October 15, 1994

                                436

                                Pakistan's city, which was declared a UNESCO World Heritage site in 2024, is _____?

                                Correct Answer: Lahore
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                In 2024, Lahore was declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site for its rich cultural and historical significance.
                                Including landmarks like the Lahore Fort, Shalimar Gardens, and the Walled City.
                                437

                                The youngest Nobel Prize laureate, Malala Yousafzai won Nobel Peace Prize for the year?

                                Correct Answer: 2014
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                In October 2014, Malala, along with Indian children's rights activist Kailash Satyarthi 

                                At age 17, she became the youngest person to receive this prize.



                                About Malala Yousafzai

                                Born: 1997

                                Belongs: Mingora, Swat

                                Received Noble Prize in 2014

                                She is the world's youngest Nobel Prize laureate

                                International Malala Day is observed every year on July 12 


                                ND-21-5-2023


                                438

                                Quaid-e-Azam Jinnah The story of a Nation Is a biography of Quaid-e-Azam written-by?

                                Correct Answer: G Allana
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The biography "Quaid-e-Azam Jinnah: The Story of a Nation" was written by G. Allana. 

                                Publication date: 1967

                                439

                                Which of the following district of occupied Jammu and Kashmir has Buddhist majority population?

                                Correct Answer: Leh
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Leh is indeed a district within the Ladakh region,

                                It is now a Union Territory of India.

                                Ladakh was previously part of the state of Jammu and Kashmir

                                It was separated and made a Union Territory in 2019.

                                Leh is one of the two districts in Ladakh, with the other being Kargil. 

                                The district headquarters are in Leh. 

                                Established 1 July 1979


                                440

                                The recipient of Nisha e Haider captain Kamal Sher Khan belonged to which Regiment?

                                Correct Answer: Sindh Regiment
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Captain Karnal Sher Khan, recipient of the Nishan-e-Haider, belonged to the 27th Sindh Regiment,

                                He was posted to the 12th Northern Light Infantry (NLI) Regiment during the Kargil conflict. 

                                He was born in Naway Kiley (now Karnal Sher Khan Kallay) village, Swabi district, Khyber-Pakhtunkhwa. 

                                He was martyred on 5 July 1999 during Kargil War between India & Pakistan.


                                441

                                Where the Inspectorate Headquarter Jails of Punjab is situated?

                                Correct Answer: Lahore
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Inspectorate Headquarters Jails of Punjab is located in Lahore

                                The Punjab Prisons Department was established in 1854 

                                9 jails in Punjab were constructed during the 19th century. 

                                Both District Jail Jhelum and Central Jail, Gujranwala are listed oldest jail in Pakistan

                                Lahore has two main jails:

                                • Central Jail (Kot Lakhpat) 
                                • District Jail (also known as Camp Jail) in Shadman.

                                At the time of the partition of Punjab, there were nineteen jails in the province. 

                                21 jails were constructed in Punjab province after partition.

                                Number

                                Central Jails 9

                                District Jails 25

                                Sub Jails 2

                                Women Jails 1

                                Borstal Institutions & Juvenile Jails 2

                                High Security Prison 1

                                Total Jails in Punjab: 43

                                Jails Constructed After Partition :21


                                442

                                In Punjab, how many jails are located which were constructed during 19th century?

                                Correct Answer: 9
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                9 jails in Punjab were constructed during the 19th century. 

                                The following jails in Punjab were built during the 19th century: 

                                • Central Jail Gujranwala (1854)
                                • District Jail Jhelum (1854)
                                • District Jail Rajanpur (1860)
                                • District Jail Sialkot (1865)
                                • District Jail Shahpur (1873)
                                • District Jail Faisalabad (1873)
                                • District Jail Multan (1872)
                                • Borstal Institution & Juvenile Jail Bahawalpur (1882)
                                • District Jail Dadu (1874): (though now in Sindh, it was formerly part of Punjab)
                                443

                                How many jails are there in Lahore?

                                Correct Answer: 2
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                 Lahore has two main jails:

                                • Central Jail (Kot Lakhpat) 
                                • District Jail (also known as Camp Jail) in Shadman.

                                There are also plans to build a new district jail in the Sua e Asal area of Lahore. 


                                444

                                When Central Jail Gujranwala was constructed?

                                Correct Answer: 1854
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Gujranwala are listed as being established in 1854

                                Central Jail Gujranwala were burned to ashes during a riot in 1992

                                Opened 1883

                                445

                                After partition how many jails were constructed in Pakistan?

                                Correct Answer: 21
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                At the time of the partition of Punjab, there were nineteen jails in the province. 

                                21 jails were constructed in Punjab province after partition.

                                Number

                                Central Jails 9

                                District Jails 25

                                Sub Jails 2

                                Women Jails 1

                                Borstal Institutions & Juvenile Jails 2

                                High Security Prison 1

                                Total Jails in Punjab: 43

                                Jails Constructed After Partition :21


                                446

                                General Pervez Musharraf was elected as President through referendum on?

                                Correct Answer: 20 June 2001
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Know everything about Musharraf

                                Born: Delhi (August 11, 1943)

                                Graduated: Pakistan Military Academy at Kakul (1964)

                                Appointed chief of army staff: October 1998

                                Appointer: Nawaz Sharif

                                Musharraf became 10th president of Pakistan in 2001 until 2008.

                                Coup: October 12, 1999 (declared a state of emergency)

                                In June 2001, Musharraf became the president of Pakistan.

                                In 2006, Musharraf’s autobiography titled In the Line of Fire was published.

                                In March 2007, Musharraf suspended then chief justice Iftikhar Muhammad Chaudhry.

                                Musharraf imposed a state of emergency on November 3, 2007.

                                Resigned from his position of army chief November 2007

                                Replacer: General Ashfaq Pervaiz Kayani

                                Musharraf lifted the emergency on December 15, 2007.

                                In 2010, Musharraf launched his political party — All Pakistan Muslim League (APML).

                                He flew to Dubai on March 17, 2016

                                On December 17, 2019, a special court handed Musharraf death sentence in the high treason case (only military ruler to be sentenced to death)

                                Died: 5 February 2023

                                Dying place: Dubai, UAE

                                Disease: amyloidosis

                                Aged: 79

                                Buried: Karachi

                                ND26-2-2023

                                 

                                 

                                447

                                Which is the National language of Pakistan?

                                Correct Answer: Urdu
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                 Article 251 of the Constitution declares Urdu to be the national language of Pakistan.

                                The national bird of Pakistan Chukar

                                The national animal of Pakistan markhor
                                National fruit of Pakistan                                 Mango
                                The national dish of Pakistan                                 nihari
                                National flower of Pakistan                                 jasmine


                                AGM 30.4.23



                                448

                                The Famous Fruit of Larkana is ______?

                                Correct Answer: Guava
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The famous fruit of Larkana is guava. 

                                Larkana is known as the "City of Guava".

                                Due to its high production and reputation for quality guavas.

                                This particularly those with pink flesh and a distinct rectangular shape.

                                449

                                Pakistan Day is celebrated throughout Pakistan each year on ____?

                                Correct Answer: 23 March
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Pakistan Day, یوم پاکستان or Pakistan Resolution Day, commemorating the Lahore Resolution passed on 23 March 1940.

                                450

                                The significance of Pakistan's role in the Organization of Islamic Cooperation is ______?

                                Correct Answer: It address issues faced by Muslim countries
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                OIC's Purpose:

                                The Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) is the second-largest intergovernmental organization after the UN, aimed at safeguarding the interests of Muslim-majority countries (e.g., Palestine, Kashmir, Islamophobia).

                                Pakistan's Role:

                                Advocacy: Pakistan actively raises issues like Kashmir and Islamophobia in OIC forums.

                                Mediation: Has mediated conflicts between Muslim nations (e.g., Iran-Saudi tensions).

                                Humanitarian Aid: Leads OIC initiatives for Rohingya Muslims, Afghan refugees, etc.

                                451

                                The impact of inflation on Pakistan's economy is ______?

                                Correct Answer: It led to higher prices of goods and services
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Inflation Defined:

                                Inflation is the sustained increase in general price levels, reducing purchasing power.

                                Impact on Pakistan:

                                Rising Costs: Essential goods (food, fuel) become more expensive, straining households.

                                Economic Strain: Businesses face higher input costs, leading to slower growth and unemployment.

                                Currency Depreciation: High inflation often weakens the rupee, worsening imports.

                                452

                                The Pakistan Super League (PSL) is associated with ______?

                                Correct Answer: Cricket
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Pakistan Super League (PSL) is Pakistan's premier professional Twenty20 cricket league, established in 2015.

                                It features franchise-based teams (e.g., Karachi Kings, Lahore Qalandars) and attracts international players.

                                Islamabad United made history as the inaugural PSL champions in 2016.

                                453

                                Malam Jabba is a popular tourist destination in Pakistan known for its _____?

                                Correct Answer: Ski Resorts
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Malam Jabba is famous for being Pakistan’s only ski resort.

                                It is located in the Swat Valley, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.

                                It attracts tourists year-round for skiing, snowboarding, chair lifts, and scenic mountain views.

                                454

                                Pakistan seeks financial assistance from the IMF in 2024 because _____?

                                Correct Answer: To address its balance of payments crises
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Pakistan approached the IMF in 2024 to secure funding aimed at stabilizing its balance of payments, a situation where it didn’t have enough foreign exchange to pay for imports and debt obligations.

                                The IMF assistance helps rebuild foreign currency reserves, meet external debt servicing needs, and restore investor confidence.

                                455

                                The National Accountability Bureau (NAB) was established to combat _____?

                                Correct Answer: Political corruption
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The National Accountability Bureau (NAB) is Pakistan's lead anti-corruption agency.

                                It established by Pervez Musharraf on November 16, 1999.

                                Its headquarters are in Islamabad.

                                It focuses on combating political corruption and ensuring accountability.

                                456

                                The role of National Assembly in Pakistan is ______?

                                Correct Answer: To make laws
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Pakistan has three branches of government:

                                1. The Legislative Branch makes the laws.
                                2. The Executive Branch carries out the laws.
                                3. The Judiciary Branch evaluates and interprets the laws.

                                The Legislative Branch, or Parliament, has two houses: the National Assembly and the Senate

                                1. National Assembly: It makes laws for the federation and keeps a check over the Executive. 

                                2. The Senate is the body that represents the provinces and promotes a feeling of equality, peace, and harmony.

                                457

                                The Sindh Festival is celebrated to promote _____?

                                Correct Answer: Culture and Heritage
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Sindh Festival was launched to promote Sindhi culture, art, music, and heritage both locally and internationally.

                                It highlights historical sites like Moenjodaro and

                                It's aims to preserve and celebrate Sindh’s rich traditions.

                                458

                                On which river is Mangla Dam situated?

                                Correct Answer: Jhelum River
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Mangla Dam, located on the Jhelum River in Pakistan’s Mirpur District, Azad Jammu and Kashmir.

                                It is a multipurpose dam.

                                It is the world's seventh-largest dam.

                                It was constructed between 1961 and 1967 with assistance from the World Bank.

                                The dam features five tunnels and a power station.

                                It is named after the nearby village of Mangla.

                                459

                                Who is responsible for supervising the banking sector in Pakistan?

                                Correct Answer: State Bank of Pakistan
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                 The State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) is responsible for supervising the banking sector in Pakistan. 

                                State Bank Of Pakistan
                                Founded 1-Jul-1948
                                Founded byM.A Jinnah
                                First GovernorZahid Hussain
                                First Female GovernorShamshad Akhtar
                                SBP Responsibitly implement monetary policy
                                HQKarachi, Sindh
                                460

                                Mir Kalil Ur Rehman was the founder of ?

                                Correct Answer: Jhang Group
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                میر خلیل الرحمان

                                Mir Khalil ur Rahman was a Pakistani newspaper editor and founder of the Jang Group of Newspapers,

                                which currently publishes many Urdu and English newspapers in Pakistan.

                                A self-made newspaper magnate, he ranks among the most successful newspaper entrepreneurs of Pakistan. 

                                Born: July 14, 1918, Gujranwala

                                Died: 1992

                                461

                                In 1739 which Muslim ruler sacked Delhi?

                                Correct Answer: Nadir Shah
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Nadir Shah invaded India during the reign of Mughal emperor Muhammad Shah.

                                He ruled from 1719 to 1748.

                                On February 24, 1739, Nadir Shah's army defeated the Mughals at the Battle of Karnal.

                                Muhammad Shah surrendered, and Nadir Shah entered Delhi on March 20, 1739. 

                                462

                                The Qadirpur gas field, located in ?

                                Correct Answer: Sindh
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Qadirpur gas field, located in Sindh, Pakistan

                                The field is located at a distance of 8 kilometres (5.0 mi) from Ghotki in Sindh Province.

                                Discovered in March 1990

                                It is the country's second-largest gas field

                                Year of current production of gas : July 2016 to March 2017

                                463

                                Who was the famous female freedom fighter during the revolt of 1857?

                                Correct Answer: Rani of Jhansi
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Rani Lakshmi Bai was the central figure of the revolt in Jhansi, and both Hindus and Muslims fought under her leadership.

                                She is remembered as a symbol of unity and resistance during the War of Independence 1857

                                464

                                Iskander Mirza abrogated the 1956 Constitution and dissolved assemblies on?

                                Correct Answer: 7th October 1958
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Iskander Mirza abrogated the 1956 Constitution and dissolved the assemblies on October 7, 1958.

                                All important questions of  "the 1956 constitution"

                                1956 constitution is promulgated on 23 March 1956

                                ***

                                Iskander Mirza abrogated the 1956 constitution

                                ***

                                Both Urdu and Bengali were declared as official and national languages of Pakistan in the 1956 constitution

                                ***

                                Constitution of 1956 · Pakistan was declared as the Islamic Republic

                                ***

                                In 1956 the designation of Governor General changed to President

                                ****

                                40 years was the age prescribed for president in the 1956 constitution

                                ***

                                The Constitution of 1956 contained 234 articles divided into thirteen parts and six schedules

                                **

                                The constitution of 1956 remained implemented for 2 years 6 months and 14 days.

                                **

                                The constitution of 1956 provided a Parliamentary form of government for the country.

                                465

                                Maulana Zafar Ali Khan died on which date?

                                Correct Answer: 27th November 1956
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Maulana Zafar Ali Khan Died 27 November 1956,  Wazirabad, West Pakistan,

                                Maulana Zafar Ali Khan from the province of Punjab supported Pakistan Resolution.

                                ***

                                He was born at Kot Mehrath, District Sialkot [Punjab] in 1873.

                                In 1908, he came to Lahore and took over the charge of the daily Zamindar, which was founded by his father Maulvi Sirajuddin Ahmad in 1903.

                                He is considered the father of Urdu journalism, and Zamindar

                                He was the Occupation was Poet, journalist.


                                466

                                The narrow strip of land that connects Pakistan with central Asia is ______?

                                Correct Answer: Wakhan
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Wakhan Corridor is a narrow strip of territory in Afghanistan, extending to China and separating Tajikistan from Pakistan and Kashmir.

                                 The corridor was formed by an 1893 and creating the Durand Line.

                                The Wakhan Corridor is about 350 km long.


                                467

                                The purpose of the Lahore Resolution of 1940 was _____?

                                Correct Answer: To demand a separate homeland for Muslims
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Lahore Resolution aimed to secure separate nations for Muslims where they could live according to their cultural and religious values.

                                It was a turning point in the Pakistan Movement, leading to the creation of Pakistan in 1947.

                                468

                                The importance of the Indus River in Pakistan is because ______?

                                Correct Answer: It is the primary source of water for agriculture
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Indus River is the lifeline of Pakistan’s agriculture, supporting the largest irrigation system.

                                It irrigates vast lands in Punjab and Sindh, making it crucial for food production and economy.

                                469

                                Lahore is called the "Heart of Pakistan" because ______?

                                Correct Answer: It is the cultural and historical center
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • Heart of Pakistan is known as Lahore
                                • Due to its rich cultural heritage, historical landmarks, and vibrant arts scene.
                                • Also called the "City of Colleges." 
                                • The second-largest city in Pakistan
                                • Capital of the province of Punjab
                                • 26th largest in the world

                                Last updated by NA on 25-April-2026

                                470

                                What is the name of Pakistan Space Agency?

                                Correct Answer: SUPARCO
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Pakistan's National Space Agency SUPARCO.

                                SUPARCO stands for Space and Upper Atmosphere Research Commission.

                                SUPARCO was set up in 1961.


                                471

                                Which River is known as the lifeline of Pakistan?

                                Correct Answer: Indus
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                River Indus flow from Country China, India, Pakistan

                                River Indus flow from State Ladakh, Punjab, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Sindh, Gilgit-Baltistan, Tibet

                                Sources of water Tibetan Plateau, Senge Zangbu, Gar River

                                Tributaries Sutlej River, Chenab River, Jhelum River, Ravi River,

                                River Sindh Length 3,180 km

                                Mouth Arabian Sea near Thatta

                                The Indus River originates in Tibet and follows a circuitous route southward through the Himalaya Mountains to the Arabian Sea,


                                **********

                                How many rivers flow in Punjab Province?

                                5

                                How many rivers flow in Sindh Province?

                                4

                                How many rivers flow in Balochistan Province?

                                7

                                How many rivers flow in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Province?

                                8

                                How many rivers are present in Pakistan?

                                24

                                472

                                After which event did the British government end the rule of the East India Company and take direct control of India?

                                Correct Answer: War of Independence
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                After the War of Independence in 1857, the British ended the rule of the East India Company.
                                India came under the direct control of the British Crown through the Government of India Act 1858.
                                473

                                By which year Pakistan has pledged to the world, in a conference hosted by US that the country will shift to 60 percent clean energy and 30 percent of its overall vehicular fleet to electric energy?

                                Correct Answer: 2030
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Pakistan pledged to shift to 60% clean energy and 30% electric vehicles by 2030.

                                The announcement was made at the US-initiated Leaders Summit on Climate.

                                Malik Amin Aslam, SAPM on Climate Change, represented Pakistan.

                                He urged developed nations to fulfill their climate finance commitments.

                                He stated Pakistan is doing more than its share to combat climate change.

                                This commitment was made during a climate summit hosted by the USA in 2021.

                                474

                                What is the Rank of Pakistan in World Happiness Report 2021?

                                Correct Answer: 105 out of 149
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                World Happiness Report 2021

                                Issuer: UN Sustainable Development Solutions Network

                                Releasing date: 19 March 2021

                                Topper: Finland 

                                Total countries: 149

                                Afghanistan: 149 

                                Pakistan: 105

                                475

                                In the absence of Quaid-e-Azam, who led the Muslims delegation in the third round table conference?

                                Correct Answer: Sir Agha Khan
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • In 1932 third round table conference held
                                • ***
                                • Sir Agha Khan led the mulim delegation. 
                                  First round table conference: 1930
                                • 2nd round table conference: 1931
                                • 3rd round table conference:1932
                                • All round table conference held in London
                                • ***
                                • The three Round Table Conferences of 1930–32 were a series of conferences to formulate the future constitution of India in the light of suggestions given by the Indian Leaders
                                • ***
                                • Gandhi a leader does not attend the first round table conference 
                                • AGM-24-12-2022
                                476

                                On the recommendation of the Rowlatt Committee, which Act was legislated?

                                Correct Answer: The Anarchical and Revolutionary Act
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Rowlatt Committee recommended laws to suppress revolutionary activities.

                                This led to the Anarchical and Revolutionary Crimes Act of 1919.

                                It is commonly known as the Rowlatt Act.

                                The Act allowed detention without trial.

                                It was passed in March 1919.

                                Lord Chelmsford was the Viceroy of India at that time (1916–1921).

                                477

                                Mohammad Rizwan become the second cricket wicketkeeper-batsman to make hundred across all the formats after ________?

                                Correct Answer: Brandon Mccullam
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Pakistani cricketer Mohammad Rizwan set up a new record for himself in Twenty20 International cricket by scoring a century in the match against South Africa, thus becoming the first-ever Pakistani wicket-keeper.

                                 He is also the second wicket-keeper batsman in the world to score a 100 in all three formats of international cricket.

                                478

                                Pakistan’s law-making body/bodies is/are _____?

                                Correct Answer: Both A and B
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Pakistan’s Parliament consists of two houses: the Senate (upper house) and the National Assembly (lower house).

                                Both participate in the law-making process.

                                479

                                محمد بن قاسم نے جب سندھ پر حملہ کیا تو اسکے پاس کتنی منجنیقیں تھیں؟

                                Correct Answer: 5
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                When Muhammad bin Qasim invaded Sindh in 712 AD, he brought 5 manjaniqs (catapults) with his army.

                                These were used to besiege forts and break strongholds, especially during the conquest of Debal.

                                480

                                The resolution was written in English language. Who translated the Pakistan resolution in Urdu?

                                Correct Answer: Maulana Zafar Khan
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Pakistan Resolution was translated into Urdu by Maulana Zafar Ali Khan. 

                                Lahore Resolution was passed in the 27th annual session of the All India Muslim League.

                                *****

                                Presented            22 March 1940

                                Ratified                 23 March 1940

                                Location              Lahore

                                Author(s)             Muhammad Zafarullah Khan

                                Presented            A.K Fazlulhuq

                                Signatories          All-India Muslim League Purpose To announce the declaration of independence from British India.

                                ****

                                Lahore Resolution Translation English to Urdu: Mulana Zafar Ali Khan

                                Lahore Resolution Presented by A.K Fazul Haq        

                                Participate From Punjab (Leader): Zafar Ali Khan

                                Participate From Sindh (Leader): Sir Abdullah Haroon

                                Participate From Balochistan (Leader): Qazi Faiz Isa

                                Participate From KPK (Leader): Sardar Aurangzaib Khan

                                CM Banglad : A.K Fazul Haq 

                                United Province: Khalid Zaman

                                RK271222

                                481

                                The Economist Intelligence Unit 2020 Democracy Index ranked the Democracy in Pakistan in which category?

                                Correct Answer: Hybrid regimes
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                Pakistan has taken 105th position out of 167 countries.
                                In the 2020 released Economist Intelligence Unit's (EIU) Democracy Index report, top five countries:
                                1. Norway
                                2. Iceland
                                3. Sweden
                                4. New Zealand
                                5. Canada 
                                482

                                Which Pakistani mountaineer becomes first Asian to summit Mount Kilimanjaro within 24 hours?

                                Correct Answer: Asad Ali Memon
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Ø  Asad Ali Memon says it took him 20 hours to complete summit and return to gate of Mount Kilimanjaro.

                                Ø  Pakistani mountaineer Asad Ali Memon on 17-02-2021 became the first Pakistani and Asian to summit the Mount Kilimanjaro within 24 hours.

                                 

                                Ø  Mount Kilimanjaro is a dormant volcano in Tanzania.

                                Ø  It is the highest mountain in Africa and

                                Ø  The highest single free-standing mountain in the world.

                                Ø  Height 5,895 metros 

                                483

                                What is the term for the geographical division of urban areas to perform certain functions at the local level?

                                Correct Answer: Municipal Corporation
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                A Municipal Corporation is a geographical and administrative division in urban areas to manage local governance and services.

                                It handles functions like sanitation, water supply, infrastructure, and urban planning for cities.

                                484

                                According to the Sindh Local Government Act 2013, how many levels are given to the local government?

                                Correct Answer: Three Levels
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Sindh Local Government Act, 2013:

                                Three-Tiered System:

                                The Act defines a three-tiered local government system:

                                1. Metropolitan Corporation
                                2. Municipal Corporation
                                3. District Council

                                Additional Local Bodies:

                                Within this structure, smaller administrative units include:

                                • Town Committees (for small urban areas)
                                • Union Councils (for rural governance)


                                485

                                Which medal did Pakistan win the Asian Athletics Championships 2025?

                                Correct Answer: Gold Medal
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Olympic champion Arshad Nadeem threw a massive 86.40m to win gold in the men’s javelin final at the Asian Athletics Championship

                                Winning date: 31 May 2025

                                Venue: Gumi, South Korea

                                Nadeem’s gold is Pakistan’s first and only medal at the championship.

                                 

                                Additional Information:

                                The 2025 Asian Athletics Championships was the 26th edition of the Asian Athletics Championships.

                                Being held from 27 to 31 May 2025 in Gumi, South Korea.

                                 ****

                                486

                                When for the rehabilitation of refugees emergency was declared for the first time in the history of Pakistan?

                                Correct Answer: August 27, 1948
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                 An emergency was declared for the first time in the history of Pakistan on August 27, 1948,

                                Specifically for the rehabilitation of refugees who had migrated from India during the Partition in 1947.


                                487

                                Textbook Boards were established in the provinces of Pakistan in?

                                Correct Answer: 1962
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The West Pakistan Textbook Board was officially established on 6th June 1962 under the West Pakistan Textbook Board Ordinance XLI of 1962.

                                The Board was tasked with preparing, publishing, and distributing textbooks to ensure standardized education across West Pakistan.

                                488

                                Under National Education Policy 1972, free education was recommended up to class?

                                Correct Answer: 10
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Under the National Education Policy 1972, free education was recommended up to class 10.

                                It would be implemented in two phases, making education up to class 8 free in 1972 and up to class 10 in 1974

                                489

                                Who was the third Governor General of Pakistan?

                                Correct Answer: Ghulam Muhammad
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Malik Ghulam Muhammad was the third Governor-General of Pakistan.

                                It serving from 1951 to 1955.

                                He was dismissed from his position due to health conditions.

                                List of Governors-General of Pakistan
                                Term Name Birth-Death Monarch Took office Left office Political party Remarks
                                1 Muhammad Ali Jinnah (1876–1948) George VI
                                (1947–1952)                                		 15-Aug-47 11-Sep-48 Pakistan Muslim League died in office
                                2 Sir Khawaja Nazimuddin (1894–1964) = 14-Sep-48 17-Oct-51 Pakistan Muslim League became prime minister
                                3 Sir Ghulam Muhammad (1895–1956) = 17-Oct-51 7-Aug-55 Independent dismissed
                                4 Iskander Mirza (1899–1969) Elizabeth II
                                (1952–1956)                                		 7-Aug-55 23-Mar-56 Republican Party became president
                                490

                                The Simla Agreement signed by Prime Minister Indira Gandhi and President Zulfikar Ali Bhutto of Pakistan on _____?

                                Correct Answer: 2nd July 1972
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • The Simla Agreement was signed on July 2, 1972, by Indian Prime Minister Indira Gandhi and Pakistani President Zulfikar Ali Bhutto, following the Bangladesh Liberation War in 1971.
                                • The agreement established the Line of Control (LoC) in Kashmir and paved the way for the repatriation of prisoners of war and the return of occupied territories.
                                491

                                Which kind of system of Government was introduced in 1973 constitution?

                                Correct Answer: Parliamentary
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • The parliamentary system of Government was introduced in the 1973 constitution.
                                • ***
                                • The 1973 Constitution, is the supreme law of Pakistan.
                                • Zulifqar Ali Bhutto introduced the 1973 constitution in Pakistan on August 14, 1973.

                                • According to the 1973 constitution, the Provincial Assembly, National assembly, and senate elect the President.

                                • Whereas the prime minister is elected by the only national assembly.

                                • The bicameral Legislature system was introduced for the first time in Pakistan in the constitution of 1973.
                                • **


                                492

                                Who from the following is the first Muslim female prime minister?

                                Correct Answer: Benazir Bhutto
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Benazir Bhutto's first election to Prime Minister in 1988 made her the first woman to lead a Muslim-majority country

                                BB was Born on June 21, 1953, in Karachi

                                BB was Assassinated on December 27, 2007, in Rawalpindi

                                BB Spouse: Asif Ali Zardari (1987–2007)

                                BB Party: Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP)

                                BB Education: Lady Margaret Hall (1973–1977),

                                BB Children: Bilawal Bhutto Zardari, Bakhtawar Bhutto Zardari, Aseefa Bhutto Zardari.

                                she served as Prime Minister of Pakistan from 1988 to 1990 and again from 1993 to 1996

                                She was the 11th and 13th Prime Minister of Pakistan. 

                                ****

                                493

                                The area between rivers Jhelum and Chenab is called?

                                Correct Answer: Chaj
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                • The area between the Jhelum and Chenab rivers is called Chaj
                                • ....
                                • The Chenab River Flows through (areas in India) Chamba-Kishtwar-Thathri-Doda-Ramban-AkhnoorFlows
                                • The Jhelum River is a river that flows from the Indian-administered territory of Jammu and Kashmir.


                                494

                                Which range lies along the border of Sindh and Balochistan Province?

                                Correct Answer: Kirthar range
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation


                                The Kirthar Mountains are a mountain range that mark the boundary between the provinces of Balochistan and Sindh,

                                The highest peak of the mountains is Zardak Peak at 7,430 ft  or 2,150 m.

                                495

                                War of Mysore was fought during the period of?

                                Correct Answer: Tipu Sultan
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Anglo-Mysore Wars were a series of conflicts between the Kingdom of Mysore

                                Ruled by Tipu Sultan and the British East India Company. 

                                Additional information 

                                The First Mysore War (1767–1769) was fought between the English East India Company and Haider Ali of Mysore.

                                It ended with the Treaty of Madras in 1769.


                                • Second Mysore War (1780–1784): Fought between the English and Haider Ali, later Tipu Sultan; ended with the Treaty of Mangalore.
                                • Third Mysore War (1790–1792): Fought between Tipu Sultan and the English, ending with the Treaty of Seringapatam.
                                • Fourth Mysore War (1799): Fought between Tipu Sultan and the British; Tipu Sultan was killed, and Mysore was brought under British control.

                                496

                                Who is known as the Poet of East?

                                Correct Answer: Allama Iqbal
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Know about Allama Iqbal

                                Born: November 9, 1877 (Sialkot)

                                Father: Sheikh Noor Muhammad

                                Mother: Imam Bibi

                                Received PhD: Germany

                                Title: Poet of the East

                                Famous books: Bang-e-Dara, Zarb-i-Kalim, Armughan-e-Hijaz

                                Famous Punjabi Books/Poem: Piyaara Jedi, Baba Bakri Wala

                                Died: 21 April 1938

                                Burial: Badshahi Mosque in Lahore

                                 

                                497

                                The first women of Ombudsperson of Punjab is?

                                Correct Answer: Ayesha Hamid
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The first woman Ombudsperson of Punjab is Ayesha Hamid.

                                She was sworn in on October 9, 2024.

                                498

                                Landi Kotal is the famous town of which agency?

                                Correct Answer: Khyber Agency
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                 Landi Kotal is the main shopping centre for the Shalmani, Shinwari, Afridi, and Mulagori tribes of Khyber Agency.

                                It was one of the largest towns in the former Federally Administered Tribal Areas
                                It is located 1,072 metres (3,517 ft) above sea level
                                499

                                Who has become the first Pakistani woman cricketer to be inducted in ICC Hall of Fame?

                                Correct Answer: Sana Mir
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Former women’s captain Sana Mir became the first female cricketer to be inducted into the (ICC) Hall of Fame.

                                She was inducted into ICC hall of fame on 9 June 2025.

                                She was among seven notable cricketers, the others being India’s MS Dhoni, Australia’s Matthew Hayden, New Zealand’s Daniel Vettori, England’s Sarah Taylor and South Africa’s Hashim Amla and Graeme Smith.

                                 

                                Additional Information:

                                ICC stands for International Cricket Council.

                                This is a cricket governing body with its headquarters in Dubai, UAE.

                                 *****

                                500

                                The August Offer (1940) was aimed at?

                                Correct Answer: Inviting a certain number of Indian representatives to join Viceroy’s Executive Council
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The August Offer was made by Viceroy Lord Linlithgow in 1940

                                It an attempt to secure Indian support for the British war effort during World War II

                                It did not offer complete independence or resolve Hindu-Muslim differences — hence, option (a) is correct.






                                Nice work getting this far. Consistency beats intensity — come back tomorrow and keep stacking sets.
